domains.summary: 10xgenomics.com exposes two developer surfaces: a Cloud MCP server and a Cloud CLI, both authenticated via a 10x Genomics Cloud login session.
12andus.com appears to expose no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI; only the public website and authenticated account-based purchase/user flows are evident.

12Go Asia exposes a public consumer website only; the only developer-facing surface found is the authenticated 12Go affiliate/partner portal, with no confirmed public HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration API.

1Forge exposes one public developer surface: the 1Forge Forex Quote REST API at https://api.1forge.com, authenticated by a single API key passed as the `api_key` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
1Password.com exposes four developer surfaces: Partnership API, Events API, Connect REST API, and the `op` CLI; all require 1Password-issued bearer/service-account credentials.
3GPP exposes a public website and an authenticated 3GPP Portal; no public REST, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
3Min API exposes one REST API and one MCP server; REST uses a Bearer API key, while MCP supports OAuth for browser clients and an `x-api-key` flow for CLI/IDE clients.
3scale.net exposes a developer-portal/API management surface with standard API auth patterns (API key, app_id/app_key pair, and OpenID Connect); no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: 3scale API management / developer portal API surface

domains.summary: CardChain exposes one MCP server at https://4ygmimr3yj.us-east-2.awsapprunner.com/mcp, authenticated via OAuth account sign-in (openid scope); no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were found.
5e-bits.github.io exposes a public REST API at https://www.dnd5eapi.co/api and a public GraphQL API at https://www.dnd5eapi.co/graphql; both require no authentication.
AbhiBus exposes no public developer integration surface in the sourced docs; only the public booking website is present, with no documented API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI for developers.
Ableton.com exposes one developer integration surface: the public-beta Ableton Extensions SDK for building Live extensions; no REST, GraphQL, or MCP surface was found.
Ably exposes REST APIs for Pub/Sub, Control, REST/Realtime SDK interfaces, plus a CLI; auth is via Ably API keys, access tokens, and CLI env/login flows, with no MCP surface found.
Ably’s public developer surfaces are the Realtime SDK, REST API, SSE API, Control API, and Ably CLI; auth is via API keys for realtime/REST/SSE and Bearer access tokens for Control API and CLI.

Ably exposes the Pub/Sub REST API, Control API, SSE/raw streaming API, Ably CLI, and REST SDK auth docs; credentials are API keys, access tokens, and JWTs, with CLI OAuth login and env-var auth also supported.

Abstract API exposes multiple REST HTTP APIs; documented auth is per-API Abstract API key, passed via `api_key` query param for most products and additionally as `Authorization: Bearer` for Email Reputation.
Abusive Experience Report API exposes one public REST surface at https://abusiveexperiencereport.googleapis.com; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Google Accelerated Mobile Pages (AMP) URL API is a single HTTP REST surface at `https://acceleratedmobilepageurl.googleapis.com/v1`, authenticated by either an API key or OAuth token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Access Approval exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API and one public MCP server; REST uses Google ADC, and no GraphQL or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: Google Cloud Access Approval MCP Server

domains.summary: accesso.com exposes REST APIs on the TE2/accesso Guest Experience platform plus OAuth2/OIDC identity and service-account token flows; auth uses HTTP Basic with an accesso API key and bearer/JWT-based OAuth2 credentials.

accesso/TE2 offers REST APIs only, with OAuth2/OIDC bearer tokens and service-account JWT-based client-credentials auth; user device registration is a protected REST surface, and webhook subscriptions are documented but not fully determined.

Accor exposes five public HTTP APIs (Accommodations, Business Offers, Customer Information, Customer Marketing Subscriptions, Properties, Hotel Policies); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the developer docs.
Dropbox exposes one public developer surface here: the Dropbox API v2 HTTP API on api.dropboxapi.com, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 bearer access tokens minted from a Dropbox OAuth app.

AccuWeather exposes two authenticated HTTP API surfaces: the developer portal Core Weather API and the Enterprise API; both use a single AccuWeather API key, with header Bearer auth on the developer portal examples and `apikey` query auth on Enterprise API.
ACKO exposes one public developer surface: a bearer-authenticated Enterprise HTTP API documented on its enterprise docs portal; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Mapped the Google ACME DNS API at https://acmedns.googleapis.com/v1 as an authenticated HTTP surface; no public, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
ActiveCampaign exposes one authenticated REST API v3, one authenticated eComm GraphQL API, and a remote MCP server whose auth mechanics weren’t confirmed from public docs.
Activepieces exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated MCP surface; both use Bearer tokens, with API keys for the REST API and short-lived MCP tokens for MCP.

Acuity Scheduling exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP REST API at https://acuityscheduling.com/api/v1, authenticated either with Basic auth (numeric User ID + API Key) or OAuth2 Bearer access tokens; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Adafruit.com exposes one public HTTP surface: the Adafruit Products API at https://www.adafruit.com/api, with no documented auth.

Adalo exposes one public developer surface: the Adalo API at https://api.adalo.com, authenticated with a bearer App API Key generated in the Adalo editor; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.
Google Authorized Buyers exposes two developer HTTP APIs: Marketplace API and Real-time Bidding API; both require Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens via Authorization: Bearer.

surfaces.name: Google Authorized Buyers Real-time Bidding API

domains.summary: Ad Experience Report API exposes one REST/HTTP surface at `https://adexperiencereport.googleapis.com/v1`, with discovery doc `https://adexperiencereport.googleapis.com/$discovery/rest?version=v1`; it supports `sites.get` and `violatingSites.list` and requires an API key sent as query parameter `key`.
Google Admin SDK API on https://admin.googleapis.com is the only public developer surface found; it requires Google OAuth 2.0 bearer auth via Authorization header.
AdMob exposes one public developer surface: the AdMob API v1 REST at https://admob.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Adobe Experience Manager as a Cloud Service exposes a GraphQL API for Content Fragments and MCP servers; both use Adobe IMS/AEM service credentials with Bearer-style OAuth access tokens.

surfaces.name: AEM GraphQL API for Content Fragments

domains.summary: Adobe.io exposes Adobe Developer Console auth flows plus HTTP APIs including Adobe Experience Platform APIs and Adobe PDF Services API; public developer-auth docs are unauthenticated, while the APIs use Adobe-issued API key, org ID, and OAuth access token credentials.
adobelogin.com exposes Adobe IMS auth APIs plus Adobe User Management API access; all discovered surfaces require Adobe-issued credentials and use IMS bearer tokens with x-api-key where applicable.

surfaces.name: Adobe IMS OAuth Server-to-Server token API

domains.summary: adsense.googleapis.com exposes the authenticated AdSense Management HTTP API (v2) using Google OAuth 2.0; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Advanced Web Ranking exposes three authenticated HTTP APIs (Developer API v2, Market Share API, AI Search API), all using the same API token passed as the `token` query parameter.

surfaces.name: Advanced Web Ranking Developer API v2
Advanced Web Ranking Market Share API

domains.summary: advicement.io exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated DynamicDocs HTTP API using an `adv-security-token` API key in the `adv-security-token` header.
Advisory Notifications exposes one authenticated HTTP API at advisorynotifications.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

Adyen exposes HTTP APIs for Checkout, BinLookup, Disputes, and Webhooks plus an MCP server; authenticated surfaces use Adyen API keys or webhook HMAC, while the MCP auth mechanics couldn’t be confirmed from available docs.
Adyen exposes the Checkout REST API via the API Explorer and an MCP server; Checkout API requires an Adyen API key, while MCP auth could not be fully determined from the available docs.
afraid.org exposes a FreeDNS member portal and a token-based Dynamic DNS update HTTP endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.
AfterShip exposes one public developer surface: the AfterShip Tracking HTTP API, authenticated either by API key headers or OAuth bearer token; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Agenta exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Public MCP surface only: TravelOS Synxis MCP Server at https://agentichoteldistribution.com/mcp/synxis?chainId={chainId}, no credential required.
Agiflow exposes OAuth-based remote MCP access plus scoped connection URLs for assistants; docs also mention API key bearer auth for headless tools.
Agility CMS exposes REST Content Fetch, REST Content Management, GraphQL, MCP, and CLI surfaces; auth uses API keys for read APIs, PATs for management/MCP, and CLI login for the Agility CLI.

Agora exposes 4 developer surfaces on agora.io: 3 authenticated REST APIs (Conversational AI, Voice & Video Calling, Cloud Recording, plus Console REST API) using Basic auth with Customer ID/Secret, and a public MCP docs server at https://mcp.agora.io.

surfaces.name: Agora Voice & Video Calling REST API

domains.summary: Agree Zone exposes one public MCP surface at https://agreezone.replit.app/mcp with open access; no separate API, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Aha! exposes one public developer surface: the account-scoped REST API at `https://{account-domain}.aha.io/api/v1`, documented at `https://www.aha.io/api` with OpenAPI JSON at `https://www.aha.io/openapi.json`, authenticated by either OAuth2 bearer tokens or user API keys via `Authorization: Bearer`.
AI21 exposes a Bearer-authenticated HTTP API, a Python SDK/CLI surface using `AI21_API_KEY`, and an MCP tool connector that also relies on the same API key.
Aiera exposes a single authenticated REST API and an MCP server; REST uses an issued API key in `X-API-Key`, while MCP auth mechanics were not fully specified in the docs read.
Airbnb exposes a developer HTTP API via the Airbnb developer portal, authenticated with an Airbnb OAuth app/access token (Bearer Authorization); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Airbyte exposes the Airbyte Cloud REST API, Airbyte Agents REST API, remote MCP server, and `airbyte-agent` CLI; all require Airbyte-issued application credentials/token-based auth.

Airbyte exposes one public REST API, a public knowledge MCP server, and an authenticated Agent MCP; auth is via Airbyte OAuth client credentials / bearer access token, with OpenAPI at https://airbyte.io/openapi.json and MCP endpoints at https://airbyte.mcp.kapa.ai and https://mcp.airbyte.ai/mcp.
AirOps exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated MCP server; both use the same workspace API key/OAuth-backed AirOps account access path.
Airport Pickups London exposes a REST API, an MCP server, and an A2A agent endpoint; all require the same `x-api-key` credential.
airSlate exposes one documented developer surface: the altaFlow REST API at https://api.airslate.io/v1, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

Airwallex exposes a public REST API, a beta CLI, a sandbox Developer MCP server, and a public Docs MCP server; authentication is via client_id/api_key for the REST API and OAuth consent for CLI/Developer MCP.
Aiwyn exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface (Aiwyn Product API) using JWT API key exchange to mint a bearer JWT; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed from the public docs.
Akeneo exposes an authenticated REST API, GraphQL API, MCP server, and Supplier Data Manager REST API; no public unauthenticated developer surface was confirmed.

Akeyless exposes one public HTTP API and two MCP server surfaces; both require Akeyless API key auth (Access ID + Access Key), and the MCP servers are launched locally via the Akeyless CLI with `--gateway-url`.

Google Workspace Alert Center API at https://alertcenter.googleapis.com is the public developer surface; it requires OAuth 2.0 Bearer auth via a Google Cloud OAuth client, and no MCP/CLI/GraphQL surface was found.

Algolia exposes HTTP Search, Analytics, and Recommend APIs, two MCP surfaces (Productivity and Public), and an Algolia CLI; auth uses Algolia application ID plus API key for HTTP APIs, OAuth-backed MCP sign-in for Productivity MCP, and Search API key for Public MCP.
All Ten MCP Server exposes a public site with an MCP endpoint at https://all-ten-chat-gpt-app.replit.app/mcp; auth could not be determined from available docs.
Alloy exposes one documented HTTP API surface at the sandbox/v1 API, authenticated with either HTTP Basic `token:secret` or OAuth 2.0 client-credentials using the same account-level API key pair.
Alpaca exposes Trading, Broker, and Market Data HTTP APIs plus a Trading CLI and an MCP server; auth is via Alpaca API key/secret for Trading/Data and OAuth client credentials for Broker.
Alpic exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated CLI, and one authenticated MCP server; all use either an API key or browser/OAuth-based account login.

Alpic exposes a private REST API and CLI for managing MCP servers, both authenticated by Alpic-issued API keys or browser login; the app dashboard is public-facing but requires sign-in for management.
Amadeus exposes self-service REST APIs on `test.api.amadeus.com` using OAuth2 client-credentials with API Key/API Secret; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs.
amazonaws.com is the AWS ecosystem; I identified S3 and API Gateway REST/V2 HTTP-WebSocket APIs plus AWS CLI surfaces, all authenticated via IAM/AWS SigV4 or AWS CLI profiles.

surfaces.name: Amazon API Gateway V2 HTTP/WebSocket API

domains.summary: amazonaws.com.cn exposes China-region AWS HTTP APIs discovered here: Amazon S3, Amazon SQS, and Amazon Glacier; all require AWS Signature Version 4 signed requests using AWS credentials.
amazontrust.com exposes only public informational/repository pages for Amazon Trust Services; no developer-facing API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Amberflo exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API at https://app.amberflo.io authenticated with an API key sent in the X-API-KEY header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Amentum exposes five HTTP APIs (Ocean, Atmosphere, Aviation Radiation, Geomagnetic, Gravity), one MCP server, and one CLI; all use the same Amentum API key, with Ocean and Geomag confirmed at openapi.json specs and the others documented only via docs portals.
amplify.aws exposes the Amplify CLI and AppSync-based GraphQL APIs; no MCP servers or separate HTTP REST surface were confirmed.

surfaces.name: AWS AppSync GraphQL API created via Amplify

domains.summary: Amplifyr exposes one public integration surface: an authenticated MCP server at https://mcp.amplifyr.me/mcp; no public HTTP/GraphQL API or CLI was found.
Amplitude exposes one MCP server (OAuth over streaming HTTP) and one CLI, the Amplitude Wizard (`npx @amplitude/wizard`), which can authenticate with an Amplitude API key.

Google Analytics exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Data API and Admin API) and a Google Analytics MCP server; auth is via Google OAuth/access tokens or service account credentials, while MCP auth could not be pinned from the docs.
Google Analytics Admin exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP server; the API uses Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.

Google Analytics exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Data API and Admin API) plus an MCP server; both HTTP APIs use Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens, while the MCP endpoint’s auth mechanics couldn’t be pinned from the docs I read.
Google Analytics exposes the Analytics Data API over HTTP and an MCP server; the HTTP API uses Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens, while MCP auth could not be confirmed from the pages read.
Anchore exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated GraphQL Reports API; both accept API key or username/password, and no MCP server or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Anchore Enterprise exposes a password-authenticated REST API, a GraphQL Reports API, and the `anchorectl` CLI; no MCP server was confirmed from public docs.
android.com exposes the Android Management API (HTTP REST) and its MCP server, plus the Android CLI; the REST API and MCP server use Google OAuth/Bearer auth, while the CLI auth was not determined.
Google Android Device Provisioning exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the Partner API and the Customer API; no MCP, CLI, or GraphQL surface was found.

androidenterprise.googleapis.com exposes two Google HTTP APIs: the legacy Google Play EMM API and the Android Management API; both require OAuth-bearing Google credentials, and the Android Management API also has an MCP server.

Android Management API exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API and one public MCP server at https://androidmanagement.googleapis.com/mcp; auth is via Google service account OAuth bearer tokens for the REST API.

androidpublisher.googleapis.com exposes the Google Play Developer HTTP API (v3) over https://androidpublisher.googleapis.com, authenticated via Google OAuth2 using either a service account or an OAuth client; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Angular exposes a public Angular CLI and a public MCP server (`npx @angular/cli mcp`); no authenticated developer API surface was found on angular.io.
aninditabasu.github.io exposes three public HTTP APIs in Indica (Rig Veda, Vedic Society, Mahabharat), each backed by an OpenAPI JSON spec and requiring no authentication.
Anthropic exposes one direct HTTP Claude API, one Anthropic CLI, and MCP documentation; API auth supports either console API keys or WIF bearer tokens, while the CLI uses `ant auth login`.

Anyscale exposes one authenticated HTTP API, an authenticated Python-backed CLI, and an MCP docs surface with unknown auth; credentials are user API keys and service account API keys, both used via `Authorization: Bearer`/`ANYSCALE_CLI_TOKEN`.
aoncadis.org exposes a public catalog/submission portal; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found, and access is public with optional ORCID sign-in for the portal UI.

surfaces.name: ACADIS / Arctic Data Center catalog and submission portal

domains.summary: Apache Superset exposes one documented REST API surface at `http://localhost:8088/api/v1`, authenticated with a JWT bearer token obtained from the login API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed from the public docs.

Apacta exposes one public developer surface: the Partner REST API at https://app.apacta.com/api/v1, documented by the OpenAPI spec https://apidoc.apacta.com/partner.yaml and secured with Bearer token auth.
ApartmentList.io has no public first-party developer surface found; discovered only a third-party Apify Actor with an HTTP API and MCP server, both authenticated by an Apify API token.
api-and-you.com exposes one documented MCP integration for SecretBox/ChatGPT and a Speedsales admin login; no public REST, GraphQL, or standalone CLI/API developer surface was found.

api.aws maps to Amazon API Gateway: one authenticated HTTP control-plane surface and the `aws apigateway` CLI, both using AWS SigV4 with AWS access keys (and optional STS session token); no public MCP or GraphQL surface found.

api.video exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the main REST API at https://ws.api.video (Basic or disposable Bearer auth with the api.video API key) and the Admin API at https://admin.api.video (Basic auth with a separately issued admin API key); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

API2Cart exposes one public developer surface for agents: an MCP server at https://api2cart.com/wp-json/mcp/v1 secured by OAuth (`mcp` scope) with dynamic client registration, authorize, and token endpoints.
Data2CRM.API exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api-backend.api2crm.com, using the developer-issued `X-API2CRM-USER-KEY` header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
api7.ai exposes AISIX/API7 Gateway integration surfaces: an authenticated proxy HTTP API, an authenticated MCP gateway, and an authenticated Admin HTTP API; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Apiary exposes an authenticated CLI (`apiary`) and a documented Apiary API at apiary.docs.apiary.io; no GraphQL, MCP, or public unauthenticated developer surface was confirmed.
ApiDapp exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.apidapp.com/1 using a beta-issued Bearer access key; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
ApiDapp exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.apidapp.com/1 using a Bearer access token issued via the ApiDapp access page; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
Apideck exposes 7 authenticated Unified REST APIs, one authenticated MCP server, and an authenticated `apideck` CLI; credentials are API key, app ID, and consumer ID.
apidocumentation.com exposes two public HTTP API docs portals: Snorkell API (OAuth2PasswordBearer or `api-key` header) and Invown API Collection (auth mechanics not fully pinned from the docs pages).

Apidog exposes one public REST API at https://api.apidog.com (Bearer `Authorization` token plus `X-Apidog-Api-Version`), and an Apidog MCP Server described in docs with auth mechanics not pinned down from the public pages.
Apify exposes the Apify API v2 HTTP surface, the hosted Apify MCP server, and the Apify CLI; authentication uses an Apify API token for API/MCP, while CLI auth could not be pinned from the docs read.
Google Cloud API Gateway exposes one public developer surface: the REST management API at apigateway.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 or service account Bearer tokens.
Apigee exposes a Google Cloud REST API requiring Bearer OAuth, plus the apigeecli CLI; no public MCP surface found.
Apigee exposes a public MCP server at https://apigee.googleapis.com/mcp; no additional public developer surfaces were confirmed from the docs read.
Apigee exposes an authenticated Google Cloud Apigee Management HTTP API, plus an Apigee MCP directory documented by Google Cloud; no separate public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Apigee Registry API exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST surface at https://apigeeregistry.googleapis.com/v1 using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens with the cloud-platform scope.
apikeys.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API (API Keys API v2) for managing and looking up API keys; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

APIMatic exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated CLI (`apimatic`), and MCP server generation docs; the API and CLI use APIMatic account auth / auth keys, while the MCP connect endpoint itself wasn’t documented.

apis.guru exposes a public REST API at https://api.apis.guru/v2 with OpenAPI spec https://api.apis.guru/v2/openapi.yaml, plus an MCP server mentioned by third-party docs; no authenticated developer credential was identified from the public docs.
API Setu exposes a public API directory and a partner portal for publishers/consumers; I found no public REST/GraphQL/MCP/CLI developer API specs on apisetu.gov.in itself.
Monoscope exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated MCP server, and one authenticated CLI; no public GraphQL surface was found.

Apollo GraphOS exposes one documented HTTP API, the GraphOS Platform API, plus the Rover CLI and MCP offerings (Apollo MCP Server and GraphOS MCP Tools); GraphOS API keys and Rover CLI login are the only confirmed auth paths.
ApostropheCMS exposes one documented developer surface: the REST API, authenticated with a logged-in user session; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
AppDeploy exposes a single public integration surface: an MCP server at https://api-v2.appdeploy.ai/mcp, using OAuth sign-in to AppDeploy (Google/Apple/X/Guest) via connector auth.
Apple Developer exposes the App Store Connect REST API; it requires an App Store Connect API key used to mint JWT bearer tokens.
applicationinsights.io exposes Azure Application Insights HTTP APIs for query and metrics, authenticated with an Application Insights API key sent as `x-api-key`.
appradiofm.com exposes a single authenticated Radio FM Partners dashboard at partners.appradiofm.com for managing stations/podcasts; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Apptigent exposes one public developer integration surface: the PowerTools REST API at https://connect.apptigent.com/api, authenticated with a portal-issued Client ID sent in `X-IBM-Client-Id`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
AppVeyor exposes a bearer-token REST API and a public in-build Build Worker REST API; no GraphQL, MCP, or standalone CLI surface was confirmed from the docs.
Appwrite exposes a REST API, GraphQL API, and MCP server; REST/GraphQL use project-scoped API keys and user sessions/JWTs, while the MCP server uses an Appwrite API key.

Aptabase exposes authenticated HTTP surfaces for analytics ingestion and the account portal, plus an open-source self-hosted deployment with auth unknown; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the public docs.
Arcade exposes an authenticated HTTP API on api.arcade.dev with Bearer API key auth, plus MCP and CLI developer surfaces documented by Arcade; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Archbee exposes one developer-facing HTTP Public API requiring a Bearer token derived from docSpaceId and apiKey, plus a public GraphQL docs component/integration page; no MCP server or CLI surfaced.
Archive.org exposes public HTTP APIs for Wayback availability and item metadata, a credentialed IAS3 upload API, and the `ia` CLI; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed.
Area120 Tables exposes one developer surface: the authenticated REST API at https://area120tables.googleapis.com/v1alpha1, using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
Argyle exposes one authenticated REST API with a public OpenAPI spec and one public docs MCP server; the API uses HTTP basic auth with API key ID and secret.
Arize exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated GraphQL API, and the `ax` CLI; all use an Arize API key (user or service key) with `Authorization: Bearer ...` or CLI/profile-based setup.
Artifact Registry exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API surface at artifactregistry.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified from the public docs.
Artillery exposes a public CLI (`artillery`) with no auth, plus the paid Artillery Pro CLI which requires an Artillery Pro license key; no HTTP/GraphQL/MCP surfaces were confirmed from the docs.
Artue exposes a public MCP endpoint at https://artue.io/api/mcp; no public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI developer API surface or credentials were confirmed from the available docs.
Asana exposes one REST API and one MCP server; both require OAuth-based auth, with PATs and standard OAuth for the REST API and a separate MCP app OAuth flow for the MCP server.

asciinema.org exposes one developer surface: the asciinema server HTTP API at /api/v1, authenticated via HTTP Basic using the CLI install ID minted by `asciinema auth`; the asciinema CLI is the companion client, not a separate integration surface.

Aserto exposes a Directory GraphQL API, Authorizer REST API, Directory gRPC API, and three CLIs (`aserto`, `policy`, `ds-load`); hosted APIs require `Authorization` plus `Aserto-Tenant-ID`, with API key or OAuth bearer token options for GraphQL and API key for Authorizer.
Ashby exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.ashbyhq.com using HTTP Basic with an Ashby API key, plus public webhook delivery documentation for configuring and verifying inbound webhooks.
AssemblyAI exposes one authenticated HTTP API and a public docs MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Asset Vision exposes one documented developer integration surface: a REST API product called Asset Vision Cloud Services; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs I found, and auth details remain unknown.
Assured Workloads exposes one authenticated REST API at https://assuredworkloads.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0 bearer access tokens in Authorization.

Atlassian.net exposes a required-auth Atlassian GraphQL API (OAuth 2.0 bearer on `https://api.atlassian.com/graphql` or browser-session/API-token basic auth on tenant gateways), Jira Cloud REST API v3 and Confluence Cloud REST API v2 (Atlassian API token basic auth or Atlassian OAuth 2.0), Bitbucket Cloud REST API (Bitbucket access token bearer or app password basic auth), and a Forge CLI login flow via `forge login` for Forge tooling.

ATO exposes an API portal with two documented HTTP APIs: Health Check API and OAuth Dynamic Client Registration API; both require portal-issued credentials, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: ATO OAuth Dynamic Client Registration API

domains.summary: Atomic exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (UserLink and PayLink), public webhooks, and a public Transact SDK; auth is via `x-api-key`/`x-api-secret` or `x-public-token` minted from the API.

AT&T exposes a public developer portal plus two programmatic surfaces found here: the OAuth token endpoint at `https://api.att.com/oauth/v4/token` and the SMS API at `https://api.att.com/sms/v3`, both authenticated with AT&T app credentials/OAuth Bearer tokens.
Attio exposes a public REST API (OAuth or workspace token) and a hosted MCP server over OAuth; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Auckland Museum exposes one public HTTP API at http://api.aucklandmuseum.com; version 2 is keyless/public and the Swagger page is a docs portal, not a machine-readable spec.
Aura exposes a logged-in web app and a hosted Aura MCP server; no public developer API spec, GraphQL, or CLI was found.
Auth0 exposes a Management HTTP API, Authentication HTTP API, and an MCP server; no GraphQL or standalone CLI surface was confirmed.
Okta Admin Management API authenticators surface requires an Okta API token via `Authorization: SSWS <token>`; no other public integration surfaces were confirmed.
authkit.app exposes an AuthKit/WorkOS OAuth authorization server and the WorkOS REST API; no CLI, GraphQL, or standalone MCP server was confirmed on the domain.

Authorized Buyers Marketplace API is a Google REST HTTP surface at https://authorizedbuyersmarketplace.googleapis.com and requires Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens with the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/authorized-buyers-marketplace` scope; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
autodealerdata.com exposes one REST/OpenAPI surface, the CIS Automotive API, authenticated via API ID/API key to mint a JWT or a Sub User Key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Autodesk Platform Services exposes OAuth-based Authentication API plus at least two authenticated GraphQL APIs: AEC Data Model and Data Exchange.

surfaces.name: Autodesk AEC Data Model GraphQL API

domains.summary: Automattic.com exposes the public WordPress.com REST API, WordPress.com MCP, WP-CLI, and a public WordPress Widgets API page; REST/MCP use WordPress.com OAuth-based credentials, while WP-CLI auth was not pinned from the docs.
Cloud AutoML exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST API at https://automl.googleapis.com; it uses Google OAuth 2.0 / ADC bearer tokens.
Automotion exposes one public MCP surface at https://automotion-mcp-production-6f08e98a6e4b.herokuapp.com/mcp; no credentials or additional developer surfaces were found.

AutoScout24 exposes one public developer integration surface: the Listing Creation REST API (OpenAPI spec at /assets/openapi/spec.yml) authenticated with account-based Basic Auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Autotask exposes REST, SOAP, Webhooks, and ExecuteCommand HTTP surfaces; REST/SOAP/ExecuteCommand use API User credentials plus an integration key, while webhook auth was not determined from the docs reviewed.

Autotrader.co.uk exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated Autotrader Connect HTTP APIs at developers.autotrader.co.uk/api, with API key or HTTP Basic auth; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Autotrader exposes no discoverable public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI docs; only consumer and dealer web portals are visible, both accessed via standard Autotrader account login.
Autovit.ro exposes a public website and a dealer professional portal; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the accessible docs.
Avalara exposes a developer portal and authenticated MCP servers; I could not confirm any public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI integration surface from the pages read.
Avaza exposes one documented HTTP API and one MCP server; both use OAuth-based auth, and the HTTP API also accepts personal access tokens.
OAG exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface and a hosted RapidAPI-backed MCP server; auth is via OAG subscription key / RapidAPI key respectively.
Aviationstack exposes one public integration surface: a REST HTTP API at https://api.aviationstack.com/v1/ authenticated with an `access_key` API key passed as a query parameter.
Aviator exposes a Bearer-authenticated JSON API, a self-describing GraphQL API at app.aviator.co/graphql, public webhook docs, and the `av` CLI which relies on GitHub CLI or a GitHub PAT.

Aviva exposes one public developer surface on aviva.co.uk: the Aviva General Insurance HTTP APIs, accessed via the Aviva API Developer Portal with SSO-based partner portal access; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Avo exposes a Basic-auth REST Public API, an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://mcp.avo.app/mcp, and the Avo CLI authenticated via `avo login`.

AWeber exposes one documented developer surface: the OAuth 2.0–protected REST API at https://api.aweber.com/1.0 with OpenAPI spec https://api.aweber.com/swagger.yaml; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs.
awslabs.github.io exposes three public MCP surfaces: AWS API MCP Server (requires AWS credentials), AWS Knowledge MCP Server (public), and AWS Documentation MCP Server (public).

Axesso exposes only HTTP REST APIs (Amazon, Walmart, Yahoo Finance, YouTube, Zillow, Home Depot, Kaufland, Otto, ASOS, Naver, Lidl, Zalando, plus account/dataset APIs) via its API portal at axesso.developer.azure-api.net, and access is authenticated by creating an Axesso API Portal account/signing in and then using the portal-issued API credentials/subscriptions for the chosen product.
axil.gitlab.io exposes one authenticated HTTP integration surface: the GitLab repository API at https://gitlab.com/api/v4, using a GitLab personal access token in the PRIVATE-TOKEN header.
Axiom exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated CLI, and one authenticated MCP server; all accept the Axiom personal access token (`xapt-`).
Azure exposes Azure CLI, Azure Developer CLI, and an Azure MCP server; CLI surfaces require Microsoft Entra ID sign-in, while MCP auth was not pinned down from the available docs.
Azure Container Apps exposes a management-plane HTTP API and Azure CLI (`az containerapp`, `az containerapp up`), both authenticated with Microsoft Entra ID via `az login`/Bearer access tokens.

b-cdn.net (bunny.net) exposes REST APIs only: Core Platform, Storage, Stream, and Edge Scripting; all use `AccessKey`-based auth, while docs also point to additional HTTP APIs for Shield, Origin Errors, CDN Logging, and Magic Containers.
B12 exposes a public MCP server at https://b12.io/mcp; no other developer-facing HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were confirmed from the available docs.

Back4app exposes a Parse REST API at https://parseapi.back4app.com with app-id plus one of REST/JavaScript/Client keys, plus docs-driven API reference and an MCP page; no public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Backstage exposes a documented Software Catalog HTTP API requiring a Backstage Bearer token; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

BALlDONTLIE exposes one sports data HTTP API, an Account HTTP API, Webhooks HTTP API, and an MCP server; all documented via OpenAPI/spec URLs, with API key auth for the HTTP APIs and MCP auth undetermined.
Ballpark exposes a GraphQL API and a remote MCP server; both require OAuth-based auth, with a long-lived personal access token available for local/internal GraphQL use.
BambooHR exposes one public developer surface: a REST HTTP API at `https://{companyDomain}.bamboohr.com/api/`, authenticated either by OAuth 2.0 app tokens or per-user API keys via HTTP Basic Auth.

Bandsintown exposes one public developer surface: a REST/HTTP Public API at rest.bandsintown.com authenticated with a per-artist API key passed as `app_id` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

Bandwidth exposes an authenticated HTTP API, CLI (`band`), and MCP server; the HTTP API uses OAuth2 client-credentials API Credentials or legacy API User basic auth, the CLI logs in with API Credentials, and the MCP server uses Bandwidth API user credentials plus an account ID.
Barceló MCP exposes one authenticated MCP server at https://barcelo-mcp.com/mcp using OAuth client credentials / OAuth2 via the listed authorization and registration endpoints.
Base44 exposes an OAuth-authenticated MCP server, a public docs MCP server, and the Base44 CLI; no public HTTP/GraphQL API surface was confirmed from the docs read.
Baseten exposes three authenticated HTTP APIs (Management, Inference, Frontier Gateway) plus two CLIs (Baseten CLI and Truss), all using Baseten API keys.
Google Cloud Batch exposes one REST API (v1/v1alpha) and the gcloud `batch` CLI; both use Google OAuth2/ADC via `gcloud auth login` or application-default credentials.

BBC’s public developer surface found was the NEWSHUB Content REST API at https://api.newshub.bbc.co.uk/v1, which requires an `apikey` header; BBC Developer Portal is employee-only and not a public integration surface.

bbc.com exposes one documented public integration surface, the BBC Information Syndication API, authenticated with a query-string API key; the developer portal itself is access-restricted and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
BBC bbci.co.uk web properties are public-facing with no discoverable developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface from the pages reviewed.

Beautiful.ai exposes one authenticated HTTP API (`https://beautiful.ai/api/v1`) using a Bearer API key, plus one authenticated MCP server (`https://openai-mcp-696419881849.us-central1.run.app/mcp`) using OAuth via Beautiful.ai provisioning.
beehiiv exposes one HTTP API (Bearer auth via API key or OAuth2) and one MCP server at /_mcp/server (workspace-connected auth via the MCP setup flow); no CLI or GraphQL surface found.

BeezUP exposes one authenticated REST API v2.0 and one authenticated GraphQL API; public docs link to the API portal but the exact credential mechanics could not be fully retrieved from the scraped docs.
Benchling exposes one REST API surface (v2), a public webhooks reference, and the Python SDK/CLI; auth is via API key or app client credentials.

Benevity exposes one public developer HTTP API surface (Benevity API) with OpenAPI spec at https://developer.benevity.org/resources/openapi.yaml, authenticated by Benevity-issued client credentials for Basic auth to mint JWTs and Bearer JWTs for API calls.

Benevity exposes two public developer surfaces: the authenticated Benevity REST API (prod and staging) using client credentials/JWT, and a public nonprofit MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was found.
Berlin Group exposes one public MCP surface at https://www.berlin-group.org/_api/mcp; no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI developer surfaces were found from the provided docs.
Betfair exposes the Exchange REST API and Exchange Stream API; REST requires an application key plus session token, while stream auth could not be fully pinned from accessible docs.
Bethmardutho.org exposes only public HTTP websites/resources from the pages found; no authenticated APIs, GraphQL, MCP servers, or CLIs were discovered.
Better.com exposes a single documented developer integration surface: an OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect HTTP auth server with dynamic client registration; no public REST/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was found.

Better Stack exposes three authenticated HTTP APIs (Telemetry, Uptime, Errors), an OAuth-capable MCP server at https://mcp.betterstack.com, and uses bearer API tokens plus an OAuth client registration flow for access.
BeyondCorp exposes one public developer surface: the BeyondCorp REST API at https://beyondcorp.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
bhagavadgita.io exposes a public website and public GitaGPT page, plus a RapidAPI-hosted Bhagavad Gita HTTP API requiring a RapidAPI key in `X-RapidAPI-Key` and `X-RapidAPI-Host`.

bharatpetroleum.com exposes one developer-facing integration surface: the BPCL SMS Pro HTTP API, documented on ebiz.bpc.co.in, with application credentials required; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
BigCommerce exposes REST and GraphQL developer APIs plus a public MCP docs surface; REST/GraphQL use API account OAuth and storefront/private API tokens, while MCP is documented as public.

Bigdata.com exposes a REST API, a Remote MCP server, and a beta CLI; all use the same user-minted API key, with the REST API and MCP server authenticated via `X-API-KEY`/`x-api-key` and the CLI via `bigdata configure` or `BIGDATA_API_KEY`.

BigDataCloud exposes one authenticated REST API surface, one authenticated GraphQL surface, and one public client-side free API surface; auth is via a single API key sent as `key` query param or `x-bdc-key` header, with MCP documented in llms.txt as an npm server but the provided well-known MCP endpoint returned 404.
BigDataCloud has one primary credential (API key) and four integration surfaces: REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, an MCP server, and an MCP CLI package; the free client-side API package is public.

BigGeo’s publicly discoverable developer surface is Datalab, a web-based product portal with documentation; no public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration surface or mintable credential could be confirmed from available pages.
BigOven exposes two REST API surfaces (v2 preferred, v1 legacy), both requiring an API key and optionally user-level HTTP Basic auth for private/user actions; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
BigQuery Data Transfer exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP server; the API uses Google Cloud service account/ADC auth.
BigQuery Reservation API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at https://bigqueryreservation.googleapis.com, using Google Cloud OAuth/Application Default Credentials via `Authorization: Bearer`.
Big Red Cloud has one public HTTP API (REST and OData) authenticated by either API key or OAuth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Cloud Bigtable Admin exposes one authenticated REST Admin API and one public MCP server endpoint; both are tied to Google OAuth/service-account credentials for the API, while the MCP endpoint is public.

surfaces.name: Cloud Bigtable Admin API MCP server

domains.summary: Bike Index exposes two REST HTTP APIs (v2 and v3), both authenticated with OAuth2 access tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the public docs.
BikeWise exposes one public integration surface: an HTTP API v2 at https://bikewise.org:443/api/v2, apparently API-key protected; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
BILL exposes two public developer HTTP surfaces: the BILL v3 API (API login/basic auth) and the Spend & Expense API (Bearer API token); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

billingbudgets.googleapis.com exposes the Cloud Billing Budget REST API (v1/v1beta1) over https://billingbudgets.googleapis.com/ with Google OAuth2 required, plus a public MCP server at https://billingbudgets.googleapis.com/mcp.

Billingo exposes one public developer API surface (REST API v3 at https://api.billingo.hu/v3, authenticated with an `X-API-KEY`) plus a Smithery-hosted MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

Binance exposes Spot REST, Spot WebSocket API, Spot WebSocket market streams, and Spot FIX; only the REST/WS/FIX trading surfaces require a Binance API key, while market-data WebSocket streams are public.
Binary Authorization exposes one REST HTTP API and gcloud CLI commands; both use Google Cloud OAuth/ADC credentials.

BINTable exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated HTTP BIN lookup API at https://api.bintable.com/v1 using a BINTable-issued API key passed as `api_key` query parameter; OpenAPI/Swagger specs are published.
BioRender exposes a public MCP connector at mcp.scalekit.com/biorendermcp, authenticated by the user’s BioRender account/OAuth login.

Bitly exposes one REST API at https://api-ssl.bitly.com/v4 (OpenAPI spec https://dev.bitly.com/v4/v4.json) and one MCP server at https://api-ssl.bitly.com/v4/mcp; both accept Bitly API tokens, and the MCP server also supports OAuth 2.1 with DCR.
Bitbucket Cloud exposes one public developer surface: the REST API at https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0, authenticated via access tokens, app passwords, or OAuth 2.0.
Bitly exposes one authenticated REST API (Bearer API token) and one authenticated MCP server (OAuth 2.1 or Bearer API token); no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

Bitquery exposes a required-auth GraphQL API and a required-auth MCP server; both use a Bitquery account/OAuth-based sign-in, with the MCP endpoint at https://mcp.bitquery.io and the GraphQL endpoint at https://streaming.bitquery.io/graphql.

Blackbaud’s public developer integration surface is SKY API: one REST/HTTP API at https://api.sky.blackbaud.com, authenticated by both a SKY API subscription key and an OAuth 2.0 Bearer access token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

Blacksmith.sh exposes one documented HTTP API (dashboardbackend.blacksmith.sh/api, auth via GitHub-backed session cookie) and one CLI surface (blacksmith testbox, auth via CLI login/session token); no public GraphQL or MCP endpoint is documented.
BlazeMeter exposes one REST API surface and two MCP servers, all requiring BlazeMeter account auth/API key; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
BlazeSQL exposes one documented HTTP Query Agent API and one MCP server; both require a BlazeSQL API key or OAuth sign-in via the MCP flow, and no public GraphQL or CLI surface was found.
Blender.org exposes one public MCP surface: the Blender MCP Server; no developer auth credential was identified.

Blockscout exposes a required-auth PRO HTTP API at https://api.blockscout.com/v2/api using the `apikey` query param with a PRO API key, a required-auth GraphQL API (e.g. `https://eth-sepolia.blockscout.com/api/v2/graphql`) using `Authorization: Bearer` with that key, a public MCP server at https://mcp.blockscout.com with no auth, and an Autoscout management API at https://autoscout.services.blockscout.com requiring an `x-api-key` from deploy.blockscout.com/api-keys.
Blogger exposes one public developer surface: the Blogger API v3 HTTP API at https://blogger.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
BlueConic exposes an authenticated tenant MCP server and a REST API docs portal; no public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed from the available docs.
Bluente exposes one authenticated REST Translation API, an authenticated MCP server backed by the same API key, and webhook callbacks documented separately; no public API surface was confirmed.
Blue exposes a GraphQL API, a REST API, and a remote MCP server; all require authentication via a Blue API key or PAT, with no public developer surface found.

Bluesky exposes one documented HTTP/XRPC API surface (public AppView plus authenticated PDS-routed calls); auth uses either app passwords or atproto OAuth client credentials via `Authorization: Bearer`.
Blue (bluesuite.app / blue.app) exposes a GraphQL API, REST API, MCP server, and CLI; auth is via API key/PAT credentials, with MCP requiring three headers and the CLI using Blue login.
BMC exposes the Control-M Automation API as an authenticated HTTP API and the `ctm` CLI; both use Control-M user credentials, and no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed.
boggio-analytics.com exposes one developer integration surface: the Football Prediction API HTTP service, authenticated with a RapidAPI subscription key via `X-RapidAPI-Key`.
Boleron publicly exposes a customer policies portal reachable through email-based login flows; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Bond.tech exposes one developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API at the Bond sandbox base URL, with Bond-issued Identity and Authorization headers; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

BookDirect.com exposes one public developer surface: a REST HTTP API v1 at https://staging.bookdirect.com/api/v1, authenticated with a user session JWT passed as Authorization: Bearer <token>; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs.

Booking.com exposes two public developer APIs: Connectivity APIs (machine account auth via Bearer token or legacy Basic) and Demand API (affiliate headers `X-Affiliate-Id` + API key); Data Portability and metasearch docs were found but not enough for a separate authenticated surface spec.
Google Books exposes a public Embedded Viewer API and a Books API v1 HTTP surface at https://books.googleapis.com/books/v1, authenticated by either an API key or a Google OAuth 2.0 access token.
Botpress exposes the Botpress Cloud API over HTTP with Bearer token auth, plus the `adk` CLI authenticated via `adk login` / `BOTPRESS_TOKEN`; no MCP or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Botpress exposes one authenticated Cloud HTTP API plus two authenticated CLIs (`adk` and `bp`), all using a Botpress personal access token.

Box exposes a REST API at https://api.box.com/2.0 with OAuth 2.0, client-credentials, and developer-token access, plus a hosted MCP server at https://mcp.box.com that uses OAuth; the legacy self-hosted MCP path is deprecated/unknown.

Box exposes one HTTP REST API at https://api.box.com/2.0 and one hosted MCP server at https://mcp.box.com; both require Box-issued OAuth-style access tokens, with developer token, OAuth 2.0 app, JWT app, and client-credentials app options for the API.
BPUT exposes a user portal, evaluation portal, syllabus details page, and results area; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
BraintreePayments exposes one public developer surface: a GraphQL API, authenticated via a sandbox account for the Explorer; no REST, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Braintrust exposes one authenticated REST API (`https://api.braintrust.dev/v1`) and one authenticated MCP server (`https://api.braintrust.dev/mcp`); both use a Braintrust API key via `Authorization: Bearer ...`, and the CLI (`bt`) authenticates with either OAuth login or the same API key.

Brand24 exposes one documented HTTP API (API key via `X-Api-Key`) and one MCP surface at `https://mcp.brand24.com/v1/mcp` using OAuth; the ChatGPT app is an OAuth-connected wrapper over the MCP/Brand24 account.

Brandfolder exposes a single public developer surface: the Brandfolder API v4 REST API at https://brandfolder.com/api/v4, authenticated with a user-scoped Bearer API key from Brandfolder profile integrations.
BoldSign exposes one REST API and one hosted MCP server; both accept either a BoldSign API key or BoldSign OAuth app-based bearer auth.
Brave.com exposes the Brave Search API (HTTP) and a Brave Search MCP server; both use a Brave Search API key.
Braze exposes a documented REST API and an MCP server; both require Braze workspace credentials, with REST authenticated by the REST API key.
Braze exposes a REST API and an MCP server; both require authentication, with REST using a workspace REST API key and MCP using OAuth-based client credentials.
Brevo exposes one HTTP API, one MCP server, and one CLI; HTTP supports API key or OAuth 2.0, MCP supports API key or OAuth, and the CLI uses OAuth login.

Brex exposes one public developer API surface (REST/OpenAPI, base URL https://api.brex.com) authenticated with a Brex user token, plus one MCP surface at https://api.brex.com/mcp authenticated via OAuth session or API key bearer token.

Brex exposes REST/OpenAPI developer APIs at https://api.brex.com and an MCP server at https://api.brex.com/mcp; both require auth, with the APIs using Bearer user tokens and MCP supporting either OAuth or Bearer tokens.
BridgeDb exposes one public REST webservice at https://webservice.bridgedb.org/; no public developer-issued auth, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was documented.
BridgeDb exposes one public HTTP/OpenAPI webservice at https://webservice.bridgedb.org/ with spec https://webservice.bridgedb.org/swagger.yaml and no documented developer credential auth.
Bright Data exposes one HTTP API, one remote MCP server, and a CLI; all require either the Bright Data API key or CLI OAuth/login, and the MCP server uses the API key in its `token` query parameter.

BritBox exposes one public developer surface: the BritBox Application API HTTP/OpenAPI service at https://app-api.backstage-dev.britbox.com, authenticated with a user bearer session token obtained via its login/register flow.

Browser Use exposes a v3 REST API and a hosted MCP server that both use a Browser Use API key (`X-Browser-Use-API-Key`/`x-browser-use-api-key` from `cloud.browser-use.com/settings`), plus a local stdio MCP server via `browser-use --mcp` that needs `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, and a CLI cloud auth flow via `browser-use auth login`.

surfaces.name: Browser Use Cloud REST API v3

domains.summary: Browserbase exposes one documented public integration API surface: a hosted MCP server at `https://mcp.browserbase.com/mcp` requiring a Browserbase API key, plus the `browse` CLI requiring the same API key via `BROWSERBASE_API_KEY`.
Browshot exposes one authenticated REST API and a command-line interface, both using the same account API key passed as `key`.
Buf exposes an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://buf.build/mcp and a buf CLI; no separate HTTP/GraphQL API surface was confirmed from the pages reviewed.
Buffer exposes one authenticated GraphQL API, an authenticated `buffer` CLI, and an MCP integration mentioned in docs but with undisclosed endpoint/auth mechanics.
BugSnag exposes three developer surfaces on bugsnag.com: Data Access API, Error Reporting & Session Tracking APIs, and the BugSnag CLI; all use the BugSnag project API key.

surfaces.name: BugSnag Error Reporting & Session Tracking APIs

domains.summary: BuildBuddy exposes one authenticated Enterprise HTTP API, an Enterprise MCP server, and the `bb` CLI; API auth uses a BuildBuddy API key in `x-buildbuddy-api-key`.
Builder.io exposes an authenticated MCP server plus authenticated HTTP/GraphQL developer APIs: Content API, Admin API, and Write API.
Buildkite exposes a REST API, GraphQL API, MCP server, and `bk` CLI; auth is via Buildkite API access tokens or OAuth tokens, with CLI login supported through `bk auth login`.
Fern exposes one authenticated HTTP public API, a public MCP server, and a CLI documented on the site.

BulkSMS exposes one documented programmatic surface: the JSON REST API at https://api.bulksms.com/v1 with OpenAPI YAML and HTTP Basic Auth using a BulkSMS API token; EAPI and SMPP are listed as developer surfaces but their auth mechanics weren’t fully determined.
Bump.sh exposes a public Portal API, an authenticated Workspace API, an MCP server at https://developers.bump.sh/mcp, and the Bump CLI (`bump`).
Bungie exposes one public developer surface: the Bungie.Net HTTP API at https://www.bungie.net/Platform, authenticated either by X-API-Key alone or by X-API-Key plus a user OAuth access token.
Bungie.net exposes one public developer surface: the Bungie.Net HTTP API at https://www.bungie.net/Platform, authenticated with an X-API-Key and optionally user OAuth 2.0 for scoped endpoints.
Bunnyshell exposes a bearer-token-authenticated public API, a CLI (`bns`) authenticated via `bns login`/token env, and an MCP server that uses the same access token.
Buser provides only public AI-oriented travel listing pages and llms.txt/llms-full.txt content; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, CLI, or issued developer credentials were found.

BusyMac exposes BusyCal with local AppleScript and Claude Desktop MCP automation, plus calendar sync integrations for Google, CalDAV, Exchange/Office 365, and Microsoft Graph; no public HTTP/GraphQL API or CLI package was identified for busymac.com itself.

surfaces.name: BusyCal Claude Desktop Extension (MCP)
surfaces.url: localhost (local BusyCal connection on your Mac)

domains.summary: ButterCMS exposes a REST API at https://api.buttercms.com/v2 authenticated by read/write API tokens, plus a public Image CDN at https://cdn.buttercms.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Buy Me a Coffee exposes one REST API secured by developer bearer tokens and a webhook delivery surface signed with `x-signature-sha256`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

byautomata.io exposes two authenticated REST APIs: Automata Market Intelligence (`api.byautomata.io`, OpenAPI JSON available) and CompetitiveIntel (`api.competitiveintel.io`, OpenAPI JSON available), both using `x-api-key` API keys.
bygravity.com exposes Orion docs and one authenticated remote MCP server at https://g.runorion.com/mcp; no public HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
caisy.io exposes two authenticated GraphQL APIs (external and internal) plus an authenticated Tus-based asset upload HTTP surface; no public or MCP/CLI surface was found.

Cal.com exposes one REST API v2, a hosted MCP server, a self-hosted MCP server, and a CLI; auth is via OAuth client or API key for the API, OAuth for hosted MCP, API key for self-hosted MCP/CLI, with legacy Platform credentials deprecated.
Google Calendar exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Calendar API v3) and one authenticated MCP server; both use Google OAuth-based access.
Calendly exposes a REST API v2 and a hosted MCP server; auth uses personal access tokens or OAuth 2.1 for the API, and MCP uses OAuth via DCR with well-known discovery.
Call Control exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API documented on its docs page, authenticated with a Call Control API key passed as a query parameter.

CallFire exposes a REST API v2 with HTTP Basic auth via API credentials, plus a legacy v1.1 API and a Swagger UI page whose underlying OpenAPI endpoint currently errors; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Calorieappguide.com appears to expose no confirmed public developer integration surface beyond an unresolvable MCP server-card endpoint; auth could not be determined.
Calorie App Guide exposes a public MCP server at https://calorieappguidemcp-production.up.railway.app/mcp with no authentication.
CalorieCam exposes one public developer-facing HTTP partner API surface; auth could not be determined from the available docs.
Calorie GPT exposes one public integration surface: a password-protected web app; no API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
cambase.io exposes only a public website; no documented developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

ActiveCampaign exposes an HTTP API, App Studio/CX App developer surface, and a remote MCP server; auth is via ActiveCampaign OAuth2 app credentials, while the MCP endpoint mechanics remain undetermined.
Campfire exposes a token-authenticated HTTP API and a token-authenticated MCP server at https://api.meetcampfire.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

CamScanner exposes one public developer surface: a CamScanner Developer API portal at dev.camscanner.com, gated by a developer account; no other public HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration surface was found.
Canada Holidays exposes one public REST API with an OpenAPI spec; no auth, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

Candid exposes HTTP APIs for Essentials, Premier, Grants, News, Charity Check (single/bulk/PDF), PDF, and Nonprofit Eligibility; all documented authenticated APIs use Candid subscription keys via header and/or query parameter, with no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface confirmed.
Candid Health exposes one documented HTTP API and one MCP server; API auth uses either client ID+secret or an API key, and the MCP server is streamable HTTP at the docs endpoint.
Canny exposes one public HTTP API and one MCP OAuth proxy; both are authenticated, with the API using a secret `apiKey` POST parameter and the MCP surface using OAuth.

Canva exposes authenticated Connect, Admin, SCIM, and CLI surfaces plus a public Dev MCP server; Connect and Admin use OAuth-based client credentials/PKCE, SCIM uses a long-lived bearer token, and the CLI logs in via `canva login`.
Canva exposes one REST/OpenAPI surface (Connect APIs), one SCIM REST surface, one remote MCP server, and the Canva CLI; all require auth.
Canvas Medical exposes one public developer-facing HTTP surface (Canvas FHIR API) and one CLI surface (canvas), both authenticated; no GraphQL or MCP endpoint was confirmed from the official docs.
Capacities exposes one REST API and one MCP server; both use user-minted bearer OAuth-style access tokens, and no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Carbon Arc exposes one authenticated HTTP API plus two authenticated MCP endpoints; all use the same Carbon Arc API token / MCP connector flow.

Carbon Arc exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.carbonarc.co/v2, documented by OpenAPI at https://api.carbonarc.co/openapi.json and using a bearer API token issued from the Carbon Arc dashboard.
CardChain AI exposes one public integration surface: an MCP connector at https://api.cardchain.ai/mcp/claude, authenticated via a CardChain AI account OAuth flow.
CARFAX exposes a public consumer website with unknown programmatic auth, plus a separate Wholesale CARFAX HTTP API authenticated by `x-api-key`.
CarGurus exposes only public website-based shopping/research tools; no developer HTTP API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found from the discovered docs.

surfaces.name: CarGurus website tools and shopping pages

domains.summary: Carta exposes one public developer API platform (OAuth2-protected REST APIs) and a separate Carta CRM API plus CRM MCP server; I found no standalone public GraphQL or generic CLI surface.
Cartesia exposes one HTTP API, one MCP server, and one CLI; auth is via Cartesia API key, short-lived access token, and an admin API key for admin endpoints.
CashbackHive exposes an MCP server protected by OAuth 2.1 dynamic client registration; no public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surface was confirmed.
Catalysis Hub exposes a public GraphQL API, a web data browser/site, and CatHub CLI tools; I could confirm no auth for GraphQL, while site/CLI auth remains unclear.
Cazoo exposes a public website and mobile app only; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or developer CLI surface or service-issued credentials were found.
CData exposes a REST API, two MCP servers (data and management), and a Connect AI Embed REST API; auth is Basic with PAT or OAuth for the main API/MCPs, and JWT for Embed.

CDC exposes three developer surfaces: the public Content Services API, the authenticated NIO Autocoder API, and the authenticated MVPS Reports External API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

Cdiscount Marketplace’s developer surface is the Octopia Seller REST API, authenticated with clientId/clientSecret-derived Bearer tokens plus SellerId header; SOAP is permanently decommissioned and no MCP/GraphQL/CLI surface was found.
AWS Cost Explorer API on https://ce.us-east-1.amazonaws.com uses AWS SigV4 authentication; no other public integration surfaces were found.
Cerebras.ai exposes a single public developer surface: the Cerebras Inference HTTP API authenticated with a Cerebras API key, plus a Cerebras Code MCP server that uses the same API key.

Google Cloud Certificate Manager exposes one authenticated HTTP API at certificatemanager.googleapis.com and the gcloud certificate-manager CLI; both use Google Cloud user credentials via `gcloud auth login`.

Chaingateway exposes one authenticated REST API (OpenAPI at https://chaingateway.io/openapi.yaml) using a bearer API token, plus public webhook-signature verification docs with a per-user personal secret; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Mapped the public integration surface for the given domain to Amazon S3 REST in us-west-1, authenticated with AWS SigV4 (IAM credentials); no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.
Channel 4 exposes no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found; the only programmatic-ish integration surface identified was the authenticated 4ideas/Commissioning web portal.

Channel99 exposes a secured HTTP/REST API for reporting/bulk data transfer at https://pulsar.channel99.com (Swagger docs at https://pulsar.channel99.com/docs/#/), authenticated with a Channel99-issued Bearer access token in the Authorization header, plus an MCP server whose auth is not yet determined.

Chargebee exposes one public REST Billing API and MCP servers; the Billing API uses HTTP Basic auth with a site API key, and the Data Lookup MCP server supports either MCP Bearer API key auth or OAuth.
ChartMogul exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated remote MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Public MCP server at https://chatgpt-app-with-next-js-t66k.vercel.app/mcp; no other developer surfaces found.

Chatwoot exposes three developer surfaces: Application APIs, Client APIs, Platform APIs, and a Chatwoot CLI; all require authentication, with user access tokens for Application APIs and CLI, platform app access tokens for Platform APIs, and inbox/contact identifiers for Client APIs.
cheat-database.com exposes one public website surface; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced, and member login uses a standard site account.
Checkatrade exposes only its public website and content pages; I found no documented developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface.

Checkly exposes one HTTP REST API, one CLI, and one MCP server; all require Checkly credentials (API key + account ID for API/CLI env auth, or CLI browser login, with MCP using OAuth-based client auth).
Checkmarx exposes a Checkmarx One public HTTP API portal and a Checkmarx MCP server; both require a Checkmarx One account/tenant, with no confirmed public unauthenticated developer surface.
Checkout.com exposes a REST API and an MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced from the docs I found.

Checkr exposes a REST API plus Customer MCP, Candidate MCP, Partner API, and Embeds; auth uses an API key, partner OAuth app credentials, MCP OAuth/DCR for customers, and Candidate MCP client credentials.
Greater Chennai Corporation exposes one public integration surface: an authenticated HTTP/OpenAPI certificates API via Apisetu for birth and death certificate verification/downloads.
Chessvia.ai exposes one public HTTP surface, the Chess Analysis API at https://chessvia.ai/api; authentication could not be determined from the available docs.
CHOMP exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated HTTP Food Database API using a CHOMP API key sent in the `API Key` header.
Choreo exposes a CLI (`choreo`) and an MCP server started via `choreo start-mcp-server`; both authenticate through the same Choreo CLI login session created with `choreo login`.
Chroma exposes a required-auth Core/Cloud HTTP API, a Chroma CLI, and MCP servers for Anthropic/package search; auth is via Chroma Cloud API key, with tenant ID persisted in CLI/cloud profiles.

Chromatic exposes a CLI authenticated by project token, and a published Storybook MCP server authenticated via Chromatic sign-in/OAuth; no public HTTP or GraphQL API surface was confirmed from the docs read.

Chrome Management API only: a single authenticated HTTP/REST surface at `https://chromemanagement.googleapis.com/v1`, using Google OAuth access tokens or service-account impersonation tokens in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Chrome Policy API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at https://chromepolicy.googleapis.com/v1, using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
Chrome UX Report exposes two HTTP surfaces on https://chromeuxreport.googleapis.com: the CrUX API and CrUX History API, both requiring a Google API key passed as `key` query parameter.
Chronograph exposes a GraphQL API and an MCP server at https://mcp.chronograph.pe/mcp; the MCP server requires a Chronograph API key, while GraphQL auth details remain unclear from public docs.

Chronosphere exposes one documented HTTP Config API and one MCP server, both authenticated with Chronosphere API tokens; the Config API uses `API-Token`, while MCP accepts Bearer token auth (or OAuth) via remote HTTP endpoint.

circl.lu exposes the public Vulnerability-Lookup HTTP API at https://vulnerability.circl.lu/api (OpenAPI-linked swagger.json), with public browse/search endpoints and authenticated user features for comments, bundles, sightings, and user management; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.
Circleback exposes a remote MCP server, a CLI, and webhook-based exports; MCP and CLI use Circleback OAuth login, while no separate public REST/GraphQL API surface was confirmed from the docs.

CircleCI exposes one documented REST API surface (API v2), an MCP server, and the `circleci` CLI; API v2 uses CircleCI personal API tokens or project API tokens via `Circle-Token`, while MCP/CLI auth mechanics were not pinned down from the pages read.

Cisco exposes Meraki Dashboard API v1, Cisco Catalyst Center API 3.1.3, and a public DevNet Content Search MCP server; Meraki uses API key or OAuth2 bearer auth, Catalyst Center uses basic auth to obtain a short-lived token, and the MCP server is public.

Citrix Cloud exposes authenticated HTTP APIs using Citrix Cloud service principal OAuth client-credentials; documented surfaces found were Citrix Cloud APIs, Citrix Endpoint Management Reporting API, Citrix Monitor Service OData API, and Workspace API Preview (auth unclear).
Google Civic Information API exposes one HTTP REST surface at https://www.googleapis.com/civicinfo/v2, authenticated with a Google API key sent as `key` query parameter.

civilsupplieskerala.gov.in exposes one developer integration surface: the Civil Supplies Department, Kerala Ration Card HTTP API via API Setu/OpenAPI, authenticated with an API Setu client ID/API key.

surfaces.name: Civil Supplies Department, Kerala Ration Card API

domains.summary: CKAN exposes an HTTP Action API plus legacy HTTP APIs; no public GraphQL or CLI surface confirmed, and a community MCP server exists but its endpoint/auth are not pinned.
Clarify exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was found.
Clarify exposes one HTTP JSON-RPC API at https://api.clarify.io/v1/rpc, authenticated by either Basic Auth or OAuth 2.0 client-credentials; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Clarity AI offers one authenticated REST API, plus a public widget sandbox and non-programmatic datafeeds; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Clarivate exposes multiple public developer APIs: Web of Science Starter, Web of Science Expanded, Web of Science Journals, Web of Science SUSHI, LSH Data Product Search & Retrieve (GraphQL), LSH AI Agent, and a public DI GraphQL explorer; most require a Developer Portal application and API key, while LSH GraphQL/auth details remain unclear.

surfaces.name: LSH Data Product Search & Retrieve API

domains.summary: Google Classroom exposes one public developer surface: the Classroom REST HTTP API at https://classroom.googleapis.com, authenticated via Google OAuth client credentials or a Workspace service account with domain-wide delegation.

Claude (Anthropic) exposes an authenticated HTTP API, an authenticated Admin API on the same endpoint, an `ant` CLI, and the Messages API’s MCP connector; auth is via API key or Workload Identity Federation, with CLI also supporting `ant auth login` or `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.
clearbit.com exposes one documented HTTP API surface (Clearbit APIs) using a Clearbit API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

ClearBlade exposes one documented HTTP platform API surface plus the cb-cli CLI; API auth is tied to ClearBlade system-level credentials or developer tokens, while CLI auth mechanics were not pinned down from the docs.
Clerk exposes a public MCP server at https://mcp.clerk.com/mcp and an authenticated Clerk CLI (`clerk`) that uses a CLI session via `clerk auth login`.

Clever Cloud exposes a public REST API v2 at https://api.clever-cloud.com/v2 (OpenAPI docs page at /developers/api/v2) authenticated with either an API token or OAuth app; the CLI is documented but its auth mechanics weren’t fully pinned from the pages read.

Clever exposes two documented developer surfaces: the Clever API v3.x HTTP API and Clever Single Sign-On OIDC/OAuth HTTP flows; both require Clever-issued bearer/OAuth credentials, and I did not find a public MCP server or CLI.
ClickHouse Cloud exposes one public integration surface for developers: the authenticated REST API at https://api.clickhouse.cloud/v1, using Basic auth with a ClickHouse Cloud API key.
ClickMeter exposes one public integration surface: a REST API at `https://api.clickmeter.com/v3` authenticated with a bearer API key.

ClickSend exposes one public developer API surface: the REST API v3 at https://rest.clicksend.com/v3, authenticated with Basic Auth using ClickSend username + API key; it also offers an MCP server that uses the same credentials.

Clidrive exposes no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface in the pages found; only public website/catalog and seller-flow HTTP surfaces were identified, with no clear integration credential documented.
Clio exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: Clio Manage and Clio Grow, both using OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Closai exposes a user/inventory integration tied to OAuth-connected accounts and an MCP connector at app.closai.io/mcp/v2/; no public API spec, GraphQL endpoint, or CLI docs were found.
Close exposes one authenticated CRM HTTP API and one authenticated remote MCP server; both accept API key auth, and the MCP server also supports OAuth via Close OAuth apps.
Cloud Elements exposes a documented HTTP Platform API secured by Basic auth using Organization Secret + User Secret; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the public docs.

Cloud Asset Inventory exposes a public MCP server at https://cloudasset.googleapis.com/mcp and a REST API at https://cloudasset.googleapis.com/v1 with authentication not fully determined from the pages read.
Cloud Build exposes one authenticated REST API and the `gcloud builds` CLI; both use Google Cloud credentials via user OAuth or service account auth.
Cloud Channel exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://cloudchannel.googleapis.com/v1; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was identified.
Cloud Deploy exposes one authenticated REST API and the `gcloud deploy` CLI; both use Google OAuth via Google Cloud credentials.
clouderrorreporting.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface found.
Cloud Functions exposes two authenticated HTTP surfaces (v1 and v2 Cloud Functions API) plus the `gcloud functions` CLI; no GraphQL or MCP surface found.
Cloud Identity exposes one developer surface: the Cloud Identity REST API on cloudidentity.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
Cloudinary exposes one authenticated Upload API, one public agent account-creation API, a CLI, and MCP servers; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Google Cloud IoT Core exposes one authenticated HTTP API at cloudiot.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0/Application Default Credentials.
Cloud KMS exposes one authenticated HTTP API at cloudkms.googleapis.com; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was identified.

Cloudmersive exposes multiple HTTPS REST APIs (Virus Scan, Security Threat Detection, Convert, OCR, Validate, Spam Detection, Phishing Detection, DLP, and Fraud Detection) authenticated by a Cloudmersive API key sent in the `Apikey` header, plus an MCP server at `https://cloudmersive.com/mcp-server` that uses an API key in the `api-key` header.

Cloud Private Catalog exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated Cloud Private Catalog HTTP API at cloudprivatecatalog.googleapis.com, using Google Cloud ADC/OAuth Bearer credentials; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

cloudprivatecatalogproducer.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API: Google Cloud Private Catalog Producer API (v1beta1) over https://cloudprivatecatalogproducer.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0 or service account credentials.
Cloud Profiler exposes one authenticated REST API surface and one authenticated RPC/GraphQL-style reference surface; both use Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
Cloud Resource Manager exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API v1 and one public MCP server; auth for the API is Google user OAuth via `gcloud auth login`.

surfaces.name: Google Cloud Resource Manager API v1
Google Cloud Resource Manager MCP server

domains.summary: CloudRF exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API at https://api.cloudrf.com using an API key sent in the `key` header.
Cloud Scheduler exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST API at `https://cloudscheduler.googleapis.com/v1`; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was identified.
Cloud Search exposes one public developer surface: the Cloud Search API v1 HTTP endpoint at https://cloudsearch.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
Cloud Shell exposes one developer surface: the Cloud Shell REST API at cloudshell.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with Google user OAuth credentials.
Cloudsmith exposes a required-auth HTTP API, CLI, and MCP server; all are authenticated with the user's Cloudsmith API key (CLI can mint/use it via `cloudsmith login`).

Google Cloud Support exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Cloud Support API v2 at cloudsupport.googleapis.com) and one public MCP server at https://cloudsupport.googleapis.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
Cloud Tasks exposes a REST/HTTP API and the `gcloud tasks` CLI; both authenticate with Google Cloud IAM credentials via Google account or service account, and no MCP or GraphQL surface was found.
Cloud Trace exposes an unauthenticated MCP server plus authenticated HTTP Trace APIs v1 and v2 using Google Cloud OAuth/access tokens in the Authorization: Bearer header.
Clubhouse exposes one public HTTP API surface at https://www.clubhouseapi.com/api, described by an OpenAPI spec; it uses authenticated bearer access tokens obtained via phone-number verification.

cms.gov exposes three developer surfaces: Marketplace API (API key via `apikey` query param), CMS Data API (public), Blue Button API v2 (OAuth-protected), and QPP Eligibility API (GraphQL with bearer JWT auth).
CNAB Online exposes one public HTTP API for CNAB return-file processing; no auth credentials were documented.
