Cockroach Labs exposes a bearer-authenticated CockroachDB Cloud API and a managed MCP server at `https://cockroachlabs.cloud/mcp`; docs also mention a separate Docs MCP server, but its public auth details are unclear.
Codat exposes a single Basic-auth API key credential for its public HTTP APIs: Platform, Spend Insights, Bank Feeds, Bill Pay, Expenses, and Lending; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Codeberg exposes a Forgejo HTTP API at /api/v1 authenticated with access tokens, plus Git-over-HTTP and Forgejo Actions runner CLI usage; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed.
Codefresh exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated CLI; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed from the public docs.
Coder exposes one REST API plus local and remote MCP servers; REST uses personal session tokens, local MCP uses Coder CLI login, and remote MCP uses OAuth2 via Coder.
CodeRabbit exposes a REST API secured by `x-coderabbitai-api-key`, an MCP client integration with auth details handled during server setup, and the `cr` CLI authenticated via `cr auth login`.

CodeSandbox exposes a private SDK/API surface authenticated by API key (`CSB_API_KEY`) and a public browser-sandboxes define endpoint used by Sandpack; no MCP server or standalone CLI surface was confirmed.
Cogedim exposes one public MCP surface at https://www.cogedim.com/mcp; no authenticated HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI developer surface was found.
Amazon Cognito user pools expose a regional HTTP API at cognito-idp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com, authenticated with AWS SigV4; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified from the docs reviewed.

Cohere exposes two authenticated HTTP API surfaces: the v2 API at https://api.cohere.com/v2 and an OpenAI-compatible API at https://api.cohere.com, both using a Cohere API key via Authorization: Bearer.

surfaces.name: Cohere compatibility API via OpenAI SDK

domains.summary: College Football Data exposes one REST API and one GraphQL API; both require a CFBD API key, and no MCP server or CLI was confirmed.

College Nation exposes a public storefront browsing HTTP surface and a public UCP shopping MCP surface; no developer-minted auth credential was identified from the store docs, despite OAuth seed facts.
color.pizza exposes one public HTTP REST API at https://api.color.pizza/v1 with OpenAPI spec at https://api.color.pizza/openapi.yaml; no required auth, optionally supports X-Referrer tracking.

Column Tax exposes one authenticated HTTP API on sandbox/prod columnapi.com plus webhook delivery to customer endpoints; auth is HTTP Basic for the API and HMAC-signed webhooks, with no other public developer surfaces found.

Amazon S3 exposes one public developer surface here: the REST HTTP API at https://s3.amazonaws.com, authenticated with AWS SigV4 using IAM or root AWS credentials; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified from the domain.

Comet exposes one documented REST API requiring a Comet API key in `Authorization: Bearer ...`, plus two MCP-server docs pages whose connect/auth details were not fully confirmed from accessible pages.
CometChat exposes two authenticated REST APIs (chat and management) plus a public read-only MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced in the docs reviewed.

commentanalyzer.googleapis.com exposes one HTTP API, Perspective Comment Analyzer API, authenticated by either a Google API key or Google OAuth 2.0 access token; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.

Commerce Layer exposes 3 authenticated HTTP surfaces: Core API, Metrics API, and Provisioning API; all use OAuth2-issued bearer access tokens, and Docs/Core/Metrics MCP servers are also available, with the docs MCP public.

commercetools exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated GraphQL API, a public Knowledge MCP, and an authenticated Commerce MCP; all developer auth is based on commercetools API Client credentials.
Common Room exposes one authenticated Core HTTP API, plus authenticated CLI and MCP surfaces; no separate GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Commure’s public integration surface is a SCIM provisioning HTTP API plus authenticated Ambient web and Dictation app access; no public GraphQL, MCP, or developer CLI/API surface was found.

ComplyAdvantage exposes two REST APIs: the legacy ComplyAdvantage API (API key via `Authorization: Token ...`) and the Mesh API docs surface with OAuth-style access-token auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
composer.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Cloud Composer API) and one MCP reference surface; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Composio exposes one REST API, a hosted per-session MCP endpoint, and the Composio CLI; all require Composio-issued project auth, with project or org API keys for REST and project auth for MCP/CLI.

Compute Engine exposes a public MCP server at https://compute.googleapis.com/mcp (no auth) and the Compute Engine v1 HTTP API at https://compute.googleapis.com/compute/v1, which uses Google OAuth2 / Google Cloud credentials.
Conductor exposes one authenticated Data API over HTTP and one authenticated MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced in the docs reviewed.

Confident AI exposes a protected Evals HTTP API, a likely public OTEL ingestion endpoint at https://otel.confident-ai.com with auth not confirmed, and an MCP server project on GitHub with auth mechanics not determined.
ConfigCat exposes a REST Public Management API, a CLI, and an MCP server; all three use the same Public API basic-auth credentials, and the API also supports SDK-key-based value endpoints.

Confluent.io exposes the Confluent Cloud REST API, Metrics API, Stream Catalog GraphQL API, and Confluent CLI; authentication is via Confluent Cloud API keys, with STS bearer tokens additionally supported on Metrics API.

surfaces.name: Confluent Cloud Stream Catalog GraphQL API

domains.summary: Conjur.org exposes a Conjur REST API, a Secrets Manager CLI, and client libraries such as the Ruby API; all require Conjur-issued authentication, with no public unauthenticated developer surface confirmed.
connectors.googleapis.com exposes one developer surface: the Google Cloud Integration Connectors REST API, authenticated with Google OAuth user credentials.
Consensus exposes one HTTP API (`https://api.consensus.app/v1`) using `x-api-key`, plus an MCP server at `https://mcp.consensus.app/mcp` that uses OAuth sign-in/Consensus account access.
Google Cloud Contact Center AI Platform exposes one documented developer surface, the Apps REST API at contactcenteraiplatform.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.

surfaces.name: Google Cloud Contact Center AI Platform Apps API

domains.summary: Contact Center Insights exposes one REST API surface (v1) requiring Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 credentials, plus a public MCP server at https://contactcenterinsights.googleapis.com/mcp with no auth.
Google People API exposes a REST HTTP API and a remote MCP server, both requiring Google OAuth client-based user authorization; no separate CLI or GraphQL surface found.

container.googleapis.com exposes a public MCP server at https://container.googleapis.com/mcp and the authenticated Google Kubernetes Engine REST API at https://container.googleapis.com/v1 using Google OAuth access tokens.

containeranalysis.googleapis.com exposes one developer surface: the Artifact Analysis REST API at https://containeranalysis.googleapis.com/, authenticated with Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 or service account credentials.

Contentful exposes REST APIs for management, delivery, preview, images, SCIM, and experience, plus a GraphQL Content API and the `contentful` CLI; most require bearer tokens, while Images API is public.
Contentsquare exposes three authenticated HTTP APIs (Data Export, Metrics, Enrichment) and an authenticated MCP server; no public developer CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Contentstack exposes four developer surfaces: REST APIs for CDA/CMA/Admin/Analytics, a GraphQL Content Delivery API, and MCP tooling guidance; auth is primarily stack API key plus delivery token, management token, authtoken, or OAuth token depending on the API.
contentwarehouse.googleapis.com exposes the Google Cloud Document AI Warehouse REST API at https://contentwarehouse.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
Parble exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.parble.com/v1 and a public webhooks management/docs surface; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed for contract.fit.
Kit exposes one REST API v4 surface (API key or OAuth2) plus two MCP servers: public developer-docs MCP and authenticated Kit MCP over https://app.kit.com/mcp.
Convex exposes a public HTTP API, a management HTTP API with OpenAPI spec, a CLI, and an MCP server; auth is via deploy keys, team access tokens, and OAuth app bearer tokens.
Coolify exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated read-only MCP server, both using the same Bearer API token.
Coralogix exposes a REST API, an MCP server, and the CX CLI; REST and CLI can use a Coralogix API key, while MCP uses OAuth 2.1/OIDC via supported client login.

CORE exposes one public developer surface: the CORE API at https://api.core.ac.uk/v3, authenticated by a CORE API key obtained via the CORE API registration page; no other public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Corrently IO exposes 9 documented HTTP APIs, all using a Corrently API token via `Authorization: Bearer`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Cortenoi exposes one public MCP surface at /mcp with no documented credentials required; no other developer surfaces were found.
Public MCP server at https://cortenoi-mcp-drmpaogcjq-ew.a.run.app/mcp; no credentials or other integration surfaces found.
Cosmic exposes REST and Dashboard HTTP APIs, a GraphQL API, a hosted/self-hosted MCP server, and the `cosmic` CLI; auth uses bucket read/write keys, PATs, agent keys, and CLI login/token sessions.
cottages.com exposes only a public booking/search website and a logged-in guest account portal; no public REST, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI developer surface was found.
Countly exposes one authenticated HTTP Server API, a CLI, and an MCP server; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Coupler.io exposes a browser-based web app and a hosted/local MCP integration surface; auth is Coupler.io account OAuth, with CLI/MCP connectors using browser consent.
Courier exposes a REST API at https://api.courier.com, an MCP server at https://mcp.courier.com, and a Courier CLI; all use the same Courier API key.
Coursera exposes a public web app at https://www.coursera.org; no public developer API, GraphQL endpoint, MCP server, or CLI was confirmed from the available documentation.

Coveo’s public developer surface is REST APIs for Search, Source, Commerce, Customer Service, Push, and Usage Analytics Write, plus an MCP server and the Coveo CLI; most surfaces use OAuth 2.0 access via the Coveo Platform, the CLI authenticates with `coveo login`, and Push auth remains unspecified in the recorded docs.
Covid API exposes one public HTTP REST surface at https://www.covid-api.com/api with OpenAPI docs at the linked JSON spec; no auth is documented.
Public MCP server at https://cowboy-model-finder-mcp-app-8b3709a7529a.herokuapp.com/mcp with no authentication; no other developer surfaces found.
cowin.gov.in exposes the Co-WIN HTTP API surface at https://cdn-api.co-vin.in/api/v2; it uses a beneficiary JWT in the Authorization Bearer header for protected calls.
cpy.re exposes one public integration surface: the Mealie REST API at https://cpy.re, authenticated with a Mealie access token (bearer token) issued via the API token flow in the docs.
Craft exposes one developer API surface and one MCP surface, both authenticated via per-connection credentials created in the Imagine tab.
Craft CMS exposes a GraphQL API authenticated with bearer tokens and a local CLI (`craft`); no MCP or standalone REST surface was identified from the docs reviewed.
CrbonFree exposes a public MCP server and a Bearer-authenticated REST API with an OpenAPI spec; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
CrbonFree exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server; the REST API uses a bearer token, and the MCP server is set up via `npx @crbonfree/mcp login`.
Create State exposes an authenticated REST API, an authenticated MCP server at https://createstate.ai/mcp/, and a `create-state` CLI; all use a Create State API key.

creativecommons.org exposes public Creative Commons Web Services REST APIs plus the Openverse API; Openverse also has OAuth2 client registration/token auth, and no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

domains.summary: Crediwatch exposes a sales-assisted authenticated HTTP API platform for embedded finance/business intelligence; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

CrewAI exposes a public REST API (`https://crewai.com/api`) and a Streamable HTTP MCP server (`https://crewai.com/mcp`); REST accepts OAuth2 or API-key auth, while MCP is documented as public though bearer tokens can use `mcp.tools.invoke`.
Cronitor exposes a basic-auth HTTP API and Monitor API plus the `cronitor` CLI; both use Cronitor API keys, with a separate telemetry/ping key for CLI ping operations and telemetry ingestion.
Crossbeam exposes a REST Partner API, an MCP server, and a SCIM API; Partner API and MCP use Crossbeam OAuth, while SCIM uses a Crossbeam-issued bearer token.

CrossBrowserTesting exposes one public developer surface: the v3 HTTP API at https://crossbrowsertesting.com/api/v3/ with OpenAPI docs on SwaggerHub; it requires Basic auth using the account email/username plus the service-issued authkey.
CrowdStrike exposes the Falcon REST API (OAuth2 client-credentials) and a Falcon MCP server; no separate CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed from the pages reviewed.
Cruise Critic exposes only authenticated web-session surfaces for the main site and community boards; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Crypto.com exposes a public Market Data MCP server (no auth) and the authenticated Exchange API over REST/WebSocket/FIX using an API key + secret signature flow.
Cube exposes one public HTTP API and one MCP server; both use Cube OAuth bearer tokens, and the API also requires `Accept: application/json; version=1.0`.
Currencycloud exposes one developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

CursorTouch exposes public-facing project repos for Windows-Use, Web-Use, Windows-MCP, Android-MCP, and MacOS-MCP, but I could not confirm any stable developer-authenticated API, GraphQL, CLI, or MCP endpoint details from the available public pages.

custhelp.com exposes Oracle B2C Service REST, SOAP, and Element Manager public REST APIs; all require authentication via Oracle account/session auth or SSO JWT, with no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

domains.summary: Customer.io exposes four developer surfaces: REST APIs (Pipelines, Track, App, and a general APIs hub), an MCP server, and a CLI; authentication details were not fully pinned from the fetched pages.
Google Custom Search exposes one HTTP surface, the Custom Search JSON API, authenticated by an API key plus Programmable Search Engine ID (`cx`).

cvpop-mcp-server.fly.dev exposes one public MCP server at https://cvpop-mcp-server.fly.dev/mcp; Fly.io’s own flyctl MCP server is separately documented and requires a Fly access token via the `Authentication` header.
cvpop.com exposes no documented public developer integration surface; only the consumer web app is visible and its auth could not be determined.

CyberArk exposes several HTTP APIs (Secure Cloud Access, Identity, Secrets Manager SaaS, Secure Infrastructure Access, and Access API); no GraphQL, MCP connect endpoint, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs I read.

Cybertaxonomy exposes one clearly documented developer surface: the CDM remote HTTP API at api.cybertaxonomy.org; public pages did not reveal a machine-readable spec or any confirmed API auth scheme, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.
CyCAT exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.cycat.org with OpenAPI spec https://api.cycat.org/swagger.json; no authenticated developer surface was documented.

D7 Networks exposes one public HTTP REST API surface at https://api.d7networks.com, authenticated with bearer tokens minted from a D7 developer application, plus SDKs in Python, Ruby, Node.js, PHP, and Go that use the same token.

Daft.ie exposes one documented public API surface (API v3) and MCP integrations that either use DAFT_API_KEY or operate locally over stdio without remote auth; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Dafty MCP Server
surfaces.url: stdIO (local process; no remote connect endpoint published)

domains.summary: Dagger exposes a local GraphQL API protected by `DAGGER_SESSION_TOKEN` Basic auth, plus the public `dagger` CLI; no MCP server or separate HTTP REST surface was identified.

Dagster exposes a GraphQL API, an External assets REST API, and CLIs (`dagster`, `dagster-cloud`, `create-dagster`, `dg`); documented API auth uses a Dagster+ user token via `Dagster-Cloud-Api-Token`, while CLI auth mechanics were not pinned down.
Daily exposes one authenticated REST API (`https://api.daily.co/v1`) and one public client SDK surface (`@daily-co/daily-js`); no GraphQL, MCP, or separate CLI surface was found.

Daloopa exposes one REST API at https://app.daloopa.com/api/v2 (Basic auth via email+API key) and one remote MCP server at https://mcp.daloopa.com/server/mcp (OAuth login, bearer token from API key, or direct X-API-KEY).
DaniWeb exposes OAuth2-protected Connect HTTP APIs for v2, v3, and v4; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
api.data.gov exposes one HTTP integration surface requiring an API key, accepted via X-Api-Key header, api_key query param, or HTTP Basic username.
dataatwork.org exposes one public HTTP REST API, Open Skills API, with OpenAPI/Swagger JSON spec and no service-issued credentials required.
Databricks exposes a required-auth REST API and Databricks CLI; I found PAT and OAuth bearer auth, with no GraphQL or MCP surface confirmed.
Datadog exposes one public developer HTTP API surface (API key + application key) and one MCP server surface (OAuth via Datadog connector/app/plugin); no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
dataflow.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API (Dataflow API) and one public MCP server at https://dataflow.googleapis.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
Dataform exposes one public developer surface: the Google Cloud Dataform REST API at dataform.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google Cloud OAuth access tokens via cloud-platform IAM.
DataForSEO exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated DataForSEO API v3 REST API over Basic auth.
Cloud Data Fusion exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://datafusion.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth2 bearer tokens.
DataGrail exposes one authenticated HTTP API (API v2) and one authenticated MCP surface (Vera MCP); no public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Dataplex exposes one public MCP surface (no auth) and one authenticated HTTP/REST API v1 using Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens.
Dataproc exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API and one public MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Datasette exposes a public JSON/HTTP API, a public CLI, and a public GraphQL plugin endpoint; authentication is generally plugin-defined, with only a local root sign-in token documented.
Datasite exposes one authenticated HTTP API program and one authenticated MCP server, both using Datasite OAuth 2.0 account access.
datastore.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface: the Datastore API, using Google Cloud OAuth/Application Default Credentials.
Datastream exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API and one public MCP server at https://datastream.googleapis.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
DatoCMS exposes a public Asset CDN at www.datocms-assets.com, plus authenticated GraphQL CDA, REST CMA, MCP, and CLI integrations.
DatoCMS exposes a GraphQL Content Delivery API, an HTTP Content Management API, an MCP server, and the datocms CLI; auth is via project API tokens, CLI OAuth login, or MCP OAuth.

Datumbox exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP REST-like Machine Learning API at `http://api.datumbox.com/1.0`, authenticated with a Datumbox API key passed as the `api_key` form field; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Day AI exposes an OAuth-protected integrations/API surface and an HTTP MCP server at https://day.ai/api/mcp; both use Day AI OAuth client credentials minted via dynamic registration.

Daytona exposes two documented HTTP APIs: the main Daytona API at https://app.daytona.io/api with API key/JWT/OAuth-based auth, and the Daytona Toolbox API at the sandbox toolbox proxy URL with auth unknown from public docs; CLI and MCP are available via the `daytona` command and `daytona login`.
Dazzly exposes one public integration surface: an MCP server at https://prod.mcp.dazzly.co/mcp, authenticated via Dazzly account login/OAuth; no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI developer surface was found.

DBOS exposes a Conductor/Console HTTP API secured by DBOS API keys, plus a DBOS MCP server that uses the same login/API-key backed Conductor access; no GraphQL or CLI integration surface was confirmed.

dbt.com exposes two developer surfaces: the dbt Cloud REST APIs (v2/v3, base URL https://cloud.getdbt.com/) authenticated with either a personal access token or service account token in the `Authorization` header using `Token <token>` or `Bearer <token>`, and the Discovery API GraphQL endpoint (https://metadata.cloud.getdbt.com/graphql) authenticated with a service token in `Authorization` as `Token` or `Bearer`; dbt also documents an MCP server for local/remote use that connects via OAuth or tokens.
Debian exposes a public CLI integration surface for package management via `apt`/`apt-get`; no Debian-issued developer credentials or public APIs/GraphQL/MCP surfaces were found.
Deel exposes a REST API and an MCP server; REST accepts bearer API tokens and OAuth2 apps, and MCP accepts OAuth2 or PAT-style bearer tokens.
Deepgram exposes one public HTTP API, the dg CLI, and an MCP server; all are authenticated with a Deepgram API key.
DeepInfra exposes two authenticated HTTP API surfaces: an OpenAI-compatible API and a native inference API, both using Bearer API keys or scoped JWTs.

Defense.com exposes an MCP surface at its “AI-Powered Cyber Security” page (auth unknown) plus SIEM-style integration surfaces for Microsoft 365 (my.defense.com, using an Entra ID app/client ID + tenant ID + client secret), Cloudflare (API token), CrowdStrike (OAuth2 API client ID/secret/base URL), and Mimecast (application ID/key, service-account email, secret key), while AWS CloudTrail ingestion relies on a customer-created IAM role with external ID.
Demandbase exposes five JWT-bearer-authenticated HTTP APIs plus an MCP server; no public developer surface or CLI was confirmed.
Depot exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.depot.dev and the Depot CLI/remote-agent command surface; no MCP or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Descope exposes one public developer surface: the Descope REST API with an OpenAPI YAML spec; auth is via Project ID bearer, Project ID + refresh JWT, Management Key, or Access Key.

DEV.to exposes the Forem REST API (v1 and legacy v0) at https://dev.to, authenticated with a user-minted `api-key` header plus required `accept` version header for v1; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

DevCycle exposes a Management API, Bucketing API, hosted MCP server, and DevCycle CLI; auth uses OAuth client credentials/access tokens for management/MCP and SDK keys for bucketing, with CLI supporting SSO login or stored client credentials.
DevRev exposes a PAT-authenticated public REST API at https://api.devrev.ai and a public AirSync MCP integration via `chef-cli mcp initial-mapping`; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

DevZero exposes two authenticated developer surfaces: Terraform and Pulumi providers, both using a team ID plus personal access token; no public HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration surface was confirmed beyond the in-cluster operators described in docs.
Dialogflow exposes a Google-authenticated Dialogflow CX REST API and a public MCP server at https://dialogflow.googleapis.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Dice exposes no public developer API surface in the materials found; the only documented integration surface is employer ATS/batch/bot integration, which requires a Dice-issued secret key obtained through account support.

Diffbot exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for Web Search and DQL Search using a Diffbot API token passed as `token` in the query string; MCP exists in GitHub but the connect endpoint wasn’t confirmed from public docs.

Digits exposes one public developer HTTP API (Digits Connect API) via OAuth 2.0 and one MCP server at https://api.digits.com/mcp using existing Digits account OAuth; no public CLI or GraphQL surface was identified.

Directus exposes a REST API, GraphQL API, and a remote MCP server; authenticate the APIs with a Directus static access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, and authenticate MCP either via MCP OAuth at `https://your-directus-url.com/mcp` or with a static token as `?access_token=<token>`.
Discord exposes REST, Gateway, OAuth2, RPC over IPC, and public webhook endpoints; auth uses bot tokens, OAuth2 user access tokens, and app client credentials.

Discord exposes a REST API, Gateway WebSocket API, and local RPC IPC surface; auth is via bot tokens or OAuth2 client credentials, with the Embedded App SDK documented but not clearly a standalone developer surface.

Discourse exposes one documented HTTP REST API surface with OpenAPI spec at https://docs.discourse.org/openapi.json, authenticated by Api-Key plus Api-Username headers; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Google API Discovery Service exposes a public unauthenticated directory/discovery-doc fetch surface over HTTPS; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were found.
Google Cloud Sensitive Data Protection exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://dlp.googleapis.com/v2 using OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
dnd5eapi.co exposes a public REST API at https://www.dnd5eapi.co/api and a public GraphQL API at https://www.dnd5eapi.co/graphql/2014; no credentials are required.
Cloud DNS exposes one authenticated REST API and the `gcloud dns` CLI; no GraphQL, MCP, or public unauthenticated surfaces found.
DNSimple exposes one public integration API (API v2 over HTTP) and one CLI; both support browser/OAuth login and token-based auth, with API v2 also supporting HTTP Basic and OAuth bearer tokens.

Docker exposes the Docker Hub API, Docker Hub CLI (hub-tool), and the Docker Engine API; Hub auth uses Docker credentials/PAT or org access tokens, while Engine auth is unknown at the API surface level.
Docker.io exposes the Docker Hub HTTP API (OpenAPI at latest.yaml) and the experimental hub-tool CLI; both authenticate with Docker Hub credentials, PATs, or org access tokens.
Docket exposes a public integrations library and references an MCP-based ChatGPT app, but no developer-facing API spec, CLI, or explicit auth details were found.
Docling exposes a public website OpenAPI surface and an MCP server reference; no developer auth credentials were confirmed from the available docs.
docs.googleapis.com exposes the Google Docs API over HTTP and a Google Docs MCP server; both rely on Google OAuth-based access, with no separate public CLI discovered.

Google Cloud Document AI exposes one public developer surface: the Document AI REST API at https://documentai.googleapis.com/ authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens (including ADC/service account flows).

Craft exposes a per-space authenticated HTTP API, a per-space authenticated MCP server, and optional Craft Assistant provider connections for OpenAI or Anthropic API keys; no public unauthenticated developer surface was found.
Docusaurus exposes a public CLI (`docusaurus`) for site development/build/deploy; no public HTTP, GraphQL, or MCP integration surface was found.
DocuSeal exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server; REST uses `X-Auth-Token` API keys, and MCP uses OAuth 2.0.
Dodo Payments exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated MCP server, and one authenticated CLI; API uses a bearer API key, MCP uses browser OAuth, and the CLI can log in with the API key.
Domains Index exposes no documented developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI; only a public website/data-explorer and purchase/member pages, with no integration credential found.
Cloud Domains exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://domains.googleapis.com using Google OAuth bearer tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
domainsdb.info exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.domainsdb.info/v1 using a Google-minted API key passed as `api_key` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Domotz exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated remote MCP server; both are documented publicly, with API-key auth for the REST API and Domotz account OAuth-style sign-in for MCP.

DoorDash exposes three developer HTTP APIs: Drive, Marketplace, and Marketplace for Retailers; Drive and the Marketplace APIs use JWT Bearer auth minted from the Developer Portal access key, while Drive (classic) was found but its auth could not be pinned from the pages read.
Doppler exposes a REST API, CLI, and experimental MCP server; REST and CLI use API/service tokens or CLI login, while MCP auth could not be confirmed from the docs.
dotCMS exposes authenticated REST, GraphQL, and MCP surfaces; all use a dotCMS API token, and the MCP server is launched via `npx -y @dotcms/mcp-server` with `DOTCMS_URL` and `AUTH_TOKEN`.

Dovetail exposes one authenticated public HTTP API, a hosted MCP server at https://dovetail.com/api/mcp, and a self-hosted STDIO MCP server; all use the same Dovetail API token passed as `Authorization: Bearer ...` or `DOVETAIL_API_TOKEN` for the local MCP setup.

Dow Jones Developer Platform exposes one confirmed public integration surface: the Screening and Monitoring REST API, authenticated with a Dow Jones-issued API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
DRACOON exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated DRACOON REST API at https://dracoon.team/api, using OAuth 2.0 client credentials obtained via the DRACOON developer portal.
DraftKings exposes a public HTTP web API at https://api.draftkings.com; no verified developer-issued auth, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
DrChrono exposes a single public developer surface: an OAuth2-protected REST HTTP API at https://app.drchrono.com, documented in the v4 API docs; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Dremio Cloud exposes one REST API, one Iceberg Catalog REST API, Arrow Flight/Flight SQL developer client access, and an MCP server; all require a Dremio PAT or OAuth access token.
Dremio exposes two REST APIs, a Developer CLI, and an MCP server; all require authentication via Dremio bearer tokens, PATs, or OAuth client credentials depending on the surface.
DriveBC exposes one public HTTP/Open511 API at https://api.open511.gov.bc.ca/ with no developer credential required.

DVLA exposes 5 HTTP APIs on driver-vehicle-licensing.api.gov.uk: public Authentication API plus secured Vehicle Enquiry Service, Access to Driver Data, Keeper At Date Of Event, and Print Request Service; secure APIs use DVLA API key alone or API key + JWT via the Authentication API.
Dropbox exposes two developer HTTP surfaces: Dropbox API v2 and Dropbox Business API, both using OAuth 2.0 bearer access tokens; app key/secret are used to register and mint those tokens.

Dropbox exposes authenticated HTTP API v2 surfaces on api.dropboxapi.com and content.dropboxapi.com, plus a public API Explorer; auth is OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens issued via the Dropbox App Console.
dropx.io has no public developer integration surface; its llms.txt indicates the domain is for sale on GoDaddy.

Dub (dub.co) exposes an authenticated REST API (Bearer API key) plus two official remote MCP servers — Dub Links and Dub Partners — both authenticated via an API key passed in the Mcp-Dub-Token header; no public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Duffel exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated v2 HTTP API at https://api.duffel.com/air, using a Duffel access token via Authorization: Bearer.

DVLA exposes 5 public HTTP APIs for developers: Authentication, Access to Driver Data, Vehicle Enquiry Service, KADOE, and Driving Licence Renewal; all are API-key based, with four of them also requiring a JWT via the DVLA Authentication API.

surfaces.name: Keeper At Date Of Event (KADOE) API

domains.summary: dweet.io exposes one public REST API surface at https://dweet.io with OpenAPI spec https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/dweet.io/2.0/swagger.json; no authenticated developer credentials were found.
Dwolla exposes one public HTTP API and one MCP server, both authenticated with a Dwolla OAuth access token minted from application client credentials.
E2B exposes one authenticated HTTP API, a public MCP gateway at https://e2b.dev/mcp, and an authenticated CLI; the HTTP API uses an API key and the CLI uses `e2b auth login`.

EAN-Search exposes three developer surfaces: a token-authenticated REST barcode API, a Bearer-authenticated GraphQL API, and a streamable-http MCP server that uses the same service-issued API token; OAuth client registration/endpoints exist but no separate OAuth-based integration surface was confirmed.
Easy PDF Server exposes one public developer surface: a hosted REST API at https://api.easypdfserver.com/make-pdf requiring the service-issued API key passed in the request body as `key`.

surfaces.name: Easy PDF Server Hosted REST API

domains.summary: EasyPost exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.easypost.com/v2 using Basic auth with an EasyPost API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

eBay offers OAuth-protected REST APIs, legacy Traditional APIs using Auth’n’Auth bearer tokens, plus web-based API/GraphQL explorers and third-party MCP servers; developer app keys and user tokens are minted via the eBay Developers Program.
ebi.ac.uk exposes public GWAS Catalog and PDBe REST APIs plus OxO2 preview, and BioSamples REST API requiring JWT bearer auth.
eDistrict Odisha exposes one public web portal surface for ServicePlus-based citizen service submission; no separate API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was confirmed.

Edmunds exposes four REST HTTP APIs (Vehicle, Editorial, Dealer, Media), all authenticated by a single API key passed as the `api_key` query parameter; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
eDRV exposes one authenticated REST API and public webhook deliveries with optional per-endpoint signature verification; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

edx.org exposes one documented developer integration surface: the authenticated edX Course Catalog REST API at https://api.edx.org/catalog/v1 using client credentials to mint JWT access tokens sent in the Authorization header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Egnyte exposes one public HTTP API, a managed MCP server, and the Egnyte Agentic CLI; all use Egnyte OAuth 2.0, with API keys used to register an app for token issuance.
EHRI Project exposes three public integration surfaces: a bearer-protected experimental Search API, a public GraphQL API, and a public OAI-PMH endpoint; no MCP server or CLI was identified.

Elastic.co exposes the Elastic Cloud API, Elastic Cloud Enterprise API, and Elasticsearch API; auth is via Elastic Cloud API keys for Cloud, and basic auth for Elasticsearch, with no confirmed MCP, GraphQL, or CLI-only integration surface.
AWS Elastic Beanstalk exposes an AWS SigV4-authenticated HTTP API and the EB CLI; no public MCP or GraphQL surface found.
elastiq.ch exposes one public HTTP API surface: the Color Name API at https://api.color.pizza/v1, with no authentication required.
Elastiq’s public developer surface is not exposed on elastiq.click; only marketing pages for Elastiq Discover were found, and no API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI or auth details were discoverable.
Elation Health exposes one documented HTTP API and one remote MCP server; the API uses OAuth 2.0 client-based authentication, and I found no CLI or GraphQL surface.
ElevenLabs exposes one authenticated HTTP API (xi-api-key), an unauthenticated MCP integration endpoint, and the ElevenLabs CLI authenticated via API key.
elmah.io exposes one public REST API v3 and one MCP server; both require elmah.io-issued auth (API key for REST, OAuth session for MCP), and no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Found only an MCP endpoint at https://email-creation-mcp-node-production.up.railway.app; public docs were inconclusive and authentication could not be determined.
Mapped MailerSend API plus HubSpot remote and developer MCP surfaces; no public integration surface was found for email-templates.read itself.
Nitrosend exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI; REST uses Bearer API keys, while MCP and CLI support either browser OAuth login or an API key.

EMPLOYERS appears to offer one public integration surface: a workers’ compensation HTTP API for digital quoting/binding, but the site exposes no machine-readable spec or documented authentication details.

eNanoMapper exposes one developer integration surface: the AMBIT/eNanoMapper REST API at data.enanomapper.net/api-docs, authenticated by an eNanoMapper login session (subjectid apiKey in headers per Swagger docs); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Endor Labs exposes one public REST API, the endorctl CLI, and an MCP server; REST/CLI auth centers on an Endor Labs API-key/secret exchanged for ENDOR_TOKEN, with browser/CLI login paths also supported.
ENECHANGE.co.jp exposes only public corporate/marketing pages; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.

ENECHANGE exposes an OAuth2-protected HTTP integration surface on enechange.jp, with OAuth client registration and mcp_read scope; no separate GraphQL, MCP connect endpoint, or CLI surface was confirmed from the available docs.
energy.gov exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated Asset Score/Audit Template v2 HTTP API, using a user token passed as the `token` query parameter.

Enode exposes a single public developer surface: an HTTP REST API authenticated with OAuth 2.0 client credentials (client_id/client_secret -> Bearer access token); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in docs.
Enterpret exposes one public integration surface: an authenticated MCP server at https://wisdom-api.enterpret.com/server/mcp, using a user-minted Enterpret bearer token in the Authorization header.

env0 exposes a protected HTTP API, Terraform remote-backend endpoint, private registry, CLI, and an MCP server; all use env0-issued API key material, with the remote backend logging in via `terraform login backend.api.env0.com`.
Envoice exposes one authenticated HTTP API (API key + secret in `x-auth-key` and `x-auth-secret` headers); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
EODHD exposes a REST API at https://eodhd.com/api with API-token query auth, plus an MCP server at https://mcp.eodhd.dev/mcp and a WebSocket real-time data surface; MCP supports API-key or OAuth 2.1.
EPA exposes a public DMAP GraphQL API, a DMAP-EF REST service, a Grants GraphQL API, and authenticated FRS query/submit HTTP APIs; auth is via an EPA api.data.gov API key or NAAS credentials for FRS.

surfaces.name: EPA DMAP-EF RESTful Data Service API

domains.summary: Eqivo exposes one public developer surface: a Basic-authenticated REST API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
eSky.com exposes no documented public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface; only the public travel website/web content was confirmed.

Estated exposes two HTTP surfaces: the legacy Property Data API v4 at https://apis.estated.com/v4/property and a separate Sandbox API at https://sandbox.estated.com/v4/property; both authenticate with query `token` API keys.
Etherpad.org exposes one developer surface: the Etherpad HTTP API, authenticated by a per-deployment API key passed as `apikey` in requests.

Ethos exposes one public developer integration surface: a partner HTTP API for embedded life-insurance workflows, gated by an Ethos-issued partner API key used as a Bearer token; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI developer interface was confirmed.

Etraveli Group exposes three public partner-facing HTTP integration surfaces (PRECISION, Tripstack, Wenrix), but no public API spec, MCP server, CLI, or confirmed credential flow was found on the reviewed pages.
Europeana exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Search and Record) using `wskey` API keys, plus a public IIIF HTTP API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
EveMarketer exposes a public HTTP market-data API with no documented auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Eventarc exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://eventarc.googleapis.com; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs.

Eventbrite exposes one public developer surface: the REST API at https://www.eventbriteapi.com/v3, authenticated with OAuth bearer/private tokens; app partners also use app key + client secret in the OAuth flow.
Every exposes one developer integration surface: an OAuth-protected Admin MCP server at https://admin-mcp.every.ai/; the public website/docs mention no public HTTP/GraphQL API or CLI.
evetech.net exposes the EVE Online ESI REST API; auth is via EVE SSO OAuth2 access tokens, with a separate SSO app credential for app registration.

Exa offers HTTP APIs for Search, Contents, and Answer at `https://api.exa.ai` using your Exa API key via `x-api-key` or `Authorization: Bearer`, plus MCP servers at `https://mcp.exa.ai/mcp` and `https://websetsmcp.exa.ai/mcp` authenticated with the same key (header or `exaApiKey` query param for Websets MCP).
ExaVault’s public developer surface is the self-hosted on-prem appliance protocols (FTP, FTPS, SFTP, WebDAV); no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the available docs.
ExchangeRate-API exposes two HTTP API surfaces: an authenticated v6 API using an API key via path or Bearer header, and a public open-access API with no credential.

surfaces.name: ExchangeRate-API Standard / Pair / Enriched / Historical / Codes / Quota

domains.summary: Executor exposes a public HTTP API at https://executor.sh/api and an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://executor.sh/mcp; OAuth client registration is via signin.executor.sh.
Ex Libris exposes authenticated Alma and Primo REST APIs plus public SFX web services; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Expedia Group exposes Rapid HTTP APIs (lodging, cars, and related explorer/docs) secured by a partner API key plus shared secret signed in the `Authorization` header; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs I could access.

Experian exposes the ConsumerView HTTP API at https://neartime.experian.co.uk, authenticated with Neartime Service account login to mint a short-lived bearer token; no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.

Experteer exposes no documented public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface on the public site; only the consumer/recruiter website is visible and its programmatic integration surface remains unknown.
Explorium exposes one documented HTTP API (API key in `api_key` header) and one MCP server (`https://mcp.explorium.ai/mcp` / `/sse`) using the same key via headers.

ExtendsClass exposes two programmatic HTTP surfaces: the browser-based REST client and a mock REST API; the REST client is tied to a site account, and Mock API uses a generated API key for collection/mock management.

facebook.com exposes the Meta Graph API (HTTP) and the Meta Developer Tools MCP server; both require Meta account/app-based auth, with Graph API access tokens (optionally appsecret_proof) and MCP OAuth sign-in.
FaceCheck exposes a credentialed REST API at https://facecheck.id plus a public x402 reverse-face-search endpoint; the REST API uses a FaceCheck API token in the Authorization header.
Factagora exposes one authenticated HTTP API (OpenAPI at docs.factagora.com/openapi.yaml) and one authenticated MCP server using the same API key via FACTAGORA_ACCESS_TOKEN.

Factiva exposes Dow Jones Developer Platform HTTP APIs for Factiva Integration and Factiva Session API; authentication is required via Dow Jones-issued developer platform credentials, but no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Factorial has one public HTTP API surface at https://api.factorialhr.com, authenticated by either an admin-created `x-api-key` or an OAuth 2 `Authorization: Bearer` token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

FactSet exposes two documented HTTP APIs (Formula API, Symbology API) using Basic auth with FactSet username-serial + API key, plus an MCP server at mcp.factset.com/content/v1 with auth not fully determined from public docs.

Faire exposes one documented developer surface: the Faire External API at https://www.faire.com/external-api/v2, authenticated with OAuth access token plus app credentials in headers; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
fal.ai offers authenticated Model APIs, Platform APIs, an MCP server, and the fal CLI; primary credentials are API keys plus optional CLI browser login.
fanart.tv exposes one authenticated HTTP API (webservice.fanart.tv) with project and personal API keys via query params or headers; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI found.

FareHarbor exposes one documented external HTTP API with a downloadable OpenAPI spec and documented webhooks; the API requires a FareHarbor-issued API token, while webhook auth could not be confirmed from the pages read.
FastMCP Cloud/Horizon is the main authenticated web surface, and FastMCP also exposes a public docs MCP server at https://gofastmcp.com/mcp.
Fathom exposes one REST API (OpenAPI-backed) with API key or OAuth auth, plus one authenticated MCP server at https://api.fathom.ai/mcp.
Fathom exposes one public REST API with API-key auth and one MCP server at https://api.fathom.ai/mcp using OAuth2.

FCM exposes one public developer surface: the Firebase Cloud Messaging HTTP REST API at fcm.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth2/service account access tokens using the firebase.messaging scope.
FCM Data API exposes one HTTP REST surface at https://fcmdata.googleapis.com/ requiring either Google OAuth 2.0 with cloud-platform scope or a Google API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Featurebase.app exposed no discoverable public developer surface from web search/sitemap; HTTP surface presence is unconfirmed due to 429s, so auth remains unknown.

FEC exposes one public developer surface: the OpenFEC REST API at https://api.open.fec.gov/v1, documented at /developers/ and backed by an OpenAPI swagger page, authenticated with an FEC API key passed as `api_key` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
FeedClaw exposes a REST API plus OAuth authorization/token/registration endpoints and an MCP integration path; auth is OAuth-based for agents, with an API key mentioned on the homepage.
Fellow exposes one authenticated REST Developer API (plus privileged Super Admin access) and one MCP server at https://fellow.app/mcp; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced.

Fellow exposes one authenticated REST API (API key via X-API-KEY) and one authenticated MCP server at https://fellow.app/mcp using OAuth-based MCP auth; webhook delivery is part of the REST API, not a separate surface.
Ferryhopper exposes a public MCP server and a B2B FerryhAPI HTTP surface; the docs confirm endpoints and partnership entrypoints but do not specify developer-minted authentication details.

Fever exposes one public integration surface for developers: an MCP server at https://data-search.apigw.feverup.com/mcp/ authenticated via browser-based OAuth 2.0; no REST, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Public MCP server at https://fgdsp698b0.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/mcp with no authentication; no other public developer surfaces found.
Read the Docs exposes one developer surface: a public REST API at docs.readthedocs.com secured by a personal Bearer API token.

surfaces.name: Read the Docs Public REST API

domains.summary: FieldView exposes authenticated HTTP APIs only: current platform APIs, previous versions, and next versions; auth is OAuth2 bearer access tokens minted with a FieldView-provisioned OAuth client ID/secret.

Figshare exposes one public developer surface: the Figshare API v2.0 HTTP/REST API at https://api.figshare.com/v2, authenticated by either a Figshare OAuth 2.0 access token or a personal token via `Authorization: token ...` or `access_token` query param; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

file.googleapis.com exposes a public MCP server at https://file.googleapis.com/mcp and the Google Developer Knowledge HTTP API at https://developerknowledge.googleapis.com, which requires a Google Cloud API key.

Files.com exposes a REST API, a hosted and local MCP server, and a CLI app; all require authentication via API key or session/browser sign-in, with API keys also usable for the REST API and MCP header-based auth.

Filestack exposes two documented HTTP APIs (Management API and File API) plus a Claude Code MCP plugin; auth uses an app API key and secret for file/security flows, and a short-lived JWT for Management API.
Filevine exposes one public developer surface: the Filevine API (HTTP/REST) with PAT bearer auth or OAuth client+PAT token exchange; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
FMP exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server, both using the user’s FMP API key via the `apikey` query parameter.
Finicity/Mastercard Open Finance US exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface using `Finicity-App-Token` plus `Finicity-App-Key`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Fintual exposes one documented REST API (API V1 at /api, OpenAPI spec on /api-docs/v1/swagger.json) authenticated with a user auth token, plus a public website/docs surface; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Fintual exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated HTTP REST API V1 with OpenAPI spec at /api-docs/v1/swagger.json, using a user access token minted via POST /access_tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.

Fire offers a single documented public developer surface: the Fire Payments Business HTTP API at https://business.fire.com, authenticated with short-lived bearer access tokens minted from Fire API application credentials; plus a separate TPP open-banking HTTP API at https://dia-api.fire.com using dynamic client registration and `tls_client_auth`.
Firebase exposes the Firebase Authentication REST API on identitytoolkit.googleapis.com, authenticated with a Firebase project API key, and the client-side Authentication docs are public.

Firebase App Check exposes one public developer surface: the Firebase App Check REST API at firebaseappcheck.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google/Firebase project access; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

Firebase exposes the Realtime Database REST API and a Firebase AI tools/skills/MCP entry point; the REST API requires a Firebase user OAuth token, while the MCP surface’s auth could not be determined from the docs reviewed.

surfaces.name: Firebase AI tools, skills, and MCP

domains.summary: Firebase Dynamic Links exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://firebasedynamiclinks.googleapis.com/v1; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.

Firebase Hosting exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://firebasehosting.googleapis.com/v1beta1, using Google OAuth access tokens with Firebase or Cloud Platform scopes; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Firebase ML exposes one HTTP integration surface at https://firebaseml.googleapis.com, authenticated via Google account/OAuth; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Firebase Rules API at https://firebaserules.googleapis.com/ is the only public developer surface found; it accepts either a Google API key or a Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer token.

firebasestorage.googleapis.com exposes one developer surface: the Cloud Storage for Firebase JSON API, plus Google Cloud CLIs `gcloud storage` and `gsutil`; all use Google OAuth/ADC-style bearer auth.

surfaces.name: Cloud Storage for Firebase JSON API

domains.summary: FireBrowse exposes a public REST API at http://firebrowse.org with Samples, Analyses, Archives, and Metadata resources; no authentication is required and no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.
Firecrawl exposes a Bearer-authenticated REST API, a CLI (`firecrawl`), and an MCP server; auth is via API key or WorkOS ID-JAG-minted API key, with a keyless fallback mentioned in docs.
FireHydrant exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Bearer API key) and an authenticated MCP server that runs via the firehydrant-mcp package using the same API key.

firestore.googleapis.com exposes a Cloud Firestore REST API over `https://firestore.googleapis.com` requiring Google OAuth/ADC Bearer auth, plus a public MCP server at `https://firestore.googleapis.com/mcp`.
Fireworks AI exposes a Bearer-token REST API at https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1 and the `firectl` CLI, both authenticated with a Fireworks API key.

FIRST FRC Events exposes a public docs portal and a token-protected HTTP API at https://frc-api.firstinspires.org/; credentials are a registered username/token pair acquired via the API registration page.
Fiscal.ai offers one authenticated HTTP API and one MCP server; both use the Fiscal.ai API key, with MCP also supporting OAuth.

Google Fit exposes one developer-facing surface: the deprecated Google Fit REST API at https://www.googleapis.com/fitness/v1, authenticated only with OAuth 2.0 access tokens from a Google OAuth client; new sign-ups are closed.
Fivetran exposes one documented REST API (Basic auth via scoped API key) plus public connector metadata endpoints, and an MCP server using OAuth with `mcp` scope.
Flare exposes a public MCP docs server, a beta CLI, and a bearer-authenticated HTTP API using API keys to mint short-lived API tokens.
Flagsmith exposes a public Flags API, private Admin API, an MCP server, and a CLI; auth uses an Organisation API Token for admin/MCP and an Environment Key for the public flags API/CLI.
Flat exposes a REST API and a beta GraphQL API, both authenticated with Bearer tokens via personal access tokens or OAuth2; no MCP server or CLI surfaced.
Flexibits exposes one developer surface: a Zapier-facing HTTP integration authenticated with a Flexibits API key generated in the Hub Integrations page.

Flexport exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated Flexport API at https://api.flexport.com (docs at apidocs.flexport.com), using either a bearer API key or OAuth2 client-credentials token; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Flickr exposes two developer HTTP surfaces: the REST API and the photo Upload API; both require a Flickr API key plus OAuth access token, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surfaces were found.
Flix appears to expose a REST API via docs.flix-dev.com / developer.api.flixbus.com; no other public integration surfaces were confirmed.
flowerdiary.xyz exposes a public marketing/site surface only; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found, and the site indicates consumer login via Google or Apple accounts.
Fly.io exposes the Machines REST API, a public GraphQL API, and an MCP server/proxy surface; auth is via Fly API bearer tokens plus optional MCP basic or bearer credentials for remote proxying.
Forgejo exposes a REST API requiring personal access tokens, an OAuth2 provider for third-party app auth, and a Forgejo CLI; no MCP or GraphQL surface was confirmed from the docs reviewed.

DocSpring (formapi.io) exposes one public developer surface: the HTTP API at https://api.docspring.com, authenticated with HTTP Basic using a minted API token pair (`token_id` + `token_secret`); machine-readable spec is published at https://docspring.com/api-docs/v1/openapi.json.
Formbyte.ai exposes a public MCP server at https://formbyte.ai/mcp and OAuth 2.0/OIDC client registration via /api/oidc/oauth/register for authenticating to its OAuth-protected integration surface.
Google Forms exposes one developer surface: the Google Forms API (HTTP/REST) at forms.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0.
Google Forms exposes one developer surface: the Forms REST API at https://forms.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens in the Authorization: Bearer header.

Formstack has one confirmed developer surface: the authenticated REST API v2025.0, usable with either a Formstack API application or a personal access token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Formula Genius exposes one public integration surface: an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://formulagenius.co/mcp, with login/consent via its OAuth authorize flow.

FOSSA exposes one developer-facing surface: a REST API on app.fossa.com authenticated with a user-minted API token sent as `X-Api-Key`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed beyond the CLI using the same token.
Fotocasa exposes a public MCP server at https://fotocasa.es/mcp and no other developer surfaces were confirmed.
Framagit exposes authenticated REST and GraphQL developer APIs; OAuth app or personal access token can be used, with no MCP/CLI found.
Frankfurter exposes public HTTP APIs v1/v2 and a public MCP server; no authentication is required.
FrankieOne exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface; I found no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface on frankiefinancial.com.
FrankieOne exposes two authenticated HTTP API surfaces (v1 and v2) using a customer API key plus CustomerID, with optional ChildID in headers; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs I found.

FraudLabs Pro exposes three authenticated HTTP API surfaces (Order, User, SMS Verification), each using the global API license key; Agent JavaScript and Webhook docs exist but their auth mechanics were not fully determined.
freediver.club exposes one public developer surface: an MCP integration endpoint at https://integrations.freediver.club/mcp, authenticated via the user’s freediver.club account OAuth flow.

freee.co.jp exposes five public integration surfaces: Accounting, Human Resources, Invoice, Sales Management HTTP APIs (OAuth Bearer access token), plus HR webhook delivery verification via `x-freee-token`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

Freesound exposes one developer surface: the Freesound API v2 HTTP API at https://freesound.org/apiv2, authenticated by either API key query/header token auth or OAuth2 authorization-code credentials issued via /apiv2/apply.
freetv-app.com exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.freetv-app.com with OpenAPI at https://freetv-app.com/openapi.json; no authentication is required.
FreshBooks exposes an OAuth2-protected HTTP API at https://api.freshbooks.com plus an OAuth token endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.

Freshdesk exposes a public Core REST API, a Web Chat API, and an EAP MCP server; REST and MCP both use a Freshdesk API key, while Web Chat JWT auth is documented but the API auth surface remains unclear.
Freshpaint exposes one documented developer surface: a server-side HTTP API at https://api.perfalytics.com/track authenticated by the account environment ID (`token`) in the request body.
Front exposes one Core HTTP API and one beta MCP server; Core API supports either API tokens or OAuth, and the MCP server uses OAuth.

Frontegg exposes four public HTTP integration surfaces for developers: environment auth, identity/user management, account management, applications, and SCIM provisioning; auth is via clientId+API key for minting an environment token, or Bearer JWTs (environment token or user JWT) on the other APIs.

surfaces.name: Frontegg Identity and User Management API

domains.summary: Fugle exposes authenticated Market Data REST and WebSocket APIs using an API key, plus an OAuth 2.0 PKCE authorization server for client-based access.
Fulfilling Productivity exposes one public integration surface: the Priority Guardian MCP connector for Claude, authenticated via Claude connector OAuth; no HTTP API, GraphQL API, or CLI was found.
Fulfillment.com exposes one public developer surface: the OAuth2-protected Fulfillment.com APIv2 REST API (docs at fulfillment.github.io/api/, no separate MCP/CLI/GraphQL found).
Mapped one authenticated HTTP API surface for fulfillment.github.io: Fulfillment.com APIv2, using OAuth2 Bearer access tokens.

Fullstory exposes authenticated Server API v1/v2 over https://api.fullstory.com using a Basic API key, a beta MCP server authenticated via OAuth or API key, and public browser/mobile/guides browser APIs; Anywhere: Activation is documented but auth wasn’t fully determined.

surfaces.name: Fullstory Guides and Surveys Browser API

domains.summary: Function Health exposes one public integration surface: an MCP server for Claude at https://services.functionhealth.com/ai-chat/mcp, authenticated via OAuth 2.0 connector authorization with scopes read:health_summary, read:biomarkers, and read:action_plan.

FunGenerators exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.fungenerators.com/ and one authenticated MCP server at https://fungenerators.com/mcp, both using the same API key via Authorization: Bearer <api_key> (or api_key query param).
Fun Translations exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server, both using a Bearer API key; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced from the docs reviewed.

Furkot exposes one developer HTTP API at https://trips.furkot.com/pub/api, protected by OAuth2 access tokens via Authorization: Bearer or access_token query param; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
FusionAuth exposes one documented REST API and one MCP server; both require a scoped FusionAuth API key.

Fyxer exposes a single public integration surface found in the docs: the authenticated Fyxer App at https://app.fyxer.com, with Gmail or Outlook account connection as the only documented auth paths; no public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was found.
G2 exposes one public REST API (G2 API v2) and one MCP server; both use OAuth-based access, with API tokens also supported for the REST API.
Google Play Games Services exposes one developer surface: the authenticated Web REST API at `https://games.googleapis.com/`, using Google OAuth access tokens/scopes.

surfaces.name: Google Play Games Services Web REST API

domains.summary: gamesconfiguration.googleapis.com exposes the Google Play Game Services Publishing API over HTTP/REST and requires Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens; no other public surfaces were confirmed.

surfaces.name: Google Play Game Services Publishing API

domains.summary: Google Play Game Services Management API is the only public integration surface found for gamesmanagement.googleapis.com; it is an HTTP API at https://gamesmanagement.googleapis.com and requires Google OAuth 2.0 user authorization from a Play Console developer account.

surfaces.name: Google Play Game Services Management API

domains.summary: gamesparks.com exposes no confirmed public developer integration surface; only the website redirects to Amazon GameLift Servers marketing pages, with no verified API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI or authentication details.
Garchi exposes one authenticated REST API v2 and one authenticated MCP server; REST uses a Bearer API token, and MCP uses OAuth 2.0 with `mcp:use`.

gathrd.uk exposes one developer integration surface: an OAuth 2.1-protected MCP server at https://gathrd.uk/mcp, with OAuth client registration at https://gathrd.uk/oauth/register; no public HTTP or GraphQL API docs were found.
Gel exposes a public GraphQL surface and the `gel` CLI, with CLI auth via `gel cloud login` or a Gel Cloud secret key.
Geneea exposes two documented REST APIs: General API and Media API, both requiring a Geneea-issued API key; RAG Service API is documented but auth mechanics remain unknown.

Google Cloud exposes the Genomics/Life Sciences HTTP APIs on `genomics.googleapis.com` (Life Sciences successor decommissioned), authenticated via Google Cloud OAuth/ADC (`gcloud auth login` or ADC); no GraphQL, MCP, or standalone CLI surface was confirmed.

Genspark exposes an authenticated SB-Git HTTP/git surface and the gsk CLI via the same `gsk` API key; AI Sheets documents MCP-based data-source connections but the public connect endpoint/auth mechanics are not pinned down.
Geoapify exposes a single public HTTP API surface at https://api.geoapify.com/v1, authenticated with an API key passed as the `apiKey` query parameter.
GeoDataSource exposes two authenticated HTTP web services: Location Search and Neighboring Countries, both using the same API key passed as `key` in the query string.

GeoNames exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface (web services via `api.geonames.org`, using a GeoNames `username` query parameter) and one public HTTP download surface for data dumps on `download.geonames.org`.

GererMesAffaires exposes one public developer HTTP API surface at https://api.gerermesaffaires.com, with documented REST API, GMA Connect, Webhook, and UI API pages; authentication appears to rely on member account login/password, while no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
getaura.ai exposes one public integration surface: a hosted Aura MCP server; no public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI developer API was found.

Composer exposes a public CLI (`composer`) and an HTTP repository protocol at `https://repo.packagist.org`; repository auth is configured via Composer auth files/`COMPOSER_AUTH`, while the CLI itself is public.
Drafts exposes a local Mac MCP server and a `drafts` CLI, both unauthenticated; no public HTTP or GraphQL developer API was found for getdrafts.com.

surfaces.name: Drafts MCP Server
surfaces.url: local on your Mac via Claude Desktop extension

domains.summary: Guru exposes a Basic-auth REST API, an MCP server at https://mcp.api.getguru.com/mcp (OAuth or API token), and a CLI; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Lago exposes one documented REST API at https://api.getlago.com authenticated with a bearer API key, plus an AI MCP server mentioned in docs but without a confirmed connect endpoint or auth mechanics.
Maxim AI exposes a public HTTP Alerts API at https://api.getmaxim.ai (auth via `x-maxim-api-key`) and a `maxim` CLI that uses `MAXIM_API_KEY`; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed.
Miso exposes one public developer surface: the Miso REST API at https://api.askmiso.com/v1, authenticated with a per-environment API key via `api_key` query param or `X-API-KEY` header.
Perseuss exposes one public MCP surface at https://www.getperseuss.com/_api/mcp with no developer credential required.

Postman exposes a web/API platform plus GraphQL request support, an MCP server, and the Postman CLI; auth is centered on a Postman account, while exact surface bindings for GraphQL/MCP/CLI remain undocumented here.
Stream exposes four authenticated HTTP developer surfaces: Chat Messaging, Video & Audio, Activity Feeds, and AI Moderation; credentials are API Key, API Secret, and JWT user token.
GetTheData exposes two public, unauthenticated HTTP APIs: Open Postcode Geo and bng2latlong; no GraphQL, MCP, CLI, or service-issued credentials were found.

GetYourGuide exposes two authenticated HTTP surfaces: a supplier-facing API at https://{host}/1 with OpenAPI spec https://integrator.getyourguide.com/assets/api_documentation/supplier-api-supplier-endpoints.yaml, and a GetYourGuide-hosted API at https://supplier-api.getyourguide.com/1 with OpenAPI spec https://integrator.getyourguide.com/assets/api_documentation/supplier-api-gyg-endpoints.yaml; both use Basic Auth.
Ghost exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Admin API and Content API) plus the public Ghost CLI; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed.
GIPHY has one public integration surface: the GIPHY HTTP API at https://api.giphy.com/v1, using a GIPHY API key via the `api_key` query parameter; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Gisgraphy exposes a public REST web-services surface at https://services.gisgraphy.com; no separate public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI developer surface was identified from the docs.

gist.githubusercontent.com exposes GitHub’s Gists REST API, GitHub GraphQL API, GitHub MCP server, and GitHub CLI; auth is via GitHub PAT or `gh auth login` for API/CLI, with MCP auth unclear from the retrieved docs.
GitBook exposes one authenticated REST API, a CLI authenticated with a developer token, and MCP endpoints for published docs (public or auth-dependent).
Gitea exposes an authenticated HTTP API and a Gitea MCP server; the API accepts token/basic auth plus SSH-key HTTP signatures, while MCP auth could not be determined from the public page.

Gitea exposes one public developer HTTP API, one MCP server, and the Tea CLI; all require a Gitea account-derived access token, with additional API access via BasicAuth for token minting and SSH-signature support on the API.
github.ae exposes the GitHub GraphQL API at https://api.github.com/graphql; it requires a Bearer token via personal access token, GitHub App installation token, or OAuth access token.
GitHub exposes REST and GraphQL APIs plus the `gh` CLI; auth is via PAT/OAuth/GitHub App tokens for APIs, MCP support is documented but endpoint auth couldn't be pinned.

GitHub GraphQL API is available at https://api.github.com/graphql with public schema download and supports bearer auth via personal access tokens, GitHub App installation tokens, or OAuth access tokens.

GitLab exposes REST, GraphQL, glab CLI, and an MCP server; all require auth except selected REST endpoints not modeled individually, with OAuth, PAT/project/group tokens, and CI job tokens supported depending on surface.
GitMCP exposes public MCP endpoints for repository-specific docs and a dynamic /docs endpoint; private-repo access uses a GitHub personal access token via `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
Gitpod.io (Ona) exposes an authenticated HTTP API, CLI, SDK, and an MCP-capable platform; access is via Ona personal access tokens.
Gitter exposes one developer surface: the Matrix Client-Server HTTP API at https://gitter.ems.host, authenticated with a Matrix access token in the Authorization: Bearer header.

surfaces.name: Matrix Client-Server API for Gitter homeserver

domains.summary: Backup for GKE exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://gkebackup.googleapis.com/ using Google Cloud OAuth Bearer tokens.
GKE Hub API exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST surface at https://gkehub.googleapis.com using Google OAuth/ADC Bearer auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Glama exposes one documented developer API surface, the OpenAI-compatible Glama Gateway API with PKCE-minted Bearer API key auth, plus an MCP Gateway endpoint whose public auth mechanics are not fully specified.
Glean exposes a required-auth Client REST API (OAuth or Glean-issued bearer token) and a public docs MCP server at https://developers.glean.com/mcp; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced from the docs reviewed.
Glinded exposes one developer surface: an OAuth-protected MCP server at https://mcp-detprm6tzq-uc.a.run.app; no public REST, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Gmail exposes one public developer API surface, the Gmail API v1 over HTTP, plus a Gmail MCP server; both use Google OAuth-based access, with the HTTP API authenticated by Bearer access tokens.
Gmail exposes one HTTP API surface and one documented MCP server; both are Google OAuth-based, with no CLI or GraphQL surface confirmed.
gmailpostmastertools.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API, Gmail Postmaster Tools API, using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens in the Authorization header.
Go-UPC exposes one authenticated HTTP product lookup API (https://go-upc.com/api/v1) using a single issued API key via either `Authorization: Bearer` or `?key=`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
authentik exposes one OpenAPI-based HTTP API plus two authenticated WebSocket surfaces; auth works with API tokens, OAuth access tokens scoped `goauthentik.io/api`, or session cookies.
goawrd.gov.in exposes a public government website with online forms that require a Goa WRD website account; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
GoCardless exposes one documented HTTP API plus a CLI and MCP tooling; API auth is token-based, with an additional signing credential for sending-money flows.

GoDaddy exposes one authenticated HTTP API (REST/OpenAPI docs with multiple swagger specs) and one public MCP server; both use GoDaddy-issued API key/secret for the API, while MCP is anonymous/public.
Gogs exposes one authenticated REST API at `/api/v1` using personal access tokens plus two public MCP endpoints, with no separate GraphQL or CLI integration surface confirmed.
GoHighLevel exposes a required-auth Marketplace HTTP API and an MCP server; both use Private Integration Tokens, with the MCP server also taking a `locationId` header.
Jinko exposes one authenticated REST API, one remote MCP endpoint, and one CLI; auth is via `jnk_` API keys or OAuth, with separate sandbox and production environments.
Google Developer Knowledge exposes one HTTP REST API and one MCP server; both accept an API key, and the MCP server also supports Google user OAuth.
goose-docs.ai exposes a public goose CLI and public built-in MCP extension docs, plus authenticated provider connections for model backends via API keys or OAuth/device-flow/browser login.

Gorgias exposes one documented REST API and one MCP server; the REST API uses HTTP Basic with account email + API key, and the MCP server uses browser-based OAuth/session auth at https://mcp.gorgias.com/mcp.
GoSure.ai exposes only a public marketing website; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, CLI, or credentials could be confirmed.
Upword exposes a public MCP server at https://goupword.com/mcp, with OAuth client credentials available via dynamic registration for `read`-scoped access.

gov.bc.ca exposes the Service BC Connect / BC Registries HTTP API family: Business Registry, Pay, Registry Search, Personal Property Registry, and SBC Connect; each uses a BC Registries API key plus Account ID in headers.

surfaces.name: BC Registries Personal Property Registry API

domains.summary: govcon.dev exposes the Tango HTTP API (OpenAPI at https://tango.makegov.com/api/schema/) and an OAuth-protected MCP server at https://govcon.dev/mcp; auth supports API keys for HTTP and OAuth client registration for both HTTP OAuth2 and MCP.

GovTribe’s public integration surface is MCP-only: a standard server, compact/server variants, and grouped MCP endpoints, all authenticated with a GovTribe-issued MCP API key via `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no REST/GraphQL/CLI surface was confirmed.
