domains.summary: Grafana has two public HTTP integration surfaces: the Grafana.com API (Bearer auth) and the Grafana HTTP API (Basic auth or service account tokens; recorded here with service account token).

Grain exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.grain.com, authenticated via Bearer OAuth access tokens, personal access tokens, or workspace access tokens, and it requires the `Public-Api-Version: 2025-10-31` header.
Notion exposes a REST API, hosted MCP server, and `ntn` CLI; all require auth via Notion bearer tokens or CLI login, and no GraphQL surface was found.

Grammarly exposes five authenticated HTTP REST APIs: Analytics, License Management, Writing Score, AI Detection, and Plagiarism Detection; all use OAuth 2.0 bearer access tokens minted from the Grammarly admin panel.
Granted AI exposes one authenticated REST API at https://grantedai.com/api/v1 using Bearer API keys, plus a public MCP server at https://grantedai.com/api/mcp/mcp with no auth required.
Graphene-Python exposes only public documentation for a Python GraphQL library; no hosted API, MCP server, or CLI integration surface was found.
GraphHopper exposes one public integration surface: the GraphHopper Directions HTTP API at https://graphhopper.com/api/1, authenticated by an API key passed as the `key` query parameter.
Graphite exposes a GitHub App–backed web product surface, a `gt` CLI, and GT MCP via the CLI; authentication is through the Graphite GitHub App for the product and a CLI auth token for `gt`/MCP.
Shopify exposes two GraphQL APIs (Admin and Storefront) plus a public UCP MCP endpoint; Admin requires a Shopify access token, Storefront accepts an access token or tokenless public access.
graphql.org exposes a public GraphQL informational/docs surface only; no developer-mintable credentials, APIs, MCP servers, or CLIs were found.
Gravatar exposes one developer surface: the Gravatar Public REST API at https://api.gravatar.com/v3, secured by a Gravatar-issued Bearer API key.

Gravitee exposes APIM Management API docs, an APIM MCP server, and Access Management docs; APIM MCP uses a service-account personal token, while the HTTP APIs’ exact auth bindings remain unknown from the pages read.
Greenhouse exposes four developer surfaces: Harvest REST API, Job Board API, Assessment API, Candidate Ingestion API, plus the Onboarding GraphQL API; no CLI or MCP surface was found.
Greenpeace exposes private ArcGIS REST and Portal Sharing HTTP APIs on maps.greenpeace.org, both secured by a portal token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were identified.

Gremlin exposes one public HTTP REST API at https://api.gremlin.com/v1 and an MCP server for Reliability Intelligence; both require auth, and no public GraphQL or standalone CLI integration surface was confirmed.
grep.app’s public developer surface is an unauthenticated MCP server; no public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI integration surface was confirmed from the available docs.
Greptile exposes a bearer-authenticated MCP server at https://api.greptile.com/mcp and a `greptile` CLI (`greptile login`); no public HTTP/GraphQL API spec was surfaced in the docs I reviewed.
Groq exposes one public developer HTTP API at https://api.groq.com/openai/v1 authenticated with a Groq API key, plus MCP-based remote tools docs with auth unknown.
groundcover exposes a private MCP server, authenticated remote API surface, ingestion endpoints, and a public llms.txt; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Groups Migration API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at groupsmigration.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Groups Settings API: one HTTP surface at groupssettings.googleapis.com, authenticated by Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens; docs also mention API keys for requests.
GrowthBook exposes a public SDK connection endpoint, plus authenticated REST API, CLI, and MCP server integrations.

GSA exposes multiple public HTTP APIs on api.gsa.gov and api.sam.gov, all authenticated with a user-minted api.data.gov/SAM.gov API key (mostly via `x-api-key`, with some endpoints also supporting `api_key` query parameter); no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
GSMtasks exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.gsmtasks.com using an API key in the `Authorization: Token ...` header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
GS Plant Foods exposes a public UCP discovery HTTP endpoint, a public UCP MCP endpoint, and public storefront read endpoints; no developer-issued credentials were found.

surfaces.name: GS Plant Foods Storefront Read API

domains.summary: Guardrails AI exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API and a Python CLI; the REST API uses a Guardrails API key, and the CLI stores the same token via `guardrails configure`.
Gumloop exposes one HTTP API, one remote MCP server, and one CLI; API/MCP use a Gumloop API key or OAuth-derived bearer token, and the CLI uses `gumloop login`.
Gumtree Australia exposes only a public classifieds website surface; I found no documented developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface.
Gusto exposes two authenticated HTTP API surfaces: Embedded Payroll API and App Integrations API; both use Bearer tokens, with additional MCP/CLI surfaces not confirmed from the docs I reviewed.
Habitify exposes one REST API (OpenAPI v2 at api.habitify.me/v2, API key auth) and one MCP server at mcp.habitify.me/mcp using OAuth via dynamic client registration; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced.
Halo API exposes one public developer surface: the Halo REST API over HTTP, secured by OAuth2 bearer access tokens from a registered Halo API client application.
Hanko exposes public HTTP APIs (Public API, Flow API, JWKS) plus a protected Admin API; auth is via Hanko API key (Bearer header) for Admin, while Public/Flow/JWKS are public or session-JWT based.

Happenstance offers one HTTP API, one OAuth-protected MCP server, and one CLI; all developer surfaces can be used with a Happenstance API key, and the CLI also supports `HPN_API_KEY`/`hpn config set --api-key`.
Harmonic exposes a required-auth REST API, GraphQL API, and MCP server; all accept either an API key in the `apikey` header or OAuth user auth, with MCP also supporting streamable HTTP and SSE.
Harness exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and the `hc` CLI; auth is via Harness personal access tokens for API/CLI and SSO-backed MCP access.
harvard.edu exposes ToolUniverse integration surfaces for HTTP, MCP/remote tools, and CLI, but the public pages available here didn’t reveal concrete auth mechanics or machine-readable specs.
Harvey exposes one documented HTTP API (Bearer API token; regional base URLs for EU/AU) and one MCP server requiring OAuth-enabled remote MCP access with Harvey account authentication.
Hashnode exposes one public developer surface: a GraphQL API at https://api.hashnode.com/ authenticated with a Hashnode developer auth token from developer settings.

Hasura exposes public version and health endpoints, plus authenticated GraphQL, Metadata, and Schema HTTP APIs, a Hasura Cloud GraphQL API using a Personal Access Token, a Hasura Pro CLI authenticated by the same PAT, and an MCP server for Hasura GraphQL that can use an admin secret or no secret depending on deployment.
Hatchet exposes a public docs MCP server and a CLI that authenticates with a Hatchet API token; no public REST/GraphQL API spec was confirmed from the pages read.
hauptsache.net exposes one authenticated MCP server at https://hauptsache.net/mcp using OAuth-based client registration/authorization; no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.
Headout exposes only public website pages plus authenticated partner and affiliate portals; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Cloud Healthcare API exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://healthcare.googleapis.com/ using Google Cloud OAuth/access tokens or service account credentials in the Authorization: Bearer header.
healthcare.gov exposes a public Content API and a separate CMS Marketplace REST API requiring an API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Healthchecks.io exposes two HTTP APIs (Management API v3 and deprecated v1) authenticated by project API key, plus the Pinging API using a project Ping key in the ping URL; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

HealthEx exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.healthex.io/v1 and one authenticated MCP server at https://api.healthex.io/mcp; both use Bearer tokens, with REST JWTs minted from an API key/secret and MCP patient tokens obtained via OAuth 2.0 + PKCE.
Healthie exposes one public integration surface: a GraphQL API requiring a Healthie API key, plus the public Healthie Dev Assist MCP project/docs for sandbox development.

Heap exposes client-side APIs publicly plus authenticated server-side APIs, including a bearer-token user deletion API and its Basic-auth auth token mint endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Heap user deletion auth token endpoint

domains.summary: Heepsy exposes a logged-in web app and a public help center; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI was found.
Helicone exposes an authenticated AI Gateway HTTP API, an authenticated GraphQL API, an authenticated Observability HTTP API, and an authenticated MCP server; model discovery is public.
Helium 10 exposes one documented public integration surface: an authenticated MCP server using Helium 10 account OAuth 2.0; no HTTP/GraphQL/CLI developer surface was confirmed.

Dropbox Sign exposes one public developer-facing API surface (HTTP REST at https://api.hellosign.com/v3) with API-key Basic auth or OAuth Bearer tokens, plus a public MCP docs-search server at https://developers.hellosign.com/_mcp/server.
Help Scout exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Docs API and Inbox API), plus public webhooks and Beacon JS; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Help Scout exposes one public developer surface on helpscout.net: the Docs REST API at https://docsapi.helpscout.net/v1, authenticated with a user-specific Docs API key via HTTP Basic auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Hemnet exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated BostadsAPI HTTP API with Bearer API keys, plus webhook delivery docs that do not count as a developer surface.

Hepsiemlak exposes no direct public developer API of its own in discovered docs; the only surfaced integration points are an Apify-hosted HTTP API, MCP endpoint, and CLI for a community Hepsiemlak scraper, all using an Apify API token.

HERE offers a public developer docs portal plus a required-auth HTTP integration surface for HERE Identity and Access Management APIs, supporting API key query auth and OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

surfaces.name: HERE Identity and Access Management APIs

domains.summary: Heroku exposes the Platform API, MCP server, and Heroku CLI; all require authenticated access via Heroku API key/OAuth token or CLI login.

Hetzner Cloud exposes the Cloud REST API, the hcloud CLI, plus separate Storage Boxes and Robot web service HTTP surfaces; Cloud API and CLI use a Hetzner Cloud API token, while the other two surfaces' auth could not be determined from the pages read.
Hex exposes a bearer-authenticated Public API, a CLI authenticated via `hex auth login` with a personal access token, and an MCP server; no public GraphQL surface was found.
HHS itself exposes no public developer API surface on hhs.gov; the discovered developer integration is HealthData.gov’s Socrata-based open data API using a HealthData.gov API key in `X-App-Token`.

HiBob’s public developer surface is the Bob Public API (HTTP/REST) and the HiBob MCP Server; both currently authenticate with a Bob service user (ID + token), while legacy API access tokens are deprecated.

Highnote exposes one authenticated GraphQL API at https://api.us.test.highnote.com/graphql using a base64-encoded API key in `Authorization: Basic ...`; docs also mention an interactive API Explorer and SDKs, but no separate public REST, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

National Highways exposes one public HTTP API surface: the unauthenticated WebTRIS Traffic Flow API at http://webtris.nationalhighways.co.uk/api/v1.0; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
Hjarni exposes a Bearer-token REST API at https://hjarni.com/api/v1 and an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://hjarni.com/mcp over streamable HTTP.

Home Graph exposes an authenticated HTTP API and RPC/GraphQL-style API for developers, plus a public MCP endpoint at https://homegraph.googleapis.com/mcp; authentication is via Google service-account credentials for the API surfaces.

Honeycomb exposes a documented HTTP API (OpenAPI at https://docs.honeycomb.io/api/openapi-public.yaml) authenticated by Honeycomb API keys, plus an MCP server at https://mcp.honeycomb.io/mcp that supports OAuth or API key auth.
Hookdeck’s public developer surface is REST API, Outpost API, CLI, and MCP; auth is via Hookdeck project API key, Outpost admin API key or tenant JWT, and CLI/MCP login or `HOOKDECK_API_KEY`.
Hoppscotch exposes a public REST testing endpoint, a GraphQL testing surface with unknown auth, and a CLI that authenticates with a personal access token.
Hot100.ai exposes a public website only; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was discoverable from the site or sitemap.
Hotjar exposes one authenticated JSON HTTP API at https://api.hotjar.io plus two public client-side JS surfaces: Events API and Identify API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs reviewed.
HouseCanary exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Analytics API and Order Manager API) using Basic auth with an API Key/Secret, plus an MCP server that reuses the same credentials via env vars.
houstonian.com exposes one public A2A agent card for a reservations assistant; no HTTP, GraphQL, CLI, or authenticated developer API surfaces were found.

hp.gov.in exposes no public integration surface itself; HP developer docs point to two OAuth2-protected REST APIs at daas.api.hp.com: HP Workforce Solutions Analytics API (OpenAPI spec available) and HP TechPulse Incident Integration API.
hp.nic.in exposes the HP Workforce Solutions Analytics REST API; I found no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces on the domain.

hpayushboard.org exposes one HTTP integration surface: the Board of Ayurvedic and Unani Systems of Medicine, Himachal Pradesh API, documented via APIs.guru and used for DigiLocker-based registration certificate access; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

domains.summary: hptechboard.com exposes HP Proactive Insights REST APIs: HP TechPulse Analytics API and HP TechPulse Incident Integration API, both OAuth2-protected, with the analytics API publishing an OpenAPI JSON spec.

HSBC offers an Open Banking HTTP API surface for Account Information (HK Personal) plus DCR onboarding docs; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed, and the portal/SDK pages are documentation/tools rather than public integration endpoints.

HSBC Open Banking Dynamic Client Registration (DCR)

domains.summary: HubSpot Forms exposes two HTTP submission surfaces: a public unauthenticated endpoint and an authenticated endpoint using HubSpot Bearer tokens (OAuth access token or private app access token).
hsl.fi exposes an HSL OpenMaaS HTTP ticket API requiring an HSL-issued API key, and a Digitransit GraphQL Routing API requiring a Digitransit API key; no MCP or CLI surface was confirmed.

HTTPie exposes a public CLI surface (`http`/`https`) with no service-issued developer credential; authentication is user-provided per request and the docs cover basic, digest, bearer, and session-based request auth.

HubSpot exposes one HTTP API at https://api.hubapi.com/, plus two MCP servers: remote HubSpot MCP at https://mcp.hubspot.com and a local Developer MCP configured via `hs mcp setup`; auth is OAuth for the API/remote MCP and CLI login for Developer MCP.

surfaces.name: HubSpot CRM / Developer Platform HTTP API

domains.summary: Hubhopper exposes one public developer-facing HTTP API Platform landing page; no public spec, MCP, GraphQL, or CLI was found, and auth mechanics remain unknown from public docs.

surfaces.name: Hubhopper Studio / podcast hosting web app

domains.summary: HubProsper exposes only a public website/product page; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Hygraph exposes a GraphQL Content API plus a hosted MCP server; both use PAT bearer auth, and the Content API can also be made public via project-configured API permissions.

Hyperbrowser exposes authenticated REST APIs for browser sessions, scraping, web search/fetch, HyperAgent, and web crawl; public X402-paid fetch/search endpoints; an authenticated MCP server; and the `hx` CLI, all primarily using the user-minted Hyperbrowser API key.

Hyperping exposes a Bearer-token REST API at https://api.hyperping.io/v1 (OpenAPI at https://hyperping.com/openapi.json) and an MCP server at https://api.hyperping.io/v1/mcp, both authenticated with a project-scoped API key.
iam.googleapis.com exposes the Google Cloud IAM REST API, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs.

domains.summary: iamcredentials.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API: the IAM Service Account Credentials API, using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens in the Authorization: Bearer header.
iap.googleapis.com exposes one developer surface: the Identity-Aware Proxy HTTP API, authenticated with a Google OAuth 2.0 access token.

IBANAPI exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.ibanapi.com/v1 with OpenAPI spec; it uses a single API key credential that can be passed as `api_key` query/form parameter or in the `Authorization` header.

IBM.com exposes IBM API Connect for GraphQL via a GraphQL endpoint and the `stepzen` CLI; both use IBM-issued admin key/API key credentials, with GraphQL API calls using `Authorization: apikey ...` and CLI login using `stepzen login <domain> -a <account>`.

domains.summary: ICE exposes a gated developer portal with authenticated mortgage servicing and fixed income/data catalogs, plus an ICE Data API page and an ICE Chat API request-access link with auth unclear.

domains.summary: Icons8 exposes one MCP server and multiple API surfaces: Search, Public Icons, Photos, Music, Illustrations, Upscaler, Background Remover, and Renderer; all HTTP APIs use the same Icons8 API key via `Api-Key` header or `token` query param.
Icons8 exposes one public MCP server at https://mcp.icons8.com/mcp/ with free PNG access unauthenticated and SVG access via an Icons8 API key (Bearer Authorization header).
Ideal Postcodes exposes one public HTTP API and the `idpc` CLI; both use an API key, and key-management CLI commands also require a separate user token.
Ideal Postcodes exposes one public REST API, a CLI (`idpc`), and an Excel add-in; no MCP or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

idealo.de exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated PWS 2.0 HTTP API at https://import.idealo.com using OAuth2 client-credentials with clientId/clientSecret from idealo Business; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
IdealSpot exposes one developer surface: the authenticated IdealSpot GeoData HTTP API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced on idealspot.gitlab.io.
iDenfy exposes one authenticated public HTTP API at https://ivs.idenfy.com using HTTP Basic Auth with an API key + secret, plus a public read-only MCP docs server for the documentation.
Identity Platform exposes one public developer surface: the REST API at identitytoolkit.googleapis.com, authenticated either with Google Cloud OAuth2 Bearer tokens or an API key.

IFTTT exposes two developer HTTP surfaces: the Service API for IFTTT-to-your-service integrations (OAuth2-based, docs only; spec URL not publicly machine-readable) and the Connect API at https://connect.ifttt.com, which supports unauthenticated public reads and authenticated backend calls via the `IFTTT-Service-Key` header.
IFTTT exposes one developer-facing Service API (OAuth2) and one Connect API (service-key authenticated), plus an MCP server at https://ifttt.com/mcp using OAuth2.

IllumiChat exposes one REST/OpenAPI API at https://app.illumichat.com/api, authenticated with workspace API keys (Bearer `wsk_live_...`), plus public widget endpoints; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Image-Charts exposes one public HTTP API surface (`/chart`), plus an enterprise signing variant using `icac` + `ichm`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

IMDb exposes one developer integration surface: a GraphQL API at the AWS Data Exchange endpoint, authenticated with AWS SigV4 plus an IMDb-provided `x-api-key`; no MCP, CLI, or public unauthenticated surfaces were found.
ImgBB exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated ImgBB Image Upload REST API at https://api.imgbb.com/1, using a single API key passed as a query parameter or form field.
Imgix exposes public Rendering and likely Video HTTP APIs, plus an account-authenticated Management HTTP API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs.
ImmoScout24 exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST developer API surface (Real Estate APIs) with client credentials and personal access token auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Impala exposes one developer-facing HTTP Booking API with a Stoplight-hosted OpenAPI spec; it requires an Impala API key sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
Import.io exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API authenticated with an API key passed as `_apikey` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
incident.io exposes a Bearer-authenticated HTTP API at https://api.incident.io/ plus a remote MCP server at https://app.incident.io/~/settings/mcp that supports OAuth or API-key auth.

Increase exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API at https://api.increase.com authenticated with OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens from an Increase OAuth application; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Google Indexing API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at https://indexing.googleapis.com/v3, using OAuth 2.0 access tokens (scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/indexing`) or a service account JSON key that mints such tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Infermedica exposes two documented HTTP APIs (Engine API with App-Id/App-Key; Platform API with OAuth2 Bearer) plus an MCP server endpoint at the provided well-known URL, with auth not determinable from the server card.
Infinite Loop exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: Car Registration API Ireland and Avatar API, both using account username/password credentials.

Infisical exposes a bearer-token REST Public API, the `infisical` CLI, and a public docs MCP server at `https://infisical.com/docs/mcp`; API/CLI auth uses machine-identity Universal Auth credentials, and OAuth applications are also supported for delegated access.

InfluxData exposes public HTTP APIs for InfluxDB OSS v1/v2 plus an InfluxDB 3 Enterprise MCP server and the v1 `influx` CLI; auth is token/Bearer for v2, Basic auth for v1, and MCP/CLI auth was not fully pinned from the docs read.
Infobip exposes one public REST/OpenAPI surface and remote MCP servers; both require auth via API key or, for MCP, OAuth 2.1 as well.
InfoseekAI exposes a public website plus an authenticated MCP connector directory and per-app MCP surfaces; public docs show no separate HTTP/GraphQL API or CLI.
Inngest exposes a bearer-authenticated REST API and a public local Dev Server MCP endpoint; no public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
INPE exposes one clear developer surface on inpe.br: a public STAC HTTP API at https://data.inpe.br/bdc/stac/v1 with Redocly/OpenAPI docs; no auth credential is required.
InPost provides REST HTTP APIs for account, location, shipping, tracking, returns, and one-time pickups, all using OAuth 2.1 Bearer tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
InPost exposes the ShipX HTTP API and Geowidget HTTP integration; both require InPost-issued credentials created in the Managera Paczek portal.
Insomnia exposes a public desktop app and Inso CLI, plus an MCP client; no public HTTP/GraphQL APIs were confirmed from the available docs.
Instacart exposes one authenticated HTTP Developer Platform API and one authenticated MCP server; no additional public GraphQL or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Instagram’s public developer surface is Meta’s Instagram Platform Graph API plus webhooks, with an unofficial MCP server wrapper; auth is via Meta app credentials and Graph API access tokens.
Instatus exposes a Bearer-token-authenticated REST API at https://api.instatus.com plus a public status-page JSON endpoint at /summary.json.
Intel.com exposes the Intel Endpoint Management Assistant REST API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from public docs.

Interactive Brokers exposes two developer HTTP surfaces: the Client Portal Web API (OAuth 1.0a consumer key + token) and the TWS API (socket-based, auth mechanics not fully pinned from the pages read).

domains.summary: Intercom exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated remote MCP server; both use Intercom-issued access/OAuth tokens, and no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Interzoid exposes two HTTP APIs (core data quality/enrichment and batch match) plus a hosted MCP server; all use the same Interzoid API license key, with core APIs documented via openapi.json and the MCP server at https://mcp.interzoid.com/mcp.

surfaces.name: Interzoid Data Quality and Enrichment APIs

domains.summary: Invideo exposes one public developer surface: a remote MCP server at https://mcp.invideo.io/sse with no authentication required.

Inviton.eu exposes no publicly verifiable developer-facing API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface from the site pages I could find; only a general event-management website with mentions of iframe/API/plugin integration, but no accessible public spec or endpoint docs.

IP2Location.io exposes 4 REST APIs, an MCP server, and a CLI; all HTTP APIs use the same API key via Authorization Bearer or `key` query param, the CLI configures that key, and the MCP server auth could not be fully determined from docs.
IP2Location.io exposes 4 HTTP APIs, plus an MCP server and CLI; IP2Location.com mainly points developers to the IP2Location.io API surface and embed/widget/integration pages.
IP2WHOIS exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI; all require the same API key, usable as a query `key`, `Authorization: Bearer`, or MCP/CLI config env/header binding.
IPAPAS exposes one public developer surface on ipapas.co.kr: an HTTP content API; authentication details were not determinable from the available docs.

ipgeolocation.io exposes 6 authenticated HTTP APIs (IP Geolocation, IP Security, ASN, Abuse Contact, Timezone, User-Agent, Astronomy), plus an MCP server and an IPGeolocation CLI; the documented API auth is a single API key passed as `apiKey` query parameter.
IPinfo exposes one public HTTP API, one MCP server, and one CLI; all three use the same user-minted IPinfo token.
IPInfoDB’s public developer integration surface could not be confirmed from accessible docs; only the main website was reachable, and no API/spec/CLI/MCP surface was discoverable.
IPQualityScore exposes one developer surface: an authenticated HTTP fraud-detection API using an IPQS API key.
iProov exposes one documented REST API surface with OpenAPI spec, plus OIDC Web product docs; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Ironclad exposes two HTTP integration surfaces: the Public API and the OAuth 2.0 API; both use OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens, with no CLI, MCP, or GraphQL surface confirmed.
isango.com exposes a public booking website and account portal; no public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was found, and partner API access appears to be manual via partners@isango.com.

Jobtome exposes a public website and a partner network page, but I കണ്ടെത്തed no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface from the available docs.

jokes.one exposes one REST API surface at https://api.jokes.one; public joke-of-the-day calls need no credential, while paid usage accepts the Jokes One API Secret via `X-JokesOne-Api-Secret` or `api_key` query parameter.
Jooble exposes one developer surface: a REST HTTP API at `https://jooble.org/api` authenticated by a Jooble-issued API key in the URL path.
Jooble exposes one developer integration surface: a REST API at https://jooble.org/api authenticated by a Jooble-issued API key passed in the request path.

Journy.io exposes one public developer HTTP API at https://api.journy.io, authenticated with a Journy API key.

jsDelivr exposes a public CDN and a public npm metadata API; no authenticated developer credentials were found, and the only CLI-related result was a public npm package page, not a jsDelivr-issued CLI.

Jumio exposes one public developer integration surface: REST APIs secured by OAuth2 bearer tokens, with transaction-specific bearer tokens for credential upload/finalization flows.
Jumpseller exposes a REST API, authenticated admin MCP server, public storefront MCP, and CLI; admin integrations use either store Login Key/Auth Token or OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens.

June.so exposes one public developer surface: a Basic-authenticated HTTP tracking API at `https://api.june.so/sdk` for identify, group, track, and page events; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Juniper Mist exposes a token-authenticated REST API, a token-authenticated WebSocket API, and a beta MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed from the available docs.

Just Eat Takeaway.com exposes two HTTP integration surfaces: the main API and JET Connect, both documented with OpenAPI specs and authenticated via API key or JWT; JET Connect also supports HMAC-signed callbacks/webhook requests.
just-eat.io exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the main Just Eat Takeaway.com API and the JET Connect API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.
JustFix exposes a public marketing website and an unauthenticated-looking customer booking portal; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the available docs.
jwt.io exposes a public web JWT debugger and public AI-agent CLI skills; no service-issued credentials, GraphQL, or MCP surface were found.

k6.io exposes an authenticated Grafana Cloud k6 REST API, the k6 CLI, and a separate experimental MCP server; REST/CLI use a Grafana Cloud API token plus stack ID, while MCP auth was not determinable from available docs.
Kaggle exposes a public HTTP API, an OAuth 2.0 provider API, an MCP server, and the kaggle CLI; auth is via Kaggle API token, OAuth client, or org-client Basic auth for the OAuth provider.

Kakao.com exposes one public developer integration surface: the Kakao Developers REST API at kapi.kakao.com, authenticated with either a Kakao app key (Authorization: KakaoAK ...) or a Kakao OAuth access token (Authorization: Bearer ...); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
kakao.golf exposes only the public website; the probed MCP server-card URL returned 404 and no developer API/GraphQL/CLI surface was found.

Kaleyra exposes one public developer integration surface I could confirm: the Kaleyra SMS HTTP API at api-alerts.kaleyra.com/v4/, authenticated with a Kaleyra API key passed as the `api_key` query parameter.
kansil.org exposes a public CROssBAR web service at crossbar.kansil.org; no developer auth credentials or machine-readable API spec were confirmed.
Kapeli exposes Dash documentation integration via a public `dash://` custom URL scheme and mentions MCP support, with no developer-authenticated HTTP API or CLI found.

KBB exposes a public developer portal and an Advertising Data API surface; access appears gated behind portal-requested credentials, but public docs were rate-limited so auth mechanics could not be confirmed.
Google Keep exposes one developer surface: the authenticated Google Keep REST API at keep.googleapis.com/v1, accessed with Google OAuth credentials.
kennethreitz.org exposes one HTTP API surface with OpenAPI schema at https://kennethreitz.org/api/schema/; auth could not be determined.
Kensho offers the LLM-ready API as an authenticated HTTP API, an MCP server, and a Python CLI/library, with Okta/browser login, refresh token, and production RSA keypair auth paths.

Keploy exposes a public REST API (API key auth), a Kubernetes Proxy REST API (auth unknown from docs), and a CLI (`keploy`) that authenticates via `keploy login`; no GraphQL or confirmed MCP connect endpoint was found in the surfaced docs.

Kerala.gov.in itself exposes no developer API surface; the linked Kerala Open Government Data portal is a web-managed data platform that requires JanParichay/NIC login for contributor access, with no public HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration surface confirmed.

Kestra exposes a REST API at `http://localhost:8080/api/v1` (API token via `Authorization: Bearer ...`, or Basic Auth if enabled), plus a public MCP server at `https://api.kestra.io/v1/mcp` that the docs present as unauthenticated/standard MCP transport.
Ketryx exposes a gated HTTP API docs surface requiring a Ketryx workspace login; no public spec, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
KeyBid on keybid.eu exposes one public MCP surface at https://keybid.eu/mcp; no other developer integration surfaces or credentials were found from the available docs.
Keycloak exposes one public developer surface: the Admin REST API, authenticated with an administrator bearer token/user account.
KeyServ appears to expose only a public website/web app with no discoverable developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface from accessible pages.
Keystone exposes a GraphQL API at /api/graphql and a keystone CLI; GraphQL uses session-based auth via user email/password or Keystone session configuration.

Google Knowledge Graph Search API exposes one HTTP surface at https://kgsearch.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated by either an API key (`key` query param) or an OAuth 2.0 access token (`access_token` query param); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

Khan Academy exposes a public, developer-facing web API surface on khanacademy.org; I could confirm only that it exists and appears GraphQL-backed, but no public spec URL or reliable auth details were discoverable.
Kill Bill exposes one documented developer surface: the authenticated REST API at apidocs.killbill.io, using HTTP Basic admin credentials plus the tenant API key header.
Kinde exposes two documented HTTP APIs: the Management API (M2M bearer auth) and the Account API (user bearer token auth); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the discovered docs.
Kindora exposes one public developer surface: a no-auth MCP server at https://kindora-mcp.azurewebsites.net/mcp/; no API/GraphQL/CLI surfaces were identified.
Kit exposes a v4 REST API, a public docs MCP server, and a creator-account MCP server; API auth supports API keys or OAuth, while Kit MCP uses creator-scoped OAuth.
Klavis AI exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.klavis.ai, using a bearer API key in the Authorization header.
Klaviyo exposes one authenticated REST API, a CLI, and an MCP server; auth options include private API key, public API key for some API calls, and OAuth bearer tokens for the API.

Knock integrates via a public Knock API, authenticated Management API, Knock CLI, and an MCP server page; auth is via environment API keys for the public API and service tokens for Management API/CLI.
Ko-fi exposes one public developer integration surface: an authenticated HTTP webhooks API for payment events; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
komoot.net has no public developer API; the only documented integration surface is the public, unauthenticated Photon geocoding HTTP API at https://photon.komoot.io/api.
kompany.com exposes one authenticated HTTP integration surface: the KYC API developer portal/API, with developer-portal account access; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Kong exposes Konnect HTTP APIs (control planes, control-plane config, API Catalog, Developer Portal), the Konnect MCP server, and the kongctl CLI; Konnect auth uses PATs or kongctl device-flow tokens.

Kontent.ai exposes public Delivery REST/GraphQL, Image Transformation, and Custom Elements JS APIs; authenticated Management, Subscription, and optionally Sync API v2; plus an MCP server with undisclosed auth.

Kraken exposes Spot REST, Spot WebSocket v1/v2, Unified FIX, a kraken CLI, and a built-in MCP server; OAuth is public-facing for client registration/authorization, and Spot REST uses HMAC-SHA512 API-Key/API-Sign auth while public market-data endpoints are unauthenticated.
KrakenD exposes a public `krakend` CLI plus HTTP gateway surfaces for Community and Enterprise editions, and an AI Gateway MCP surface; Enterprise licensing is handled via a provided license file.
Kredit exposes a public ChatGPT app surface and a Secure Collect HTTP product that requires UAE PASS-based login; no public API, GraphQL, MCP endpoint, or CLI docs were found.

Krisp exposes a hosted MCP server at https://mcp.krisp.ai/mcp (Streamable HTTP, OAuth2 PKCE) and a Webhook API for meeting outputs; the SDK docs show developer SDKs but no machine-readable public API/GraphQL spec was found.
Krónan exposes one documented developer surface: a REST HTTP Public API at https://api.kronan.is/api/v1/ using `Authorization: AccessToken <token>`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Kubera exposes one authenticated HTTP Data API and two authenticated MCP endpoints (/api/v1/mcp and /api/v2/mcp), all using a Kubera-issued API/MCP token sent as `Authorization: Basic <AUTH_TOKEN>`.

Kubernetes.io exposes the Kubernetes REST API surface; auth is via kubeconfig-based cluster credentials, and the API spec is published as a generated OpenAPI/Swagger JSON in the upstream Kubernetes repo.

Labs64’s public developer surface is NetLicensing: one RESTful HTTP API and a documented NetLicensing MCP entry; REST API auth is via NetLicensing account/API key credentials, while MCP auth could not be confirmed from the docs read.

Lakera.ai exposes two public HTTP APIs: the Bearer-authenticated Guard API at https://api.lakera.ai/v2 and the Bearer-authenticated Platform API at https://platform.lakera.ai/api/v1-beta; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

LambdaTest/TestMu AI exposes HyperExecute CLI, Kane CLI, SmartUI Upload API v2, and the remote TestMu AI MCP server; auth is via username/access key, OAuth session, and SmartUI project token depending on surface.
Lambus exposes no confirmed public developer API/CLI; only an AI Connector MCP-related surface was found, but its actual connect endpoint and auth mechanics remain unknown.
Lamini exposes a bearer-authenticated REST API and a Python SDK using the same Lamini API key via `LAMINI_API_KEY`; no GraphQL, MCP, or standalone CLI surface was confirmed.
LanceDB exposes a documented Enterprise REST API at an endpoint under `/v1`, authenticated with an `x-api-key` header; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the available docs.

HM Land Registry exposes at least the public Use land and property data REST API (API key via Authorization header) and the Business Gateway Official Search of Whole (with Priority) with Data REST API (auth mechanics not fully determined from the docs read).

surfaces.name: Use land and property data API
HM Land Registry Business Gateway API: Official Search of Whole (with Priority) with Data

domains.summary: LangChain’s public integration surface includes the LangSmith Deployment Control Plane HTTP API (api.host.langchain.com, OpenAPI documented in docs) and LangChain’s MCP adapter library; the control plane accepts `X-Api-Key` or bearer auth, while the MCP adapter docs describe client-side use of HTTP/SSE MCP servers without a LangChain-issued credential.

Langfuse exposes a Basic-authenticated public REST API, a Basic-authenticated MCP server, and a CLI that uses the same project API key pair via environment variables; the docs MCP server exists but its auth mechanics were not determined.
Cloud Translation exposes one authenticated REST/HTTP API at `https://translation.googleapis.com`, usable with Google Cloud OAuth/service-account bearer auth or an API key.
LanguageTool exposes one authenticated Proofreading HTTP API and one public POST-only check endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

lastminute.com exposes a public MCP server at https://mcp.lastminute.com/mcp; the developer portal is a Stoplight workspace login, but no public HTTP/GraphQL spec or CLI surface was confirmed from accessible docs.

LaunchDarkly exposes a REST API, an MCP server, and a CLI; the REST API supports personal/service access tokens or session cookies, while MCP and CLI auth details were not fully pinned down from the pages read.

LeadConnector/HighLevel exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at `https://services.leadconnectorhq.com`, authenticated via `Authorization` header using either a Private Integration Token, OAuth 2.0 access token, or legacy API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
LeapScholar exposes only a public llms.txt documentation surface; no authenticated API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was identified.
Learnifier exposes one public developer surface: an Admin REST API documented on the Learnifier developer page; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Learning Commons exposes one authenticated Knowledge Graph REST API, one MCP server with auth unknown, and a public Evaluators Playground; API keys are issued via the Learning Commons Platform.

surfaces.name: Learning Commons Knowledge Graph MCP server

domains.summary: LegalZoom exposes an OAuth 2.0 authorization server plus an MCP-related docs page; no public HTTP API spec or CLI was found from the surfaced docs.
Lemon Squeezy exposes one public REST API surface (Bearer API key auth) plus public docs/help pages; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
Deel exposes a REST API at https://api.letsdeel.com/rest authenticated with a Deel API key, plus an MCP server at https://api.letsdeel.com/mcp using OAuth.

Letta exposes a documented HTTP Agents API, a remote client API, and MCP tool integration; only the main Agents API had clearly documented bearer-key auth, while the others' auth mechanics were not fully pinned down.

Lever exposes one public developer surface: the Lever REST API at `https://api.lever.co/v1` (sandbox: `https://api.sandbox.lever.co/v1/`), authenticated with OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Leyware MCP Server exposes one public MCP surface at https://leyware-mcp.vercel.app/mcp and no credentials were identified.
liblab exposes a required-auth CLI (`liblab`) and public docs about MCP generation; no public HTTP or GraphQL developer API surface was confirmed.
Library Agent API v1 is the only public integration surface I found; it is an HTTP/REST API at https://libraryagent.googleapis.com/ and requires Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
LibreTranslate exposes one HTTP API and one MCP server; both use LibreTranslate API keys, and the `ltmanage` CLI is documented for key management.

licensing.googleapis.com exposes the Google Workspace Enterprise License Manager REST API at https://licensing.googleapis.com/ and requires Google OAuth Bearer auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

LinkedIn has two documented public integration surfaces I could confirm: Consumer Solutions Platform (Share on LinkedIn) and the Marketing API Program; both use OAuth-based app credentials, with no MCP server or CLI found.
Linktree exposes one developer HTTP surface for its Agentic API, authenticated via OAuth client registration and the linked authorize/token flow.
Linode exposes one public developer API surface (Linode API v4) and one CLI (linode-cli); both use personal access tokens, and the API also supports OAuth client-based auth.
No public developer integration surface was confirmed for list-manage.com; only Mailchimp-hosted list-manage tracking/forms references were found, with auth left unknown.
Listen Notes exposes one developer surface: the Listen Notes HTTP API at `https://listen-api.listennotes.com/api/`, authenticated with a Listen Notes API key sent in the `X-ListenAPI-Key` header.

LiteLLM exposes one main OpenAI-compatible proxy HTTP API, an MCP gateway, and the `lite` CLI; all use LiteLLM virtual keys, typically via `Authorization`/`x-litellm-api-key`, with CLI login using proxy URL plus API key.
Lithic exposes one public developer API and one MCP server, both authenticated with a Lithic API key via the Authorization header.

`live.com` itself exposes no confirmed public integration surface; the only developer surface found under Microsoft-linked pages is Microsoft Graph REST API, authenticated via Microsoft Entra app credentials or delegated Microsoft user tokens.
LJ Aero exposes one developer surface: the authenticated DFlight REST API using an `x-api-key` header; no other public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surfaces were found.
Replit exposes one public integration surface here: MCP connections for Replit Agent, authenticated via OAuth sign-in through Replit Integrations.

surfaces.name: Replit MCP Servers for Replit Agent

domains.summary: LlamaIndex exposes a public hosted MCP docs server and a bearer-authenticated LlamaParse REST API backed by a single LlamaCloud API key.

surfaces.name: LlamaIndex hosted MCP docs search server

domains.summary: Laminar (lmnr.ai) exposes a streamable-http MCP server at `https://api.lmnr.ai/v1/mcp` authenticated with a project API key, plus the `lmnr-cli` command authenticated via user login/device flow.
LobeHub exposes one public HTTP API (OpenAPI at /openapi.json) using OAuth2, a public WebMCP endpoint at https://lobehub.com/api/mcp, and the `lh` CLI.

Local Falcon exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server; both use the same Local Falcon API key, with REST also supporting Bearer header auth and MCP supporting Bearer or query-string key auth.
Localiza exposes a developer portal at developers2.localiza.com, but I could not confirm any public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface or its exact auth mechanism from accessible docs.
Google Local Services API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at https://localservices.googleapis.com/v1, using OAuth2 access tokens with the Google Ads scope.
LocalXpose exposes one authenticated CLI (`loclx`) plus an authenticated HTTP tunnel surface; credentials are via a personal access token from the dashboard.
LocationIQ exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for geocoding, routing, static maps, nearby places, timezone, and balance; all use a single access token via the `key` query parameter.
Locust exposes a public documentation site and a public CLI (`locust`); no authenticated developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or login credential surface was found.
Found one public integration surface: the Vista Software Public API on loft.com.br, authenticated by a Vista-issued API key sent as the `key` query parameter.

loganalytics.io exposes one developer surface: the Azure Monitor Logs Query REST API at https://api.loganalytics.azure.com, authenticated with an Azure AD bearer token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Cloud Logging exposes a public MCP server at https://logging.googleapis.com/mcp and a Cloud Logging API v2 HTTP surface; API auth mechanics weren’t pinned from the pages read.

LogMeIn exposes two developer surfaces on logmein.com: the LogMeIn Central API (HTTP, Basic auth via CompanyID+PSK) and the LogMeIn Resolve API family, including an HTTP REST surface and a GraphQL reporting endpoint, both using GoTo OAuth bearer tokens.

LogRocket exposes one documented MCP server, a GraphQL API, and HTTP APIs for user identification and Galileo highlights; the MCP server accepts a project-scoped LogRocket API key via `Authorization: Bearer`.
Better Stack/Logtail exposes a public MCP server plus two authenticated HTTP APIs: Telemetry API (Bearer API token) and log ingest API (Bearer source token).

surfaces.name: Better Stack log ingest HTTP API

domains.summary: Logto exposes one developer-facing HTTP surface, the Management API at `https://{tenant-id}.logto.app/api`, protected by M2M app credentials via HTTP Basic auth; the OpenAPI spec is at `https://openapi.logto.io/source.json`.
Loket-acc.nl exposes the Loket REST API and OAuth2 flows; authentication is via Loket-issued OAuth client plus Loket user login, with no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI found.
LONA exposes one public MCP server at https://mcp.lona.agency/mcp protected by MCP OAuth, plus a public MCP tools reference in docs; no HTTP REST or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Looker exposes a REST API plus two MCP surfaces: a managed MCP server and an MCP Toolbox workflow; all require either Looker API credentials or Looker OAuth, depending on the surface.

Looma Labs exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated Dream Machine REST API at https://api.lumalabs.ai/dream-machine/v1 using a bearer API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced publicly.
Loqate exposes REST APIs keyed by API key, a public docs MCP server, and the Reach MCP/CLI interfaces that use a Loqate API key via `LOQATE_API_KEY`.
Lorikeet exposes a public integrations library and an authenticated MCP integration surface; no public HTTP/GraphQL API or CLI was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
lotteimall.com exposes a public AI-friendly content surface via llms.txt; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
lotteon.com exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated LotteON OpenAPI at https://openapi.lotteon.com using a seller-issued bearer API key in the Authorization header.
Lovable exposes a public Build-with-URL HTTP surface and an authenticated MCP server at https://mcp.lovable.dev; integration docs are public.
lovelymango-zzaim-mcp exposes a public MCP server at /mcp and a Supabase OAuth 2.0 auth surface with dynamic client registration.

lowes.com exposes LowesX HTTP APIs for Cart Connect, Agentic Commerce, Order Management, and Pro Scan; all require auth, with OAuth client-credentials + X-Client-Id for the first three and x-api-access for Pro Scan.

LSEG exposes LSEG Data Platform HTTP APIs, the LSEG Data Library for Python CLI, an Eikon Data API, and a filings GraphQL endpoint; confirmed auth uses App Key plus either OAuth client-credentials or password-grant/desktop login depending on surface.

Lucid exposes one public developer surface here: the Lucid REST API at https://api.lucid.co, authenticated with either a Lucid API key or an OAuth 2.0 access token; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Lucid exposes one authenticated REST API, a public MCP server, and the public `lucid-package` CLI; no GraphQL surface found.

Lufthansa exposes two documented REST API groupings in the developer portal—Open API and Partner APIs—both using developer-issued credentials via API key or OAuth client credentials; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Lugg exposes only public web surfaces discoverable here: consumer booking/estimate, retailer partner info, and mover signup; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

surfaces.name: Lugg retailer partner dashboard / big & bulky delivery

domains.summary: Lume Imobiliária exposes a public website and a streamable-http MCP server protected by OAuth with `mcp:read`; no public HTTP API, GraphQL API, or CLI was found.
Lumin exposes one REST API at https://api.luminpdf.com/v1 with API key or OAuth 2.0 auth, plus an MCP server at https://mcp.luminpdf.com/mcp.

LunarCrush offers one public REST API, an MCP server, a CLI, and AI/content endpoints; all require the same API key except the MCP endpoint URL itself, which is public but still authenticated with the API key.
LunarCrush exposes one authenticated REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI, all using the same bearer API key.

Lusha exposes one REST API (OpenAPI v3 at api.lusha.com/v3) authenticated with an `api_key` header, plus one MCP server at mcp.lusha.com that accepts either OAuth connector auth or an `x-api-key` header using the same Lusha API key.
lycorp.co.jp exposes the LINE Messaging API HTTP surface (Bearer channel access token); I found no separate public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface on the domain itself.
Lyft exposes the Concierge API for Lyft Business integrations; authentication uses a Lyft Developer Portal API client/Client ID connected to a Business program.

Lytical exposes one public MCP server at `https://mcp.lytical.ai` secured by OAuth 2.0, plus a tracking-script delivery HTTP endpoint on `https://lytcdn.com` that uses the site tracking token in the `site` query parameter.
m3ter.com exposes a single authenticated REST API surface at https://api.m3ter.com; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Maersk exposes an HTTP API catalogue at https://api.maersk.com via the Maersk Developer Portal, authenticated with a portal-issued Consumer Key in the `Authorization` header; some API products are also OAuth-protected but the exact mechanics were not confirmed.
Maersk exposes an HTTP developer API catalogue at https://api.maersk.com, authenticated primarily with a portal-generated Consumer Key sent as Authorization APIKeyHeader.
Magento exposes two developer surfaces: REST and GraphQL APIs, both authenticated; REST supports bearer admin tokens or integration OAuth credentials.
Magic Patterns exposes one authenticated REST API v3 and one MCP server; both use the same Magic Patterns API key in `x-mp-api-key`.

MAIF exposes a developer portal at developer.apis.maif.fr with authenticated access for partner accounts, plus public pages describing digital insurance journeys; no HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI developer surface with a machine-readable spec was confirmed from the available pages.

maif.github.io exposes three developer-relevant HTTP surfaces: Izanami feature access API, Otoroshi admin REST API, and Meteole weather model client API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

MailboxValidator exposes one authenticated HTTP email-validation API (API key in `key` query param) plus an MCP integration page, but no public machine-readable spec or confirmed MCP connect endpoint surfaced.

Mailchimp exposes two documented developer surfaces: the Marketing REST API (OpenAPI spec pointer in GitHub, API key or OAuth 2) and the Transactional API (OpenAPI spec pointer in GitHub, API key); it also provides a Transactional Messaging MCP server at https://mandrillapp.com/mcp authenticated with the Transactional API key.
MailerLite exposes one REST API, a CLI, and an MCP server; all require either an API token or OAuth-based client login depending on surface.

MailerSend exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.mailersend.com/v1 (Bearer API token) plus a public beta MCP server at https://mcp.mailersend.com/mcp; no separate GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Mailgun exposes the Mailgun Email API (US/EU OpenAPI REST) plus a local Mailgun MCP server, both authenticated with a Mailgun API key.
Mailjet exposes one public developer API surface (Email API v3/v3.1 over REST) and one MCP server; both use the account API Key + Secret Key, and no separate GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

Mailtrap offers authenticated REST APIs for Email Sending/SMTP, Email Sandbox, and API token management, plus an authenticated MCP server; the shared credential is a Mailtrap API token, accepted as `Authorization: Bearer` or `Api-Token` on HTTP APIs and passed with an account ID to the MCP server.
mainfunc.ai exposes no confirmed public developer integration surface on its own domain; the seeded MCP endpoint 404s, and no API/GraphQL/CLI docs or machine-readable specs were found.
Make exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and a CLI; API auth is via API token or OAuth 2.0 client, MCP auth is via OAuth or MCP token, and CLI auth exists but mechanics weren’t pinned down.
Make A Viz exposes a web editor surface at https://makeaviz.com/editor; I could not confirm any public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface from the site.
MakeMyTrip exposes myBiz HTTP APIs for Travel Request and Expense Management, plus an HRMS SFTP-based integration; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI developer surface was confirmed.
Notion exposes an authenticated REST API, Admin API, MCP server, and Notion CLI; auth is via PATs, internal/public OAuth tokens, an org bot token for Admin API, and `ntn login` for the CLI.
Malwarebytes.com exposes no public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI on the pages found; the only integration surfaces surfaced were authenticated ThreatDown Nebula and OneView web consoles.
Managed Identities API exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST surface at managedidentities.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
Mandrillapp.com currently exposes a public MCP endpoint at https://mandrillapp.com/mcp and Mailchimp Transactional developer access via API key-authenticated HTTP/SMTP integrations.

Manufacturer Center API exposes one authenticated REST surface at https://manufacturers.googleapis.com using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens or Google service account credentials via Authorization: Bearer.
Manus exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at https://api.manus.ai authenticated by API key or OAuth2 bearer token; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Mapify exposes one developer integration surface: an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://mapify.so/mcp.
MarcoPolo exposes one developer-facing MCP server plus workspace CLI/SDK access, both authenticated with a workspace-scoped API token; no separate REST or GraphQL API was confirmed.
Marqeta exposes one public developer API surface, the Core API (HTTP/REST) authenticated with Marqeta-issued basic credentials, plus a beta MCP server that also requires Marqeta API credentials.

Marqo exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for agentic search, conversational discovery, and event tracking; developer auth uses the Marqo index ID header and separate API keys, with no MCP or CLI surface confirmed.

masbasbas.com exposes a public storefront browsing HTTP surface and a public UCP MCP surface; no developer-issued API credential is evidenced beyond the store's OAuth placeholders, which do not resolve to a usable documented integration surface.

mashape.com now redirects developers to Kong’s public integration surface: Konnect HTTP APIs, a Konnect MCP server, and CLIs decK/Inso; Konnect access token is the primary credential, plus Kong Gateway license for enterprise quickstarts.
Massive exposes a REST API, WebSocket API, and remote MCP server; REST/WebSocket use a Massive API key, and the MCP server uses OAuth via mcp.massive.com.
Mastercard Developers exposes many product HTTP APIs behind per-project credentials and advertises an MCP surface, but the MCP endpoint/auth details were not confirmable from accessible docs.
Matomo exposes two HTTP APIs (Analytics Web API via token_auth or OAuth 2.0, and Tracking Web API) plus an MCP Server; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
maven.org exposes two public developer surfaces: a public Central Search REST API and an authenticated Maven Central Publisher API using a portal token.

Maxio exposes one public developer API surface: the Maxio Advanced Billing HTTP API, authenticated with an API key via HTTP Basic auth; MCP is described publicly but no connect endpoint or auth mechanics were confirmed.

Trellix (mcafee.com) exposes developer-facing HTTP APIs via the Trellix Developer Portal and the on-prem ePolicy Orchestrator Web API; EDR APIs are documented but auth specifics weren’t pinned down from the pages read.

surfaces.name: Trellix ePolicy Orchestrator On-prem Web API

domains.summary: Glinded exposes one authenticated MCP surface at https://mcp-detprm6tzq-uc.a.run.app using Clerk OAuth2; no public HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
How To Student exposes a public MCP server at https://mcp-hnh4eph34q-uc.a.run.app/mcp with no developer authentication required.
Adobe AEM exposes a single remote MCP surface at https://mcp.adobeaemcloud.com/adobe/mcp/ using Adobe IMS user OAuth authenticated through supported MCP clients.
mcp.com.ai exposes public docs plus HAPI Server HTTP, public runMCP HTTP, and QBot CLI; HAPI and QBot require beta access/login, with no separate MCP endpoint/spec confirmed from the docs pages.

mcp.read exposes the Google Developer Knowledge MCP server and Google Merchant API HTTP surface; both use Google OAuth or an API key, with the Merchant API REST base at https://merchantapi.googleapis.com.
mcpevals.io exposes one developer surface: the MCP Evals Node.js package/GitHub Action, which requires an OpenAI API key via `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
MCP Market exposes a public website/catalog on mcpmarket.com; no programmatic HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration surface or auth could be confirmed from the available public pages.
mcpservers.org publicly lists MCP server projects; no authenticated developer API, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found on the domain.

mcptotal.io exposes one authenticated MCP server at https://mcptotal.io/mcp using OAuth 2.1 with PKCE and dynamic client registration; issuer metadata advertises the authorize, token, and registration endpoints plus the supported scopes.

Medidata mdsol.com exposes one developer integration surface: the Rave Web Services (RWS) HTTP API at `https://<tenant>.mdsol.com/RaveWebServices`, authenticated with either Basic auth or MAuth/HMAC over HTTPS.

Medcorder exposes one documented developer HTTP API, the Nearby Doctor API at api.medcorder.com with an OpenAPI spec at medcorder.com/openapi.yaml; it requires a Firebase ID token. No GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.

Medeloop.ai exposes four public product surfaces—Analytics, EvidenceKit, Grants, and Care—but I could not confirm any public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI specs or concrete developer authentication methods from the available pages.
MediaAlpha exposes no discoverable public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface from its site; only a public marketing website was confirmed.
mediawiki.org exposes the public MediaWiki Action API and REST API; I also recorded the OAuth developer page as documentation, with no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface confirmed.
Medium exposes a single public developer-facing web surface at https://medium.com; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Mediumapi.com exposes one developer integration surface: an unofficial REST API on RapidAPI authenticated with a RapidAPI key via `x-rapidapi-key`.
Medusa exposes authenticated REST Store and Admin APIs plus a docs-described MCP remote server; Store API supports publishable API key, customer JWT, or cookie session auth.

Campfire exposes a token-authenticated HTTP API at https://api.meetcampfire.com and an authenticated MCP server at https://api.meetcampfire.com/mcp; both use the Campfire API token in the Authorization header.
MeetGeek exposes a Bearer-token REST API plus two MCP servers: local stdio MCP using an API key env var and cloud Streamable HTTP MCP using OAuth via auth.meetgeek.ai.

Meetup exposes one developer API surface: a GraphQL API at https://api.meetup.com/gql-ext secured by OAuth 2 bearer access tokens; OAuth client registration is available for Pro members, with authorize/access endpoints on secure.meetup.com.
Meilisearch exposes one authenticated HTTP API, a stdio MCP server, and the meilisearch-importer CLI; all use Meilisearch-issued API keys/master key via Authorization or env vars.
Mem offers one public developer API over HTTP with API key bearer auth and one hosted MCP server at https://mcp.mem.ai/mcp using OAuth.

Mem0 exposes one managed REST API, one cloud MCP server, and one CLI, all using the same Mem0 API key; the self-hosted OSS bundle also provides a local REST server with auth that is documented but not fully pinned down here.

surfaces.name: Mem0 Open Source REST API Server

domains.summary: Memorystore for Memcached exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST API at memcache.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
Mentirosa exposes a single public MCP server at https://mentirosa.vercel.app/mcp with no authentication.
Meraki exposes one public developer surface: the Meraki Dashboard API v1 over HTTP, authenticated by either an API key in `X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key` or OAuth 2.0 bearer auth.
Meraki exposes the Dashboard REST API, plus MCP and CLI wrappers around it; authentication is via a Meraki Dashboard API key.

Mercedes-Benz exposes a developer portal plus documented HTTP product APIs; from the pages found I could confirm the portal requires a Mercedes me ID, while the specific Fleet/API product auth details and any GraphQL/MCP/CLI surfaces were not yet confirmed.

surfaces.name: Third Party Data Access EU Data Act

domains.summary: Mercure.rocks exposes one public developer surface: the Mercure Hub HTTP API at `/.well-known/mercure`, authenticated with a hub JWT via `Authorization: Bearer`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Mercury exposes a REST API, an OAuth integration surface for partner apps, and a hosted read-only MCP server; authentication is via Mercury API tokens, approved OAuth client credentials, or MCP OAuth login.

Mercury Insurance exposes a partner API portal plus four authenticated REST APIs (HO3 Policy Quote, Personal Auto Policy Quote, Personal Auto Policy Change, and Mercury Search) and one authenticated Homeowners Insurance Verification API; all use the same portal login/Bearer auth on the Mercury API servers.
Merge.dev exposes the Merge Agent Handler HTTP API, MCP server, and `merge` CLI; authentication is via a Merge Agent Handler account for CLI/MCP and an org access key for the HTTP API.

Mermaid.ai publicly exposes two MCP surfaces: a Mermaid MCP Server at https://mcp.mermaid.ai/mcp (also SSE at /sse) and a public Claude MCP App at https://chatgpt.mermaid.ai/anthropic/mcp; the server uses a Mermaid Chart account with Bearer auth for account-backed tools.

Mermaid Chart exposes two MCP surfaces: an authenticated MCP server at https://mcp.mermaid.ai/mcp (also SSE) using a Bearer token, and a public Claude MCP app at https://chatgpt.mermaid.ai/anthropic/mcp; no HTTP API, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Mervia.ai exposes a public marketing website and describes a ChatGPT-hosted shopping app/MCP-style integration, but no developer API, GraphQL, CLI, or documented authentication/credentials were found.
MessageBird exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for the core platform and Conversations API, plus request-signature verification for inbound webhooks; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Metabase exposes a REST API, an Agent API, an MCP server, and the `mb` CLI; auth is via API key for HTTP/CLI and embedded OAuth for MCP.
Dataproc Metastore exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at metastore.googleapis.com using Google OAuth2/ADC Bearer tokens; no public, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

Metaview exposes one MCP surface and documented ATS integrations for Greenhouse, Ashby, and Lever; auth is via Metaview account OAuth for MCP/Greenhouse/Lever OAuth, or a Metaview-issued MCP API key for MCP and Lever/Ashby key-based setup.

Meteosource exposes one public developer surface: the Weather API over HTTP, authenticated with a single API key passed either in `X-API-Key` or as `api_key` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

Meterplan exposes two public integration surfaces: a no-auth MCP server at https://meterplan.com/mcp and a public REST/OpenAPI API at https://meterplan.com/openapi.json (plus a second public OpenAPI spec for the Smart Meter Texas endpoint at https://meterplan.com/openapi/smt.yaml).

surfaces.name: Meter Energy Smart Meter Texas Data API

domains.summary: Method exposes one public developer-facing surface: the Method API HTTP/REST API, authenticated with a team/environment-specific Bearer API key (`sk_...`) in the `Authorization` header.
Metorial exposes one documented REST API at https://api.metorial.com, authenticated with a bearer API key in the Authorization header; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Metriport exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the Medical API and the FHIR Converter API, both using a user-minted API key in the `x-api-key` header; no MCP server or CLI was found.

Metronome exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated REST API at https://api.metronome.com/v1 using a bearer API token in the Authorization header; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Miataru exposes one HTTP API surface (Miataru API at http://miataru.com/) secured by per-device `DeviceKey`; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Microcks exposes an authenticated HTTP API and documented MCP endpoint support; no separate GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Microsoft Graph exposes one public developer surface here: the REST API v1.0 at https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0, authenticated with a Microsoft Entra app registration.

Microsoft Entra identity platform on microsoftonline.com exposes OAuth 2.0 auth code, client credentials, and device code flows via login.microsoftonline.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: Microsoft identity platform OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow

domains.summary: Middesk exposes a required-auth REST API, GraphQL API, and MCP server; all can use Middesk API keys, with MCP also documented as OAuth 2.1-based.
Middleware exposes a public HTTP API docs entry and an MCP server for OpsAI; auth details were not fully confirmed from the surfaced pages.
Midify appears to expose a public web app surface at https://midify.app with no discoverable developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface from public docs.
Midpage exposes a REST API, an MCP connector, and a PostgreSQL read replica; the REST API uses a Midpage API key, while the MCP connector requires an active Midpage subscription/account.

Migros.com.tr exposes one discovered developer surface: a Parse-hosted HTTP API for catalog/search/campaign data, authenticated with a Parse API key via `X-API-Key`; no native Migros public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surfaced in the available docs.

Milvus exposes RESTful HTTP APIs on the instance endpoint with Bearer `username:password` tokens when auth is enabled, plus a public remote MCP SDK Code Helper server and a local MCP server that delegates to a Milvus instance.

surfaces.name: Milvus MCP server (remote SDK Code Helper)

domains.summary: MineSkin exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.mineskin.org (OpenAPI v2.0.0) using a bearer API key from account.mineskin.org, plus an MCP server advertised at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json with auth not determined.
Mintlify exposes a public search MCP server, an authenticated admin MCP server over OAuth2, and a Bearer-token REST API using Mintlify API keys.
MintMCP exposes a documented MCP gateway at app.mintmcp.com; no separate public REST, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was confirmed from the available docs.
Mirai exposes a public website plus hotel-client-only booking engine and MCP server tied to a hotelier account; no public GraphQL, CLI, or documented OpenAPI spec was found.
Mist exposes a public REST API at api.mist.com and a Terraform provider CLI; both support API token auth, with a legacy basic-auth username/password path for the provider and API.
Mistral AI exposes a public docs portal, a Bearer-authenticated Studio HTTP API, and the Vibe Code CLI authenticated via a Mistral API key.
Mixedbread exposes a Bearer-authenticated HTTP Search API, an MCP server, and the `mxbai` CLI; all use a Mixedbread API key.
Mixmax exposes one public developer REST API and one MCP server; REST uses a user-minted Mixmax API token, while the MCP server at https://mcp.mixmax.com/mcp uses OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow.

mobile.de exposes REST APIs for Search/Ad-Integration, Seller API, and Lead API; Search API uses HTTP Basic with an API account, while Seller and Lead auth details were not fully determined from the retrieved docs.
Mockaroo exposes two HTTP surfaces: the authenticated Generate API and configurable Mock APIs; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Mockoon exposes a protected admin HTTP API, cloud self-hosted mock deployment, and the Mockoon CLI; authentication is via bearer tokens for cloud access and admin API access.
Modal exposes a Python SDK/HTTP platform API and a CLI, both authenticated with Modal account/service-user tokens; secrets are a separate credential for app runtime injection.

Modal exposes a CLI (`modal`) authenticated by Modal API tokens, plus HTTP Web Functions/Servers on `*.modal.run` that are public by default or can require Proxy Token headers `Modal-Key` and `Modal-Secret`.
Mode exposes two public developer surfaces: the main REST API at `https://app.mode.com/api` using HTTP Basic auth with API tokens, and the Enterprise Discovery API using a Bearer Signature Token.
modelcontextprotocol.io exposes two public MCP streamable-http endpoints and no documented developer-facing HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surface beyond the site’s MCP specs and docs.

surfaces.name: Model Context Protocol main-kill-isr remote server

domains.summary: ModerateContent exposes one public HTTP REST API for image moderation; no developer-issued credentials, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI were confirmed.
Modern Treasury exposes a required-auth HTTP API plus a required-auth MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
MODO exposes one public developer surface: the MODO Conexiones HTTP API for Open Banking/PCP login and token flows, authenticated with `client_id` + `client_secret`.

Moesif exposes a public REST API surface (Collector/Management API v1) authenticated with an API key via `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs pages reviewed.
MojoAuth exposes one documented developer surface: a REST HTTP API at https://api.mojoauth.com secured by a MojoAuth API key (`ApiKeyAuth` in the OpenAPI spec).
Mollie exposes one public REST API at https://api.mollie.com/v2 and one MCP server documented at docs.mollie.com; both require account-issued credentials, with no GraphQL or CLI surface found.
Monarch Initiative exposes one public HTTP REST API surface, Monarch API v3, with OpenAPI spec at https://monarchinitiative.org/openapi.json and no authentication required.
monday.com exposes a GraphQL API, a Platform MCP server, and a CLI; auth uses bearer API tokens or OAuth for GraphQL, OAuth for MCP, and CLI auth remains undocumented.
Mondoir exposes one authenticated HTTP API (OpenAPI at https://mondoir.art/openapi.json) and one authenticated MCP server at https://mondoir.art/mcp, both using ApiKeyAuth/OAuth2 as documented.

monitoring.googleapis.com exposes Cloud Monitoring API v3 over HTTP with Google OAuth Bearer auth, and a public MCP server at https://monitoring.googleapis.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface identified.

Monotype exposes one documented developer API surface, the Monotype Fonts HTTP API, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 using Monotype-issued client ID/secret (Basic to token endpoints, Bearer access token to API calls); no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
MoodTrip exposes one authenticated REST/OpenAPI surface and one public MCP server; auth is via OAuth2 for the API and the MCP endpoint is public.
Moody’s integration surface includes Moody’s Analytics API Hub HTTP APIs protected by `x-api-key` plus a public docs portal; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the available pages.

surfaces.name: Moody's Analytics API Hub docs portal

domains.summary: moonmoonmoonmoon.com is a public web-based collaborative artwork (Moon) with no documented developer auth, API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces.
moonrepo.dev exposes public moon and proto CLIs plus a moon MCP server; no authenticated HTTP/GraphQL APIs were found in the developer docs I reviewed.
Moov exposes one authenticated REST API plus two MCP surfaces: a public docs MCP server and a credentialed TypeScript SDK MCP server.

Morningstar offers authenticated Direct Web Services HTTP APIs (AI Insights, Investment List) using a Morningstar bearer token obtained via the Authentication API, plus a licensed-account MCP server at https://mcp.morningstar.com/mcp.

surfaces.name: Morningstar Direct Web Services - AI Insights

domains.summary: MoSPI appears to expose a public MCP surface at https://datainnovation.mospi.gov.in/mospi-mcp; no other developer-facing HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surfaces or credentials could be confirmed from the available public pages.
MotaWord exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated MCP server at `https://api.motaword.com/mcp`, and a `motaword` CLI; all rely on MotaWord OAuth2-based credentials.
MotherDuck exposes one public REST Admin API, one remote MCP server, and DuckDB client/CLI integration, all authenticated with a user access token.
Motion exposes one public integration surface: a read-only MCP server at https://projects.motionapp.com/mcp authenticated with Motion OAuth via the user’s Motion account.

MotionHub has one programmatic surface, the MotionHub App at https://app.motionhub.ai, authenticated either by OAuth 2.0 (for MCP-connected assistants) or by an administrator-managed API key; no separate public GraphQL or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.url: connection URL provided by your account administrator

domains.summary: MoviePick exposes one public integration surface: an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://moviepick.app/mcp, with dynamic client registration and OAuth endpoints at /oauth2/v1/register, /authorize, and /token.

mp.gov.in exposes one developer-facing integration surface I could confirm: the MPPCB Central Server Software Open API (HTTP REST) with site-specific authenticated upload/calibration endpoints using a Site ID header plus an RSA-encrypted Authorization header.

surfaces.name: MPPCB Central Server Software Open API

domains.summary: mParticle exposes three documented programmatic surfaces: Platform API, Data Planning API, and Data Subject Request API v3, plus the mParticle CLI; API auth appears key-based, while CLI auth could not be pinned from the available docs.
