domains.summary: MSCI exposes a gated HTTP API surface for the MSCI Index API via the developer portal; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Mtaa API is a public REST API with a single HTTP surface at https://mtaa-api.herokuapp.com/api and no authentication.
MT Newswires exposes one public developer integration surface: an authenticated MCP service for licensed news access; no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Musixmatch exposes one authenticated HTTP API (API key via `apikey` query param) and one MCP server that uses the same API key.
Mux exposes one authenticated HTTP API, a hosted MCP server with OAuth, and the Mux CLI; credentials are Mux Access Token ID/Secret Key and an MCP OAuth session.

MX exposes one public HTTP integration surface, the MX Platform API (REST, docs at https://docs.mx.com/api-reference/platform-api/reference/mx-platform-api), authenticated with either Bearer auth or Basic auth, plus related MX APIs (Batch over SFTP, Data Enhancement Off Platform via `MD-API-KEY` + `client_id`, Nexus via `MD-SESSION-TOKEN`, and SSO/OAuth flows via MX-issued registrations/tokens) as recorded.
Public MCP surface at https://my-mcp-server-flame.vercel.app/mcp; no authentication required.
mybusinessaccountmanagement.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API: My Business Account Management API, using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
Google My Business Business Calls API is a single authenticated HTTP surface at mybusinessbusinesscalls.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0 user consent.
Google My Business Business Information API exposes a single authenticated REST surface at mybusinessbusinessinformation.googleapis.com using OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
My Business Lodging API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at https://mybusinesslodging.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
My Business Notifications API exposes one authenticated HTTP API at mybusinessnotifications.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no other public surfaces found.
My Business Place Actions API is the only public integration surface: an HTTP/REST API at https://mybusinessplaceactions.googleapis.com/v1 using either Google OAuth user auth or an API key.
mybusinessqanda.googleapis.com exposes one HTTP API, the My Business Q&A API, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
Google My Business Verifications exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://mybusinessverifications.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
mycolive.com exposes a public website only; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface could be confirmed from accessible pages.
MyDeetz exposes a public marketing/docs surface and a private business signup/dashboard at app.mydeetz.ai; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI was confirmed.
MyFitnessPal exposes one private REST API at `https://api.myfitnesspal.com/v2`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

MyFonts has a public website/llms.txt surface and one authenticated customer-account HTTP API using OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens from the MyFonts account portal; no additional public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
MyRegistry exposes two developer HTTP APIs: Merchant API and Registry API; both use the same `developerKey` API key credential.
n8n exposes a public REST API, a CLI wrapper over that API, and an instance-level MCP server; all are authenticated primarily with an n8n API key, with MCP also supporting a personal access token.

Namecheap exposes one public developer surface: the XML HTTP API at https://api.namecheap.com/xml.response (sandbox: https://api.sandbox.namecheap.com/xml.response), authenticated with the account’s API key plus ApiUser, UserName, and ClientIp query parameters.
Namespace exposes a `nsc` CLI authenticated via `nsc login`; I found no public HTTP, GraphQL, or MCP developer surface in the docs I checked.
NamSor exposes one public developer surface: the NamSor API v2 HTTP API at https://v2.namsor.com/NamSorAPIv2, authenticated with a single API key sent as `X-API-KEY`.
Nango exposes a Bearer-authenticated HTTP API, a public docs MCP server, a CLI, and MCP/tool-calling for action functions.

surfaces.name: Nango action functions via MCP/tool calling

domains.summary: NASA exposes the public NASA Open APIs at api.nasa.gov with a developer API key, plus Earthdata CMR Search, CMR STAC, and Earthdata GraphQL surfaces whose auth requirements weren’t fully determined from the pages reviewed.

Nativeads.com exposes a public DSP website and references advertiser/publisher API docs, but I could not confirm any machine-readable API spec or authenticated programmatic surface details beyond the account login.
Natoma.ai exposes public docs and a governed MCP gateway; the only clearly documented developer auth surface found is SCIM provisioning secured by a Natoma-generated bearer token.
NAVER exposes two developer HTTP API surfaces: NAVER Login API (OAuth client credentials from a registered app) and NAVER Service API (public docs found, auth mechanics not fully pinned).
No public developer integration surface was found for naviplancentral.com; only a login-gated InvestCloud client portal page was exposed, so auth remains unknown.

NBA.com exposes a public NBA Game Operations Manager API portal with stream and REST docs, a no-auth NBA-API Python CLI/package surface for stats scraping, and a separate public NBA Top Shot GraphQL API; no mints-credential was identified for NBA.com itself.
NBG provides a developer portal with multiple HTTP API products, authenticated via OAuth2 bearer tokens using portal-issued app credentials.

Neartail exposes an OAuth-protected MCP server plus OAuth authorize, token, and dynamic client registration endpoints; the issued developer credential is an OAuth client with scopes `readOnly` and `full`.

Neighbor exposes a public web marketplace/help site only; no developer HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was found, and the only recorded credential is a Neighbor user account for the consumer marketplace.
Ubersuggest exposes one public developer surface here: an authenticated MCP server at https://ubersuggest-mcp.neilpatelapi.com/mcp using Ubersuggest account OAuth 2.0.
Neon exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated CLI, and one authenticated MCP server; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Neon exposes one public HTTP API (API key auth), an MCP server (auth not fully pinned), and the Neon CLI (browser/API-key login).
NeoWs exposes one public developer surface: a REST/OpenAPI API at https://neowsapp.com with OpenAPI spec https://neowsapp.com/v1/api-docs, authenticated by an API key.
Neptune Flood exposes one public developer surface: the Quick Quote v4 Simple HTTP API, authenticated with Neptune-issued API username + API secret; no other public API/MCP/CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Neptune Flood Quick Quote v4 Simple API

domains.summary: Nesha.ai exposes only a public web app/sign-in page; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Netatmo exposes one documented HTTP/REST Connect API using OAuth2 bearer tokens, plus an MCP surface advertised by a server card URL whose auth could not be determined.

Netatmo exposes a single developer HTTP API at api.netatmo.com with OAuth2 Bearer auth, plus webhook delivery endpoints secured with a client-secret HMAC signature; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

NetBox exposes a token-authenticated REST API, a token-authenticated read-only GraphQL API, an MCP server with unknown auth from public docs, and a Rust-based `netbox` CLI that uses the same API token.
NetBox at netboxdemo.com exposes a REST API and a read-only GraphQL API, both authenticated with a NetBox API token via Authorization: Bearer.
NetLicensing exposes one authenticated RESTful API and a documented MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

Netlify exposes one public developer integration surface here: an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://netlify-mcp.netlify.app/mcp using Streamable HTTP, authenticated via Netlify OAuth tokens obtained through its OAuth server.
NetSuite exposes one public developer surface: SuiteTalk REST Web Services (including REST record metadata and SuiteQL); it supports OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens or legacy token-based authentication.
Network Connectivity exposes one public developer surface: the Google Cloud Network Connectivity REST API at networkconnectivity.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
Network Management API exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface and one public MCP server; no separate GraphQL or CLI surface found.
Mapped Google Cloud Network Services API as a REST/HTTP surface at https://networkservices.googleapis.com/ requiring Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 bearer authentication.

Neurnav exposes an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://neurnav.com/mcp and OAuth endpoints for authorization, token exchange, and dynamic client registration; no public REST/GraphQL/CLI surface was found.
Neutrino API exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST surface; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Neutrino API exposes one public HTTP API surface with API key + user ID auth; no public GraphQL or CLI found, and a Boomi MCP connector exists but its connect endpoint/auth couldn’t be determined.

Newegg has one public developer API (Marketplace REST) and one SDK/library surface, both using the same API Key + Secret Key credentials in `Authorization` and `SecretKey` headers; no GraphQL, MCP, or standalone CLI surface was found.
New Relic exposes NerdGraph (GraphQL), REST API v2, and a streamable-http MCP server; all require authentication via New Relic user API key, with MCP also supporting the built-in OAuth flow.

Nexafin exposes one public integration surface: an MCP server at https://app.nexafin.com/mcp, authenticated via OAuth 2.1 client registration on Authkit with scopes openid, profile, email, and offline_access.

Vonage (nexmo.com) exposes SMS and Voice HTTP APIs plus the Vonage CLI; SMS uses Basic auth with API key/secret, Voice accepts either JWT (app ID + private key) or Basic auth, and the CLI is configured via `vonage auth set`.

nFusion Solutions exposes one authenticated REST API surface at https://api.nfusionsolutions.biz with OpenAPI spec https://api.nfusionsolutions.biz/swagger/1/swagger.json; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

nFusion Solutions exposes one public integration surface: an authenticated REST/OpenAPI market data API at https://api.nfusionsolutions.biz (spec: https://api.nfusionsolutions.biz/swagger/1/swagger.json); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: nFusion Solutions Market Data API v1

domains.summary: ngrok exposes one public REST API at https://api.ngrok.com, a local unauthenticated Agent API at http://127.0.0.1:4040/api, and the ngrok CLI plus `ngrok api` commands authenticated via API key/authtoken.
nic.at exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated Domainfinder REST API at https://domainfinder.nic.at/api/v1/suggest; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from public docs.

nile.dev exposes a Management HTTP API, a public Auth API surface, an MCP server, and the `nile` CLI; auth uses a Nile API key plus workspace slug for management/MCP, while the auth sign-in endpoint is public.
NVD exposes three HTTP REST APIs—CVE, CPE, and Source—each using the same optional NVD API key in the `apiKey` request header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
NLP Cloud exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.nlpcloud.io/v1 using an API token (`Authorization: Token ...`), with OpenAPI at https://nlpcloud.io/openapi.json.

NOAA exposes two public developer APIs on noaa.gov: the NWS API at api.weather.gov (requires a identifying User-Agent header) and NCEI CDO Web Services v2 at ncei.noaa.gov/cdo-web/api/v2 (requires a token header).

domains.summary: Nomic exposes one public developer surface: the Nomic Platform HTTP API at `https://<your-domain>.nomic.ai/api/v0`, authenticated with a Bearer `nomic_api_key` (developer or admin scopes as needed).
Nookipedia exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated REST API at api.nookipedia.com using an API key in X-API-KEY; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Northflank exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated CLI, both using a user/team API token (`Authorization: Bearer ...` for HTTP, `northflank login` for CLI).
NotebookLM exposes a consumer web app plus community CLI/MCP integrations that require a Google sign-in session, and an enterprise API exists but its auth details weren’t pinned from public docs.

Google Cloud notebooks.googleapis.com exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for Agent Platform Workbench/Notebooks and NotebookLM Enterprise; both use Google OAuth/Bearer auth, and no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
NoteitHub exposes a single HTTP API surface authenticated via OAuth 2.0 client registration and bearer tokens; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
NotePlan exposes a public local MCP server and a JavaScript plugin/CLI-style plugin API; both are public and require no NotePlan-issued developer credential.

Notion exposes one REST API, one hosted MCP server, and the `ntn` CLI; all require auth via PAT, internal-connection token, or OAuth access token, with the MCP server using OAuth and the CLI supporting `ntn login` or `NOTION_API_TOKEN`.
Notion exposes a REST API, hosted MCP server, and `ntn` CLI; all require authentication via internal connection bearer token, OAuth access token, or PAT, depending on the surface.
Notion exposes a REST API, an Admin REST API, a CLI (`ntn`), and MCP support; auth uses bearer tokens for internal connections, OAuth public connections, PATs, and org bot tokens for Admin API.
Notion exposes a REST API, Admin REST API, hosted MCP server, and `ntn` CLI; all require Notion-issued bearer credentials, with the CLI using `ntn login`.
Novu exposes a private REST API, a bearer-authenticated MCP server, a CLI sync command using the secret key, and a separate public docs MCP server.
NOWPayments exposes one authenticated REST API (API key; IPN secret for webhook verification) plus public docs/guides and no confirmed CLI, GraphQL, or MCP connect endpoint from public pages.
npmjs.com exposes the npm Registry HTTP API and the npm CLI; registry auth uses npm session/access tokens via Bearer headers, plus OIDC id_token exchange for trusted publishing.
NPR exposes one public developer surface: the NPR API (HTTP) using an `apiKey` issued via NPR account registration; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
nrm.se exposes no public developer APIs, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI; only public website pages including the ESB specimen loan application form, which requires no integration credential.

domains.summary: NSIDC offers a single public integration surface here: an HTTP data-access/download surface for NSIDC DAAC data, authenticated via Earthdata Login; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.

Ntropy exposes one authenticated HTTP API (OpenAPI at https://docs.ntropy.com/openapi.json, base https://api.ntropy.com/v3, X-API-KEY header), plus a public docs MCP server at https://docs.ntropy.com/_mcp/server.
NutriMindz AI exposes only a public website/nutrition search pages; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, CLI, or auth credentials were found.
Nuvei exposes two documented HTTP API surfaces: REST 2.0 and the older API Reference/Test Lab (REST 1.0/Web SDK/Simply Connect), both using the merchant API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

surfaces.name: Nuvei API Reference / Test Lab (REST 1.0, Web SDK, Simply Connect)

domains.summary: Nuwa Labs exposes a public storefront read-only HTTP surface, an authenticated UCP MCP endpoint, and an account OAuth flow for customer-account access.
NyquistAI exposes one public integration surface: a remote MCP server at https://mcp.nyquistai.com/agent/mcp, authenticated via NyquistAI account OAuth login.

NYTimes exposes five public HTTP APIs (Archive, Article Search, Books, Most Popular, Top Stories) authenticated by an NYT API key in the `api-key` query parameter; RSS feeds are public/unclear and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
O2.cz exposes no confirmed public developer API/MCP/CLI surface; only the Moje O2 web app was identified, authenticated by O2 ID login (email/password, with optional SMS OTP).
CalorieCam exposes one public MCP server at https://oaimcp-production.up.railway.app/mcp with open access; no additional developer surfaces were found.
OakbrookOne exposes a public MCP server at https://oakbrook-gpt-api-432296117705.europe-west2.run.app/mcp and no other developer surfaces were confirmed.
oauth.com exposes only a public documentation site with OAuth 2.0 guidance; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
oauth2.googleapis.com exposes Google OAuth 2.0 REST API only; it requires OAuth 2.0 access tokens (or query token) and also supports an API key query parameter for some calls.

obono.at exposes one public developer surface: the obono API (REST/HTTP) at https://demo.obono.at, documented at https://obono.at/vorteile/obono-api, with auth via obono account login using HTTP Basic on the Authorization header.
Obot exposes an authenticated HTTP API, authenticated OpenAI/Anthropic LLM gateway endpoints, a public MCP registry API, and authenticated MCP server connect endpoints, all using Obot API keys.

obp.ch exposes four relevant HTTP integration surfaces: Swiss NextGen API, Finstar PSD2 Account API, Finstar PSD2 Payment API, and Finstar Open Banking Authorization; the first three require OAuth2 client credentials, while the authorization API is public.
Observable exposes public Plot and Framework docs/products, plus authenticated workspace notebook/embed access via API keys or notebook keys; custom SSO is an enterprise OIDC configuration surface.
OceanDrivers appears to have no public developer integration surface discovered; only the public marketing website was found, and no API/MCP/CLI/GraphQL docs or credentials were identified.
OK Kanji exposes only a public web app at okkanji.com; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Okta exposes the Okta Admin Management HTTP API and an Okta MCP server; both require auth via scoped Okta OAuth 2.0 access tokens, with legacy SSWS API tokens also accepted by the management API.

Okta MyAccount Okta Applications exposes one HTTP surface at `/idp/myaccount/okta-applications`, authenticated with OAuth scope `okta.myAccount.oktaApplications.read` and requiring `Accept: application/json; okta-version=1.0.0`.

Okteto exposes one documented HTTP API (Bearer Admin Access Token) and the `okteto` CLI (Personal Access Token via `okteto context use --token` or `OKTETO_TOKEN`); no GraphQL or MCP surface was found.
OKX exposes a documented REST API, an Agent Trade Kit MCP server, and an Agent Trade Kit CLI; REST/Agent tools use OKX API keys, and the MCP flow also supports OAuth client registration.

surfaces.name: OKX Agent Trade Kit MCP Server

domains.summary: OMDb exposes two HTTP surfaces: the main OMDb API and the Poster API, both requiring the same API key passed as `apikey` query parameter; OpenAPI spec is at https://omdbapi.com/swagger.json.
Omni exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI; all authenticate with Bearer API tokens, with OAuth flows minting CLI/MCP-specific PATs.
Omnisend exposes a REST HTTP API and two MCP server docs; HTTP auth supports API keys via `X-API-KEY` and OAuth, while MCP auth could not be pinned from public docs.

Ona exposes an authenticated HTTP API, authenticated CLI, and authenticated MCP integrations; auth is via Ona browser login or personal access token, with Linear/GitHub integrations additionally using the user’s connected third-party account/token.
ondata.github.io exposes one public MCP surface (CKAN MCP Server) and one CLI (normattiva2md, which needs an Exa API key for search mode); no HTTP or GraphQL APIs were found.
Ondato exposes no clearly documented public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface; only public website/product pages and an llms.txt policy were confirmed.
On-Demand Scanning API is a Google Cloud HTTP REST surface at https://ondemandscanning.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth/ADC; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.
OneDev exposes a RESTful API on the installed server URL; public docs confirm basic username/password authentication, and no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Footprint exposes one public developer API at https://api.onefootprint.com authenticated by a secret API key in X-Footprint-Secret-Key, plus public outbound webhooks; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

OneSignal exposes a REST API at https://api.onesignal.com and an MCP server; both require OneSignal credentials, with App API key/App ID for app-scoped operations and Organization API key for org-scoped APIs.

OneUptime exposes a public MCP server, a REST API, a CLI, and a public status-pages API; MCP is confirmed unauthenticated, while REST/CLI/status-page auth remains undetermined from the retrieved docs.

Onfido/Entrust exposes one public developer integration surface: the Entrust Identity Verification REST API at api.onfido.com, authenticated by either API token (Basic) or OAuth client-credentials access token (Bearer).
Onfleet exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at https://onfleet.com/api/v2, authenticated with an organization API key sent in the Authorization header as Bearer.

OnSched exposes one public developer surface: the v3 REST API at https://v3.onsched.com/v3, authenticated via OAuth2 client credentials Bearer tokens, dashboard JWT + x-api-key, or public x-client-id + x-api-key for public routes.
Beautiful.ai exposes one public MCP surface at `https://openai-mcp-696419881849.us-central1.run.app/mcp` (OAuth) and one HTTP API at `https://www.beautiful.ai/api/v1` (Bearer API key).
OpenAI exposes one documented HTTP API and the OpenAI CLI; both use Bearer auth via an API key, with an additional workload-identity short-lived bearer token option for the API.
OpenALPR/Rekor exposes one public developer surface: the Rekor CarCheck REST API at https://api.openalpr.com/v3, authenticated with a CarCheck secret key passed as `secret_key` query param.

OpenAPI Generator exposes a public hosted HTTP API at https://api.openapi-generator.tech and a public CLI (`openapi-generator-cli`/`openapi-generator`); no service-issued developer credentials are required.
OpenAQ exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated OpenAQ API v3 HTTP API; no additional public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

Open Banking UK exposes several developer surfaces: Read/Write Data APIs (authenticated via Directory/DCR-issued client credentials plus bearer tokens), Dynamic Client Registration, Open Data APIs, and MI Reporting APIs; no MCP or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Open Banking MI Reporting / ASPSP APIs

domains.summary: openbankingproject.ch exposes one public developer integration surface: the Swiss NextGen Banking API-Framework HTTP API (OpenAPI/ReDoc docs, bearer-token auth via Authorization header); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced publicly.
Open Build Service exposes one public HTTP API (authenticated with HTTP Basic using an OBS account) and the `osc` CLI for API access; no GraphQL or MCP surface was found.
OpenCage exposes one authenticated REST geocoding API plus authenticated MCP and CLI access, all using the same user-issued geocoding API key.
OpenChannel has one discoverable public integration surface: the OpenChannel Market HTTP API (OpenAPI referenced via APIs.guru); auth mechanism and credential issuance were not publicly documented.
OpenDataCommons.org exposes no developer-authenticated integration surface; the only programmatic surface found is the public Open Definition Licenses API at licenses.opendefinition.org.
Opendatasoft/Huwise exposes a REST Explore API v2.1 with API key or OAuth2 bearer auth, plus a managed MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Open Exchange Rates exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://openexchangerates.org/api using an App ID via `app_id` query param or `Authorization: Token ...`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

OpenFIGI exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.openfigi.com with OpenAPI schema https://api.openfigi.com/schema; it is usable anonymously with lower limits or with an OpenFIGI API key sent as `X-OPENFIGI-APIKEY`.

Google OpenID Connect docs exist at developers.google.com, but no machine-readable spec or separate developer surface beyond the OIDC provider endpoints could be confirmed; auth mechanics remain unknown from the available pages.
OpenLink exposes a required-auth Virtuoso SPARQL HTTP endpoint (Digest user/pass or OAuth 1.0 via Virtuoso keys) plus OPAL surfaces including an MCP server and the OPAL chat/assistant endpoint.

OpenMeter exposes one documented public developer surface: the OpenMeter Cloud REST API, authenticated with a bearer API key; API v3 is documented but its auth mechanics and canonical spec URL are not fully pinned from the available docs.
OpenObserve exposes one documented HTTP API with Basic auth and one MCP server with OAuth-based connection; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
OpenPipe exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.openpipe.ai/api/v1 using a Bearer `Authorization` header with an OpenPipe API key.
openpolicyagent.org exposes public OPA docs plus a local `opa` CLI; I found no MCP surface, and the API authorization tutorials are examples with unknown auth rather than a documented hosted service.
OpenReplay exposes one authenticated public HTTP API, an MCP server, and a self-hosted CLI; auth is via Organization API Key for the API and browser-based login for MCP.

OpenRouter’s public developer surface includes a REST API, a separate management-key admin API, an MCP server, and Codex CLI integration; auth is via Bearer API key, management Bearer key, or OAuth PKCE-minted user key.
OpenSearch exposes one documented REST API surface and an MCP server; REST uses HTTP Basic auth, while MCP auth mechanics were not fully pinned down from the available docs.
opensource.org exposes one public HTTP API for OSI Approved Licenses at https://opensource.org/api, with no documented auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.
Open States exposes one active HTTP JSON API v3 and one deprecated GraphQL API; both use an Open States API key sent as `X-API-KEY`.
OpenStatus exposes one authenticated REST API v2, one authenticated Streamable HTTP MCP server, and an authenticated CLI; all use the same `x-openstatus-key` API token from the dashboard.
openstf.io appears to expose STF documentation/wiki and an authenticated/unknown-status web service; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed from the available docs.
openstreetmap.org exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated OpenStreetMap Editing API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
openSUSE exposes the Open Build Service HTTP API (OpenAPI YAML pointer) and the `osc` CLI; both use account credentials via HTTP Basic auth.
Open Targets exposes a public GraphQL API and two public MCP servers; no developer-issued credentials were documented.
Open Targets Platform exposes a public GraphQL API and a public MCP server; no developer-issued credentials are documented.
OpenUV exposes one authenticated REST API surface at https://api.openuv.io/api/v1 using an API key sent in `x-access-token`; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Opsgenie exposes one public REST API and the Lamp CLI; both use the same API key via `Authorization: GenieKey <apiKey>` or `Basic base64(:<apiKey>)`, and no MCP/GraphQL surface was found.
Optimizely exposes a GraphQL surface for Optimizely Graph with Bearer/OIDC, HMAC, and Basic auth; I also found an MCP server reference but could not confirm its connect endpoint or auth mechanics.

Optiversal exposes one documented developer surface: the authenticated Enrichment Retrieval HTTP API at /api/enrichment/v1, using an API key in x-api-key; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Opto 22 exposes two public developer APIs: the groov Manage REST API (API key in `apiKey` header) and the SNAP PAC REST API (HTTP Basic auth); both have Swagger/OpenAPI YAML specs.

Oracle exposes MCP surfaces for Autonomous AI Database and SQLcl, a GraphQL surface in Oracle REST Data Services, and Oracle Health Millennium/Soarian FHIR APIs requiring an Oracle Health Developer Program app.

surfaces.name: Oracle Autonomous AI Database MCP Server
Oracle Health Millennium and Soarian FHIR APIs

domains.summary: organization.read maps to the Microsoft Graph Organizations API on https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0, authenticated with Microsoft Entra ID OAuth 2.0 using Organization.Read.All.

AWS Organizations exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://organizations.us-east-1.amazonaws.com and the corresponding AWS CLI `aws organizations`, both using AWS SigV4 with normal AWS credentials.

OrgHunter exposes one public developer integration surface: a REST HTTP Charity API at https://data.orghunter.com/v1 using the OrgHunter Charity API key as `user_key` query auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
orgpolicy.googleapis.com exposes the Organization Policy Service API over HTTP and a public MCP server at https://orgpolicy.googleapis.com/mcp; HTTP auth uses Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.

OriginalVoices exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated MCP server, and one authenticated CLI; all use a user-minted API key, with the API using `Authorization: Bearer` and MCP/CLI using `X-Api-Key` or `ov auth login`/`OV_API_KEY`.
ORNL exposes two public developer APIs found here: the unauthenticated MODIS/TESViS REST web service and the public OpenEnergyHub Opendatasoft Explore API v2.1; no credentials were identified.

surfaces.name: ORNL MODIS Web Service (TESViS REST API)

domains.summary: Orthanc exposes a public documentation site plus two authenticated HTTP integration surfaces: the core REST API and the DICOMweb API, both using HTTP Basic auth from the Orthanc server config; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs I found.

Ory offers one main developer surface set: the REST API, the Ory CLI, and the Ory Docs MCP server; API access uses project/workspace API keys and OAuth2 client credentials, while the MCP server is documented at a public endpoint with auth mechanics not fully pinned.
OS Config API is a single authenticated HTTP REST surface at https://osconfig.googleapis.com/ using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
OSF exposes one documented developer surface: the OSF APIv2 HTTP API, authenticated by either an OSF Personal Access Token in `Authorization: Bearer` or OSF OAuth application credentials.

osisoft.com exposes PI Web API as the public developer integration surface; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs, and PI Web API uses authenticated HTTP access (no separate minted developer credential discovered).
Cloud OS Login API exposes one HTTP REST surface at https://oslogin.googleapis.com/ and requires a Google OAuth access token; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Oso Cloud exposes an authenticated HTTP authorization API, a CLI, and a local MCP server; all use the user’s Oso API key via `OSO_AUTH`.

GoDaddy provides an authenticated OTE REST API surface for domains and certificates plus a public MCP server for read-only domain search/availability; API access uses GoDaddy-issued API key/secret pairs.
OTOMOTO exposes one authenticated HTTP API for advert management and related operations; access is via a manually issued API key, with no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

OTOMOTO exposes one developer integration surface: a private HTTP API for business customers, authenticated with an OTOMOTO-issued API key via `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Otter.ai exposes one developer-facing surface: an OAuth2-protected REST Public API at https://api.otter.ai/v1, authenticated with an Otter API key sent as `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Outreach exposes a REST API and MCP server, both using user OAuth bearer auth; the REST API also supports limited S2S JWT-based bearer auth, while Data Sharing is documented but auth was not determined.
ovios-home.com exposes a public Shopify storefront HTTP surface and a public UCP MCP endpoint; no developer-issued credentials are documented.
Owler exposes one public developer surface: the Owler Enterprise HTTP API at https://apiv2.owler.com, documented by an OpenAPI spec and authenticated with an Owler API key.
Oxford Dictionaries exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated HTTP REST API at https://od-api.oxforddictionaries.com/api/v2, using App ID + App Key headers.

Oyster exposes a REST HTTP API on `api.oysterhr.com` and `api-sandbox.oysterhr.com`, authenticated via OAuth2 Bearer tokens or client-credentials app secrets; webhook registration is also via the REST API.
PaardPlaats exposes only its authenticated web marketplace surface; no public HTTP API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.

Paccurate exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at https://api.paccurate.io, authenticated with a Paccurate-issued API key sent as `Authorization: apikey <key>`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the public docs I found.
Packagecloud exposes one authenticated REST API at https://packagecloud.io/api/v1/ and one authenticated CLI, `package_cloud`, both using account-issued tokens/credentials.
Packagist.org exposes one public developer surface: the Packagist.org HTTP API, with authenticated endpoints using Bearer token or HTTP Basic auth.
PactFlow exposes a single public developer surface: the HAL-based PactFlow HTTP API at `https://{tenant}.pactflow.io`, authenticated with a tenant API bearer token in `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
Paddle Billing exposes a REST API and two MCP servers; the API and Paddle MCP use a Bearer Paddle API key, while the docs MCP is public.
PagerDuty exposes authenticated REST, Events v1/v2, and remote/local MCP surfaces; REST and remote MCP accept API tokens and OAuth, while Events auth remains unclear from the pages reviewed.
Mapped the Google PageSpeed Insights REST API at https://www.googleapis.com/pagespeedonline/v5; it requires a Google API key sent as `key` query parameter.

Paligo exposes one public developer surface: a Basic-authenticated REST API at https://api.paligo.net with docs at https://docs.paligo.net/en/paligo-rest-api.html; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs I found.
Paloma exposes a public marketing site and a web app at app.palomaparties.com; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was discoverable from the available docs.
PandaDoc exposes one public REST API with API key or OAuth 2.0 auth, plus a documented MCP server; no public CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
PandaScore has one developer-issued credential: a dashboard access token, used for the REST API and live WebSockets via `Authorization: Bearer` or `token=` query auth.
Pandorabots exposes one authenticated HTTP API using `user_key` + `app_id`, one public anonymous-talk HTTP API, and a CLI whose auth mechanics could not be confirmed from accessible docs.
Pane exposes one public integration surface: an MCP server at https://mcp.pane.money using Streamable HTTP/SSE, authenticated by either OAuth 2.0 or a Bearer API key.

Papaya Global exposes one public developer surface: the Workforce Payments REST API at https://api.papayaglobal.com, authenticated with a bearer access token minted from an API key + client secret; webhooks are delivery callbacks, not a separate developer surface.
Paperspace exposes authenticated HTTP APIs plus two CLIs; no public GraphQL/MCP surface was confirmed from the docs I found.

papiNet exposes a public-facing API portfolio page and an Order Status OpenAPI-based HTTP API; no public GraphQL or official CLI was found, and only a community-generated MCP server surfaced with unknown auth.

Paragon exposes a Proxy API, Users API, Task History API, a Connect SDK, and an MCP server; HTTP APIs use Paragon User Token or project API key auth, while the SDK is package-based and the MCP auth mechanics were not fully determined.

UK Parliament offers several public, unauthenticated HTTP APIs for developers—Members, Committees, Bills, Written Questions Service, Commons Votes, Lords Votes, and House of Commons Oral/Written Questions & Motions—documented from the Developer hub and most with OpenAPI specs at their swagger URLs; no auth credential is required.

surfaces.name: UK Parliament Written Questions Service API

domains.summary: Parqet exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Parqet Connect) and an MCP surface; OAuth 2.0 Connect credentials are created in the Developer Console.
Particl exposes one developer integration surface: a hosted MCP server at `https://mcp.particl.com/mcp`, authenticated with a Particl API key generated in the Particl dashboard via Enable MCP.

Particle Health exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API for patient data, documents, management, and HL7 workflows, secured by OAuth 2 client-credentials with a Bearer access token.

PassBy exposes three public developer surfaces we could confirm from the site: Almanac, Agentic, and a Feeds & API offering, but the public pages do not reveal any machine-readable API spec, MCP endpoint, CLI, or concrete auth mechanics.
Pathmode exposes one REST API and one MCP server; both use `pm_live_` API keys, with some REST endpoints also allowing a logged-in session cookie.
Patronus AI exposes a REST API, Python SDK, and MCP server; the REST API and SDK use a Patronus API key, while webhooks are documented as a separate delivery integration with unknown auth.
Payabli exposes one authenticated HTTP API, a public MCP docs server, and a Query CLI; API auth uses `requestToken` with either an API token or a user API token.

Payload CMS exposes REST, GraphQL, and Local APIs plus an MCP plugin; REST and GraphQL accept either a user JWT/session token or an API key, while Local API is in-process and MCP auth is undocumented.

Paylocity exposes public developer docs/reference and a Postman collection, plus a required OAuth 2.0 client-credentials-authenticated REST API surface at https://api.paylocity.com/api/v2; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
payments.service.gov.uk exposes one developer surface: the authenticated GOV.UK Pay HTTP API, using a GOV.UK Pay API key in the Authorization: Bearer header.
PAYONE exposes HTTP APIs for the Commerce Platform, Server API, and Client API; an MCP server is detected but its auth could not be determined from the available endpoint.

Payoneer exposes one documented developer integration surface: the PSD2/Open API REST API, authenticated with OAuth2 client app credentials via Bearer access/application tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Payoneer Open API / PSD2 REST API

domains.summary: PayPal exposes REST APIs, a remote MCP server, and REST webhooks; REST uses OAuth2 access tokens from client ID/secret, with optional PayPal-Auth-Assertion JWT for merchant-on-behalf flows.

PayPI exposes two HTTP surfaces on api.paypi.dev: a partner API authenticated with a partner API secret, and a subscriber API authenticated with a subscriber secret; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
PayRun.io exposes one authenticated REST payroll API via the developer portal; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Paystack exposes one authenticated public API (Bearer secret key) and a public MCP server for AI agents; developer docs are public.
PDF Blocks exposes one developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API with OpenAPI spec and `X-Api-Key` header auth; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

PdfBroker.io exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and one npm-based CLI wrapper; all use the same client-credentials OAuth secret pair, with MCP also accepting those creds as headers and the CLI via env vars.
PDF Generator API exposes one authenticated REST API (v4) and one hosted MCP server; both use short-lived JWTs minted from the user’s API key/secret.

Peec AI exposes one public developer API surface (Customer API, OpenAPI at https://api.peec.ai/customer/v1/openapi/json) and one MCP server (https://api.peec.ai/mcp); both use API-key/PAT auth, with MCP also supporting OAuth.
Pendo exposes the Engage REST API, Feedback REST API, a public Web SDK docs surface, and a region-specific OAuth-protected MCP server.
Google People API exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface and one authenticated MCP server, both using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
People Data Labs exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at https://api.peopledatalabs.com/v5, authenticated with a PDL API key via `api_key` query param or `X-Api-Key` header.

peoplefinderspro.com exposes one documented HTTP API, the Self Service Developer API, authenticated with an API access profile user/password via HTTP Basic; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Permit.io exposes one REST API, one GraphQL integration surface, one MCP server, and the Permit CLI; REST and CLI are auth-required, while GraphQL/MCP auth could not be pinned from public docs.

Perplexity exposes four authenticated HTTP APIs (Agent, Search, Sonar, Embeddings), an authenticated MCP server, and API-key management endpoints; auth is via Perplexity API key, with an MCP OAuth client flow available for the computer/MCP integration.
Persona exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Persona API) and one public MCP server endpoint for AI clients.

Personio exposes one public developer API surface at api.personio.de/v1 with bearer auth via either OAuth client credentials for personnel data or the recruiting access token; I found no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces on personio.com itself.
Personio exposes one public HTTP API surface (Personio Public API) with OAuth client-credentials and a separate Recruiting access token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
petprojectcofounder.ai exposes a public FastAPI HTTP surface with OpenAPI at /openapi.json and a public MCP server at /mcp; no developer credentials are required.
pgEdge exposes one documented public integration: the pgEdge Postgres MCP Server, available over MCP and HTTP/JSON-RPC, authenticated by pgEdge API tokens or user accounts.

surfaces.name: pgEdge Postgres MCP Server HTTP API

domains.summary: PhantAuth exposes one HTTP/OpenID Connect API at https://phantauth.net, authenticated with the demo client credentials shown in docs; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were identified.
PhantAuth exposes one public HTTP API surface for user/client/team/tenant/domain generation and OIDC-related use; auth is required via the demo OpenID Connect client credentials.
PhantAuth exposes one public developer surface: an OpenID Connect / random user HTTP API at https://phantauth.net, with docs on Speca; no CLI, GraphQL, or MCP surface was found.

surfaces.name: PhantAuth OpenID Connect / Random User API

domains.summary: Phantombuster exposes one documented REST API (api/v2, key auth via header or query) and one MCP server at mcp.phantombuster.com using OAuth; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
phila.gov exposes multiple public Gatekeeper HTTP APIs with no credential required; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
Phish.in exposes a public REST API v2 and public MCP server, plus a legacy API v1 that uses a bearer API key and an OAuth authorization server for client registration/login flows.
pier-luma.vercel.app exposes one public MCP surface at /mcp; no other developer-facing HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surface was confirmed for the app itself.
pier.digital exposes no discoverable public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface; only a member login portal was found, with no public integration credentials.
Pigment’s public developer surface is a workspace-specific MCP server; authentication uses the user’s Pigment workspace account via OAuth handled by supported MCP clients.
Pims exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API at https://api.pims.io, documented by the site/blog, but no public spec or auth mechanics could be confirmed.

Pinecone exposes one authenticated public REST API, one authenticated CLI, and a public docs MCP server; auth uses API keys, service-account client credentials, or user login tokens depending on the surface.
Pine Labs (pinelabs.com) exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated MCP server, and one authenticated CLI for its Online Payments product; no public GraphQL surface was found.
Pine Labs Online exposes one REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI; all require Pine Labs client credentials and the REST API uses a Bearer access token.

Pinwheel’s public developer surface is REST-only: production/development/sandbox API hosts plus the Link and Webhooks endpoints, all authenticated with a Pinwheel API secret sent as `x-api-secret`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Pipedream exposes a bearer-auth REST API, a project-scoped Connect API, an MCP server at mcp.pipedream.com, and the `pd` CLI; auth is via OAuth client-credentials or user API key for REST, OAuth client-credentials for Connect/MCP, and `pd login` for CLI.
Pipedrive exposes one public REST API surface (API v1) with OpenAPI spec and two auth options: API token or OAuth 2.0; the developer docs site is public.

Pirsch exposes a public HTTP API v1 at https://api.pirsch.io/api/v1 with Bearer auth via either an OAuth client token flow or an access key; API v2 is documented but not yet released, and no GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was confirmed.

Pixelesq exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated MCP server for Claude at https://pixelesq-mcp.pixelesq.workers.dev/mcp, using a Pixelesq account via Google or email OTP; no HTTP API, GraphQL API, or CLI surfaced in the docs.
Plaid exposes a broad HTTP API suite (including OAuth), a remote MCP dashboard server, and a Plaid CLI; all require Plaid credentials, with the dashboard MCP using a Plaid OAuth access token.
PlanetScale exposes one HTTP API, one hosted MCP server, and the `pscale` CLI; API/CLI use service tokens, while MCP uses OAuth-based auth.
Plausible exposes three public developer surfaces: the Stats API and Sites API over HTTP using Bearer API keys, the public Events API with no credential, and an MCP server referenced from the docs.

Google Play Custom App Publishing API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at playcustomapp.googleapis.com with service-account OAuth2 using the androidpublisher scope; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

surfaces.name: Google Play Custom App Publishing API

domains.summary: Google Play Developer Reporting exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP endpoint.
Google Play Integrity API exposes one HTTP REST surface at https://playintegrity.googleapis.com, authenticated via Google OAuth, API keys, or service accounts.
Plivo exposes a public MCP docs server plus HTTPS REST APIs (Account, Voice, Messaging) on api.plivo.com/v1/Account/{auth_id}/ authenticated with HTTP Basic using Auth ID + Auth Token.

Pocket Network exposes a public, keyless RPC portal and public docs; I found no confirmed public GraphQL or MCP endpoint, and the `pocketd` CLI is documented but its auth mechanics were not pinned down.
PocketSmith exposes one HTTP API at https://api.pocketsmith.com/v2 plus two MCP servers (full-access and read-only), authenticated via developer keys or OAuth for the API and OAuth-only for MCP.
Poemist exposes one public HTTP API at https://www.poemist.com/api/v1 with docs at https://poemist.github.io/poemist-apidoc/; no authenticated developer surface could be confirmed.
Polar exposes two HTTP APIs (Core and Customer Portal) authenticated by Bearer tokens, plus two public MCP servers for production and sandbox.
Policy Analyzer exposes one authenticated REST API at policyanalyzer.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
PolicyNote exposes one REST API at https://data.policynote.com/ and an MCP server at the same endpoint; both require a PolicyNote API key exchanged for a short-lived Bearer access token.
Policy Simulator exposes one public developer surface: a Google Cloud REST API at policysimulator.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
Policy Troubleshooter exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
Google Developer Knowledge exposes one REST API and one MCP server, both authenticated with a Google Cloud API key.

Polygon.io (Massive) exposes one REST API, one WebSocket API, one S3-compatible Flat Files surface, and a remote MCP server; REST/WebSocket use the same API key, Flat Files use S3 access/secret keys, and MCP uses OAuth.
Port exposes one HTTP REST API at https://api.port.io and one MCP server at https://mcp.port.io; both use Port-issued bearer auth, with clientId/clientSecret minting short-lived API tokens.
Porter exposes one public developer surface: the `porter` CLI, authenticated via a Porter account token obtained with `porter auth login` and usable in CI through `PORTER_TOKEN`.
Portfolio Optimizer exposes one public HTTP/Web API at https://api.portfoliooptimizer.io/v1; it is usable anonymously with limits, or with an API key sent in HTTP headers for higher limits.

Portkey exposes an authenticated HTTP AI Gateway API, an authenticated Admin API, an authenticated MCP Gateway (API key, OAuth, or JWT), and the Portkey CLI (uses a Portkey API key via `npx portkey`).

PostgREST exposes one REST API surface and an accompanying machine-generated OpenAPI document at the API root, both authenticated by JWT Bearer tokens, plus the `postgrest` CLI which uses the server config/database URI for startup.

PostHog exposes one developer API surface (REST/OpenAPI), one OAuth server, and one official MCP server; authentication uses project tokens for public ingestion, personal API keys for private API access and MCP, or OAuth access tokens for third-party apps.
Postmark exposes one public developer surface: the REST API at api.postmarkapp.com, authenticated with either a server API token or an account API token via the documented X-Postmark-* header.

Predibase’s public developer surface appears to be Rubrik Security Cloud GraphQL at `https://<your-tenant>/api/graphql`, authenticated with a Rubrik service-account OAuth2 client-credentials bearer token; no public REST/MCP/CLI surface was confirmed.
Prefab (now Reforge Launch) exposes a CLI (`prefab`) and HTTP SDK access via its API, both authenticated with Prefab API keys.
Prefect exposes a REST API (Cloud and self-hosted), an MCP server, and the `prefect` CLI; authentication is via Prefect API key, with MCP also supporting browser-based Prefect Cloud OAuth login.
Preply exposes one public HTTP API via OpenAPI and one public MCP endpoint; no credentials are required.

AWS Price List Service exposes a single authenticated HTTP API at https://api.pricing.us-east-1.amazonaws.com, using AWS SigV4 IAM credentials; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was identified beyond the AWS CLI as a client.
Printful exposes REST APIs at https://api.printful.com/ (v1) and https://api.printful.com/v2, both using Bearer private tokens; the developer portal login page is public.

Priority Guard exposes one public HTTP API (OpenAPI at https://priority-guard.com/openapi.json) and a public OAuth 2.0 authorization server with authorize, token, and dynamic registration endpoints; no MCP/CLI found.

surfaces.name: Priority Guardian OAuth Dynamic Client Registration

domains.summary: Prisma exposes one public MCP server (auth via Prisma Console) plus a public Prisma CLI and public GraphQL documentation; no public HTTP API surface was confirmed.
Prismic exposes public Repository API plus authenticated Content API, GraphQL API, and MCP server; developer access is primarily via @prismicio client packages.
privateca.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST API (Certificate Authority Service API) using Google OAuth/ADC Bearer auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Private MD Labs appears to expose no public developer API/MCP/CLI; only the public website/help center and an authenticated patient account area were found.
Probely (Snyk API & Web) exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated `probely` CLI, and an MCP server documented on GitHub; all use the same API key credential.

Process Street exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at api.process.st authenticated with an administrator-created API key in the Authorization Bearer header; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.

Google SAS Portal API (Testing) exposes a REST/HTTP surface at https://prod-tt-sasportal.googleapis.com, authenticated with a Google Cloud service account bearer token plus X-Goog-User-Project; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.

Product Hunt exposes a GraphQL API at https://api.producthunt.com/v2/api/graphql authenticated by Bearer access tokens from the API dashboard, plus a third-party MCP listing with undocumented connect details.
ReadMe exposes an authenticated HTTP API v2 and a public MCP docs server; no CLI or GraphQL integration surface was confirmed from the available docs.
Profit.co exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API at `https://api.profit.co/app/dao/v6` documented on `profit.dev`, authenticated by `apiKey` + `accessKey` query parameters.

Promptfoo exposes an enterprise HTTP API, an MCP server, and the `promptfoo` CLI; all documented integrations use a Promptfoo API key, obtainable via `promptfoo auth login` or the account/API settings.
PromptLayer exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated workspace MCP server, one public docs MCP server, and Python/JavaScript SDKs; auth is via PromptLayer API key.

PromptPerfect exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.promptperfect.jina.ai, backed by https://promptperfect.xyz/openapi.json, authenticated with a secret API key sent in `x-api-key`; the marketing site is public.
OpenAI exposes one authenticated HTTP API and a docs-described MCP/connectors integration surface; no public CLI or separate GraphQL surface was confirmed.

ProofX exposes one public MCP surface at https://proofx-mcp.fly.dev/mcp (streamable HTTP, no auth required); OAuth 2.0 credentials are available via dynamic client registration for protected flows with scopes protect and verify.
ProofX exposes public verification plus authenticated web, Voice ID, and iOS app surfaces; no API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI docs were found on proofx.co.uk.
PropelAuth exposes a backend HTTP API, OAuth2/MCP auth endpoints, and a CLI; backend API and CLI use PropelAuth-issued API keys, while OAuth2/MCP use OAuth client credentials/PKCE flows.
Property24 exposes an OAuth/OpenID Connect integration surface at its /connect authorize/token endpoints; no public APIs, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI were found.
Prove’s public developer surface is a restricted HTTP API documentation portal at developer.prove.com, gated by a portal access code; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Mapped the Google Proximity Beacon API as a single authenticated HTTP surface using Google OAuth bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
PRSS public site is unauthenticated; ContentDepot is a developer-facing portal surface but its auth mechanics could not be determined from public pages.

surfaces.name: PRSS website / NPR Distribution marketing and support pages

domains.summary: PlanetScale exposes one HTTP API and two hosted MCP endpoints; HTTP API supports service tokens or OAuth, and both MCP servers use OAuth.
PTV exposes one public developer HTTP surface: the PTV Timetable API v3, authenticated by `devid` plus `signature` query parameters; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
publicca.googleapis.com exposes one Google Cloud REST API and the matching `gcloud beta publicca` CLI; both use Google user OAuth / ADC credentials.
pubmed.gov exposes NCBI E-utilities for PubMed access, the NCBI Datasets v2 REST API, and the Entrez Direct CLI; all use an NCBI API key.

Cloud Pub/Sub exposes an authenticated REST API at https://pubsub.googleapis.com and a public MCP server at https://pubsub.googleapis.com/mcp; developers authenticate the API with Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens (via Google Cloud auth flows / gcloud or ADC).
Pub/Sub Lite exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated REST API at pubsublite.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
PulseMCP exposes a public REST API documented at /api/docs/v0.1, but I could not determine any authentication requirements from the available pages.

Pulumi exposes a Pulumi Cloud REST API, a CLI-backed REST interface via `pulumi api`, and an MCP server; auth is via Pulumi access token or `pulumi login` for CLI-backed access, while the MCP endpoint details were not pinned.

surfaces.name: Pulumi Cloud REST API via pulumi api

domains.summary: Pusher Channels exposes one documented HTTP API and a CLI; HTTP API auth uses app credentials (app_id/key/secret/cluster), while CLI auth mechanics couldn’t be pinned from the docs.
PyPI exposes public Index, JSON, RSS, Stats, Integrity, and XML-RPC HTTP APIs, plus a required-auth Upload API (API token or Trusted Publisher OIDC) and an unknown-auth secret reporting API.

Pyxl exposes one documented developer surface: an OAuth-protected MCP server at https://www.pyxl.pro/api/mcp using Pyxl OAuth (authorize/token/register endpoints) with scopes images:read, images:write, and user:read; no separate public HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Qase exposes a REST TestOps API, an MCP server, and the qasectl CLI; all require a Qase API token, with the CLI also supporting `qasectl login`/`QASE_API_TOKEN`.
Qdrant exposes a required-auth REST API at https://api.qdrant.tech, plus a public MCP server at https://api.qdrant.tech/_mcp/server; auth is via Qdrant Cloud API key in the `api-key` header.
Qodo exposes a public docs site, an authenticated Qodo portal, and an authenticated Context Engine MCP surface; no public REST/GraphQL API or CLI was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
Qovery exposes one public REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI; all require a Qovery-issued token or CLI login, with no GraphQL surface found.
QR Code Maker exposes one documented HTTP surface for its Zapier API, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
qrc-chatgpt-app.vercel.app exposes a public MCP server at https://qrc-chatgpt-app.vercel.app/mcp with no additional developer credentials detected.
qstash.dev exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API (QStash), one authenticated MCP server via Upstash credentials, and a public QStash CLI for local development.
Qualpay exposes a single developer-facing REST Payment Gateway API with an OpenAPI-backed reference portal; it requires a Qualpay security key passed as Bearer auth in the Authorization header.
Qualtrics exposes a public HTTP API and an MCP server; both use a Qualtrics API token, and I found no confirmed GraphQL API or CLI surface.
Boomi Data Hub exposes two HTTP surfaces: the Repository API (including Quarantine) using JWT bearer auth, and the Platform API using Basic Auth.

surfaces.name: Boomi Data Hub Repository API (Quarantine collection)

domains.summary: Quartr exposes one developer HTTP API (API key via `x-api-key`) and one MCP server (OAuth via MCP client sign-in); no public GraphQL or CLI surfaced.
Quay.io exposes one public REST API at https://quay.io/api/v1/ plus docker registry login, authenticated by OAuth access tokens or deprecated access tokens; no MCP or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
QuickBlox’s public developer surface is the REST Server API at https://api.quickblox.com, authenticated via API key or session token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

QuickChart exposes one public HTTP API at https://quickchart.io with OpenAPI at https://quickchart.io/openapi.json; it accepts an optional API key via query `key` or `Authorization: Bearer`, and provides chart, QR, validation, short-URL/template, and natural-config endpoints.

Quicknode exposes a public Agent API and paid Admin API over HTTP, a remote OAuth-protected MCP server, and a public Quicknode Skills CLI; core RPC access also supports API-key and wallet-based x402/MPP payment auth.
Quizlet exposes a public llms.txt page and an OAuth-based developer API surface requiring Quizlet OAuth app credentials; no additional public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Quo exposes one authenticated REST API (API key in `Authorization`) and one authenticated remote MCP server at `https://mcp.quo.com/mcp`; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced in the docs reviewed.
QuoteCraft AI exposes one public MCP server at https://quotecraft-ai-zeta.vercel.app/mcp; no other developer surfaces were found.

quotes.rest exposes the They Said So Quotes REST API (OpenAPI YAML at quotes.rest/yaml/theysaidso.quotes.openapi.yaml?v5.1) with Bearer API-key auth; the quote-of-the-day routes are documented as public, but the site also says public access now requires an API token.
Rabbu exposes a public MCP server at https://rabbu.com/mcp; no other developer surfaces were confirmed.
Ragie exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated CLI, and per-partition authenticated streamable-http MCP servers, all using a Bearer API key.
Railway exposes a GraphQL Public API, a hosted MCP server, and the Railway CLI; authentication uses a user access token or project token for the API/CLI, and OAuth-based login for remote MCP.

Ramp exposes one authenticated Developer API (OpenAPI at https://ramp.com/openapi.json), a CLI (`ramp auth login`), an unauthenticated Developer MCP, and a separate Ramp MCP with auth details not fully pinned from the docs read.

Random Lovecraft exposes one public HTTP API at https://randomlovecraft.com/api with OpenAPI spec https://randomlovecraft.com/openapi.yaml; no auth is required and no other integration surfaces were found.

Randommer.io exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://randommer.io/api with OpenAPI spec https://randommer.io/api/docs/v1/swagger.json, using a single API key sent in the `X-Api-Key` header.
randomuser.me exposes one public HTTP API at https://randomuser.me/api/ and no documented auth credentials, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces.
Ranked.ai exposes one developer REST API and one MCP server; both require authentication, via API key for REST and OAuth 2.0 for MCP.
RapidAPI exposes a hosted HTTP API surface and a GraphQL API surface, both authenticated with a RapidAPI app key via `X-RapidAPI-Key`; no MCP server or CLI was confirmed from the discovered docs.

Raptive exposes one public developer surface we could confirm: the browser-based Identity API at `window.adthrive.identityApi`, with no credential required; no REST/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surfaces were found from public docs.
Rapyd exposes a documented Merchant HTTP API at https://api.rapyd.net, authenticated with Rapyd API keys via the Authorization Bearer header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
RavenPack’s developer surface is Bigdata.com: a REST API, MCP server, and Bigdata CLI, all authenticated with a Bigdata API key.
raw.githubusercontent.com appears to be a public raw-content surface with no distinct developer auth; GitHub’s authenticated REST API is separate.
RAWG exposes one public developer surface: the RAWG Video Games Database HTTP API at `https://api.rawg.io/api`, authenticated with a required RAWG API key passed as `key` query parameter.
Razorpay exposes a REST API, a remote/local MCP server, and a CLI; REST and local MCP use API key basic auth, while remote MCP also supports OAuth 2.0 read_only.
Request Baskets exposes one REST API and one public request-collection HTTP surface; auth uses a master token for service management and per-basket tokens for basket-specific management.
Read AI exposes one public REST API and one remote MCP server, both authenticated with OAuth 2.1 access tokens from a dynamic client registration flow; no CLI or GraphQL surface was identified.
Google Reader Revenue Subscription Linking exposes one authenticated REST API surface; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
ReadMe exposes two MCP servers and the `rdme` CLI; auth uses a ReadMe API key, private-project site password, or `rdme login` for the CLI.

ReadMe exposes an MCP server at https://docs.readme.com/mcp authenticated with a ReadMe API key (`rdme_`); public docs also mention HTTP and GraphQL API reference surfaces, but their concrete API auth/spec details were not fully determined from the pages read.
Readwise exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the original Readwise API and the Reader API, both using a personal access token in `Authorization: Token XXX`.
Reagent Travel publicly संकेतs custom integrations via an HTTP API and MCP server, but exposes no public spec or self-serve auth details; developer access appears to be request-only through contact.
realestate.com.au exposes multiple partner HTTP APIs under REA Partner Platform, all authenticated via OAuth 2.0; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: REA Partner Platform Listing Upload API

domains.summary: RealEstateAPI exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface plus two MCP servers (production auth-required, developer public); the only discovered developer credential is an API token sent as `x-api-key`.
realtimebidding.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface, the Google Authorized Buyers Real-time Bidding API, using Google OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens.
realtor.com exposes a Lead Delivery HTTP API for Connections Plus lead delivery; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the available docs.

Rebilly exposes one public developer API surface (Core API) plus an MCP server; both require auth, with secret API key+organization ID, publishable key, or JWT for the HTTP API, and OAuth-backed JWT-style access for MCP.
reCAPTCHA Enterprise exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://recaptchaenterprise.googleapis.com, using Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
Reclaim.ai exposes a documented developer API key used for an unofficial MCP server; no public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surface could be confirmed from available docs.
Google Cloud Recommender exposes a REST API at `https://recommender.googleapis.com/v1` and a `gcloud recommender` CLI; both use Google Cloud OAuth/ADC credentials.
Google Cloud Recommender exposes one authenticated HTTP API over Google OAuth 2.0 and one public MCP server at https://recommender.googleapis.com/mcp.
Recorrido.cl exposes a public booking/search website and a public help center; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Recurly exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: v3 JSON API and v2 XML API (OpenAPI YAML for v2.3); no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
redBus exposes a partner-facing redBus Plus web login at https://plus.redbus.com/; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Voyado (redeal.io) exposes one public developer surface: the Voyado Engage HTTP API at tenant base URLs, authenticated with an API key sent in the `apikey` request header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
redirection.io exposes a Bearer-authenticated public HTTP API and an OAuth-protected MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Redis exposes two public HTTP APIs (Redis Cloud REST API and Redis Software REST API), plus the Redis MCP server and redisctl CLI; Cloud REST uses `x-api-key` + `x-api-secret-key`, Software REST uses HTTP Basic Auth.
Memorystore for Redis exposes a public MCP server and a Google-authenticated REST API; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
Redocly offers a public Docs MCP server at https://redocly.com/mcp, with optional OAuth-authenticated access via MCP OAuth client registration; Redocly CLI is available as the `redocly` command.
Redox exposes three developer surfaces: Redox Data Model API, Redox FHIR API, and Redox Platform API; plus an MCP server with undocumented auth mechanics.
Refiner exposes one documented developer surface: a REST API at `https://api.refiner.io/v1` authenticated by personal API key via Bearer header, query param, or HTTP Basic.
Refinitiv/LSEG exposes the Refinitiv Data Platform API catalog and a logged-in API Playground; no public machine-readable spec was confirmed from the pages reviewed.
Refuge Restrooms exposes one public HTTP API with OpenAPI spec at /api/swagger_doc.json and no authentication required.
RegCheck exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface (SOAP/ASMX plus REST/JSON wrapper) using HTTP Basic Auth with username and API key/password; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs reviewed.
regmovies.com exposes one developer HTTP API surface via the Regal Developer Portal, authenticated with a portal-issued API subscription key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Regulations.gov exposes one public developer surface: the Regulations.gov REST API at https://api.regulations.gov/v4, authenticated with an api.data.gov API key in `X-Api-Key`.

Reliance General / IndusInd General exposes a policy-services HTTP surface at https://rzonews.reliancegeneral.co.in/API; I found no confirmed public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface or documented integration auth, so auth remains unknown.

surfaces.name: Reliance General / IndusInd General insurance policy services API

domains.summary: Remitly exposes a public website only; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
remove.bg exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one CLI, both using the user-minted remove.bg API key.
Render exposes one public REST API, one hosted MCP server, and the Render CLI; all require a Render API key for authentication.
RentCast exposes one public developer API surface (REST/HTTP) plus an MCP server; both use a single API key sent in the `X-Api-Key` header, and the API docs/AI-tools pages are public.
Replicate exposes a Bearer-token-authenticated HTTP API and a hosted SSE MCP server, plus a public llms.txt; no separate GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Replit MCP Server at https://replit-mcp.com/server/mcp uses OAuth 2.1/PKCE via Replit OIDC; no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.
Replit’s public developer surface is its beta MCP server plus Replit Auth; no separate public REST/GraphQL API or CLI was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
Microsoft Graph Reports API is a protected HTTP surface at `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0`, authenticated with a Microsoft Entra app registration and a Microsoft Graph bearer access token.
Requesty exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (inference and management) plus a CLI integration for Claude Code; no separate public GraphQL or MCP connect endpoint was confirmed from the docs.

Research Solutions exposes one confirmed public integration surface: the Article Galaxy MCP at https://mcp.articlegalaxy.com/ag/mcp, which requires an Article Galaxy account; Scite MCP is mentioned in terms but its connect endpoint and auth mechanics were not confirmed.

Google Workspace Reseller API is the only public integration surface found on reseller.googleapis.com: HTTP REST at https://reseller.googleapis.com/reseller/v1, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 user credentials.
Resend exposes a REST HTTP API (OpenAPI), an MCP server, and a CLI; all authenticated developer surfaces use either a Resend API key or OAuth client registration flow.
Resolume exposes local HTTP APIs for Arena/Avenue and Wire plus local MCP servers for Arena and Wire; no separate developer-issued credentials were found beyond enabling the built-in webserver.

surfaces.name: Resolume Arena MCP Server
surfaces.url: local Resolume Arena MCP server bundled with the app

surfaces.name: Resolume Wire MCP Server
surfaces.url: local Resolume Wire MCP server bundled with the app

domains.summary: Responsive exposes a REST API authenticated with user-issued API keys and an MCP server authenticated via user-delegated OAuth; no GraphQL or CLI surface was found.

Restate exposes a public docs MCP server, a CLI, and its self-hosted/server HTTP ingress/admin surface; auth details are documented for CLI tokens and request-identity keys, while server HTTP auth could not be fully determined from the pages read.

retail.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Vertex AI Search for commerce API / REST v2) using Google OAuth2 or service-account bearer tokens; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

surfaces.name: Vertex AI Search for commerce API

domains.summary: Retell AI exposes a Bearer-authenticated REST API, a remote MCP server over Streamable HTTP using the Retell API key, and a public-key-backed chat widget surface.

Rev AI exposes two public speech-to-text HTTP APIs (asynchronous and streaming, including RTMP ingest) authenticated with a Rev AI access token; legacy Rev.com API docs exist but its auth surface is unclear.
Reverb exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.reverb.com/api using a Personal Access Token in Authorization: Bearer; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaced.
Reverb exposes one developer integration surface: the Reverb API HTTP endpoint at https://api.reverb.com/api, authenticated with a personal access token sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
ReversingLabs exposes the Spectra Intelligence REST API (HTTP Basic auth) and a Spectra Intelligence MCP server; no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.
Rightmove.co.uk exposes a public site plus an authenticated Apify-hosted Rightmove Scraper via HTTP API, MCP, and CLI, all using an Apify API token.

Rillet exposes a Bearer-token HTTP API at https://api.rillet.com and a remote MCP server at https://api.rillet.com/mcp; both use the same Rillet API key, and the HTTP API also recommends X-Rillet-API-Version: 3.

Rippling exposes a required-auth REST API and an MCP server; both use a Rippling API token, with the REST API sending it as `Authorization: Bearer` and the MCP server reading it from `RIPPLING_API_TOKEN`.

RiteKit exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.ritekit.com/v1 authenticated by a developer Client ID in `client_id`, plus a Python CLI client `ritetag-api` that uses an access token via `-t` or `ACCESS_TOKEN`; no GraphQL or MCP surface was found.
Robohash.org exposes one public HTTP image-generation surface at https://robohash.org with no developer auth; no API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surfaces were found.
Rome2Rio exposes a partner-gated HTTP API with downloadable API credentials via its get-listed/self-serve integration flow; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Rootly exposes one REST API, an official CLI, and an MCP server; all support auth via Rootly API key or OAuth, with the MCP server also supporting OAuth 2.0.
Routific exposes one public developer surface: the Routific Engine HTTP API at https://api.routific.com/v1, authenticated with a user-minted API key passed as `Authorization: bearer <token>`.

Royal Mail exposes one clearly documented developer API surface: the Click & Drop / Royal Mail Public API (REST/OpenAPI) at `https://api.parcel.royalmail.com/api/v1`, authenticated by a Click & Drop API key in the `Authorization` header; the main developer portal also lists additional APIs but the public auth details weren't fully determined from the pages reviewed.

RubyGems.org has public Compact Index and API V2 HTTP surfaces, an authenticated HTTP API, and the `gem` CLI; credentials are RubyGems API keys, OTP MFA codes, and Trusted Publishing OIDC for GitHub Actions.
Rudder exposes one public developer surface: a REST API authenticated with `X-API-Token` API keys; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.

RudderStack exposes four public developer surfaces we recorded: HTTP APIs (HTTP API, Profiles API, Data Catalog API, Tracking Plan API) authenticated via either HTTP Basic auth or a workspace Service Access Token as Bearer auth, a Rudder CLI that uses the Service Access Token via login/env, and a Rudder MCP server whose auth mechanics were not clear from the docs.
Cloud Run exposes a Google-authenticated Admin API over HTTP and a public MCP server at https://run.googleapis.com/mcp; no CLI or GraphQL surface identified.
Runlayer exposes a tenant-scoped Management API plus MCP surfaces (Runlayer MCP and Runlayer Plugin), all authenticated by API keys or Agent Account OAuth tokens.
Runloop exposes a bearer-token REST API, an `rli` CLI using `RUNLOOP_API_KEY`, and an MCP/AI-tools integration; I could not confirm a machine-readable OpenAPI spec URL from the docs in this run.

Orion exposes one public developer surface: a remote MCP server at https://g.runorion.com/mcp, authenticated with an Orion account via browser-based MCP OAuth; no HTTP/GraphQL API or CLI was confirmed in public docs.

Runpod exposes an authenticated REST API at https://rest.runpod.io/v1 (API key via `Authorization: Bearer ...`), an authenticated GraphQL API at https://api.runpod.io/graphql (API key via `api_key` query param), an authenticated Runpod API MCP server (uses the same API key), a public docs MCP server, plus two CLIs: `flash` (login with Runpod API key or `RUNPOD_API_KEY`) and `runpodctl` (configure with API key via `runpodctl doctor` or `runpodctl config --apiKey`).
RunReady exposes a public MCP server at https://runready.dev/mcp and no other developer surfaces were confirmed.
Runscope exposes one developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API for API Monitoring; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Google Cloud Runtime Config exposes one authenticated REST API and a gcloud CLI surface; both use Google OAuth credentials (user login or ADC), with no public or MCP/GraphQL surface found.
runZero exposes one authenticated HTTP API, plus an MCP server and CLI whose auth mechanics could not be pinned from the pages read.

Rutter exposes one public REST API and the Rutter Link JS/React SDK; REST auth uses Basic client credentials, optionally plus per-connection access_token, and Link uses a public_key to start merchant auth.

sadhguru.org exposes 3 documented HTTP integration surfaces: Global PRS API, ISO Website integration API, and MyMedic integration API; all require auth (Bearer + x-system-token for Global PRS, OAuth2 for ISO Website and MyMedic), with no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

Google Safe Browsing exposes two HTTP API surfaces: deprecated v4 at `https://safebrowsing.googleapis.com/v4` using a Google API key query param, and v5 at `https://safebrowsing.googleapis.com/v5` with auth not fully determined from docs.

Synapse (Sage Bionetworks) exposes one public developer surface: the Synapse REST API at https://rest-docs.synapse.org/rest/ with OpenAPI spec https://rest-docs.synapse.org/openapi/openapispecification.json, authenticated by either a Synapse Personal Access Token in Authorization: Bearer or a verified Synapse OAuth 2.0 client via OIDC discovery.
Sakari exposes one public developer surface: an OAuth2-protected REST API at https://api.sakari.io; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
Saleor exposes one authenticated GraphQL API and a public MCP workflow using mcp-graphql with the Saleor GraphQL schema.
SalesEQ exposes only its web app; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Salesforce exposes a REST API, GraphQL API, Salesforce CLI, and hosted MCP servers; REST/GraphQL/CLI authenticate via Salesforce user/org login, while hosted MCP auth could not be determined from the available docs.
