Salesloft exposes one public REST API surface and frontend integrations; both use OAuth, with API-key auth also supported for the REST API.
SambaNova exposes one public inference HTTP API at https://api.sambanova.ai/v1 plus CLI integrations for Claude Code and Cline; all use a SambaNova API key.
SAMRUM exposes one public integration surface: a remote MCP server at https://mcp.samrum.io/mcp using OAuth 2.1/PKCE via an active SAMRUM account.
Sanity exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated GraphQL API, a hosted MCP server, and the Sanity CLI; auth is via Sanity account login/OAuth and project API tokens.

sap.com exposes the SAP BTP CLI, SAP Integration Suite API Management public APIs, and an ABAP ADT MCP server; BTP CLI uses SAP platform-user login, while API Management programmatic access uses a service key.

surfaces.name: SAP Integration Suite API Management public APIs

domains.summary: sasportal.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface: the SAS Portal API at https://sasportal.googleapis.com/v1alpha1, using Google OAuth Bearer auth; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.
Savecraft exposes a hosted MCP server at https://mcp.savecraft.gg; I found no confirmed public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surface beyond the web app and docs.

Scalar exposes a public Docs MCP at https://scalar.com/mcp and an authenticated Installation MCP at Scalar-provided installation URLs, plus public docs/product HTTP pages; authentication for Installation MCP uses a Scalar personal access token.

Scalekit exposes a REST API, an AgentKit API reference, an MCP server, and a CLI; REST auth uses dashboard-minted OAuth client credentials, while the MCP server and CLI auth mechanics were not fully pinned from the docs.

Scalingo exposes a v1 REST API, a separate authentication API, a database API, an addon-provider API, and a CLI; all programmatic surfaces are authenticated, mostly via Bearer tokens exchanged from a Scalingo API token, while the addon-provider API uses HTTP Basic.
SchoolDigger exposes one authenticated REST API surface at https://api.schooldigger.com/v2.3 using appID + appKey query parameters; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

scideas.net exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Perfectpdf and Regression, both API-key-in-body) and two public HTTP demos/services (Islington solar estimator and New North Road Traffic); no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
SciSpace exposes a signed-in web app and enterprise workspace, but no public REST/GraphQL/MCP/CLI developer surface or machine-readable API spec was found.

Scite exposes one public integration surface for developers/agents: an MCP server at https://api.scite.ai/mcp authenticated with a Scite account via OAuth discovery; no public REST, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
Scrapfly exposes one authenticated HTTP Web Scraping API, a hosted MCP server with API key or OAuth auth, and a CLI with auth unknown from available docs.
ScrapingBee exposes an authenticated HTTP API (HTML API plus Google API), an authenticated remote MCP server, and an authenticated CLI; all use a single ScrapingBee API key.
The domain itself exposes no public integration surface; the only relevant API found is ScreenshotOne’s external screenshot API, which uses `access_key` plus optional `signature` auth.
Scrimba exposes a public MCP server at https://scrimba.com/mcp with no authentication; no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI integration surfaces were confirmed.

Search Ads 360 exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the legacy Search Ads 360 API v2 and the newer Search Ads 360 Reporting API v0, both using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
SearchAPI exposes one documented REST API surface secured by an API key, plus an MCP integration page whose connection auth could not be confirmed.
Google Search Console exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://www.googleapis.com/webmasters/v3 and one public MCP server at https://searchconsole.googleapis.com/mcp.
SeatPin exposes one developer integration surface: an OAuth-protected HTTP API surface backed by dynamic OAuth client registration; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
sebaonline.org exposes a public website and public results pages; the site also links to authenticated SEBA online services, but no API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was found.
Google Cloud Secret Manager exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://secretmanager.googleapis.com/v1, using Google OAuth/ADC Bearer tokens with IAM authorization.
Security Command Center exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: the v2 Security Command Center API and the v1 Security Center Management API, both using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer auth.
Seekist exposes one public MCP server at https://seekist.ai/mcp; no developer-issued credentials or additional API/GraphQL/CLI surfaces were found.

Segment exposes public client/server analytics integration surfaces (Analytics.js, HTTP API, Node.js, Ruby) authenticated with a per-source Write Key; no GraphQL, MCP, or standalone admin API surface was confirmed.
Spacelift exposes an MCP server and the `spacectl` CLI; no public HTTP/GraphQL API surface was confirmed from the docs found.
SelectPdf exposes three developer HTTP APIs: HTML to PDF, PDF Merge, and PDF to Text; all require a SelectPdf API key, while the self-hosted .NET library is out of scope.
Semaphore exposes one public HTTP API, one hosted MCP server, and two CLIs (`sem` and `sem-ai`), all authenticated via Semaphore API token; hosted MCP also supports OAuth 2.1.
Semrush exposes one HTTP API and one MCP surface; both use a Semrush API key, and the HTTP API also supports OAuth 2.0.
Send exposes one developer surface: an OAuth2-protected MCP server at https://send.co/mcp; no public HTTP or GraphQL API was confirmed from the discovered docs.

Sendbird’s public developer surface is its Chat Platform API plus Chat SDKs/UIKit and Desk Platform API, all authenticated with a Sendbird API token from the Dashboard (`Settings > Application > General > API tokens`), sent as `Api-Token` for HTTP/API calls.
SendGrid exposes the authenticated v3 REST API and SMTP sending; API access uses SendGrid API keys and SMTP uses SendGrid SMTP credentials.
Sentry exposes a public web API, OAuth authorize/token endpoints, an MCP server, and the Sentry CLI; API and CLI support bearer tokens and OAuth, while MCP uses OAuth2.
Sentry exposes a REST API, an OAuth2-protected MCP server, and the sentry-cli CLI; auth is via Bearer auth tokens, OAuth2 app tokens, or CLI login/token config.

Sentry Software exposes a Monitoring Studio X REST API (spec not located) and a Hardware Sentry CLI plus OTLP gRPC receiver; the receiver uses basic auth, while the Monitoring Studio API auth could not be determined.
SEON exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one authenticated MCP surface; both use a SEON API key via the Authorization: Bearer header.
Sephora exposes a restricted developer portal with HTTP APIs only; access is via per-app API key/secret from the developer network, and no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Sequel.io exposes one developer HTTP API surface and one MCP server, both requiring a Sequel admin account; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
SerpApi exposes one public HTTP search API, a hosted MCP server, and a CLI; all use a SerpApi API key.
servicebroker.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface, the Google Cloud Service Broker API, using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens.
Mapped the Google Service Consumer Management API as a single authenticated HTTP surface using Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
servicecontrol.googleapis.com exposes the Google Cloud Service Control REST API at servicecontrol.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth2 Bearer access tokens.

Service Directory exposes one public developer surface: the Google Cloud Service Directory v1 REST API at servicedirectory.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 / Google account credentials.

servicemanagement.googleapis.com exposes the Google Cloud Service Management API over HTTP REST at https://servicemanagement.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with Google OAuth bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

Google Cloud Service Networking exposes one HTTP/REST API at servicenetworking.googleapis.com, authenticated with a Google Cloud OAuth Bearer access token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.

Service Usage exposes two authenticated HTTP surfaces: REST API v1 and v1beta1 on serviceusage.googleapis.com, both using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Setu exposes three developer HTTP APIs found here: UPI Setu, AA Gateway, and Insights; UPI Setu and Insights use credentials from Setu/Bridge, while AA Gateway auth could not be fully determined from the public docs.

share.eu exposes a public storefront browsing HTTP surface and a public UCP/MCP commerce surface; no separate authenticated developer API, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed from the available docs.

SharePoint.com’s public developer surface centers on Microsoft Graph REST API (authenticated via Microsoft Entra OAuth bearer tokens) plus public SharePoint dev/SPFx documentation; no separate MCP server or SharePoint-specific CLI integration surfaced.
Shazam exposes a public, unauthenticated website surface and a developer-facing ShazamKit integration page; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found from the available docs.
SheerSEO exposes one public developer integration surface: an HTTP SEO API for rank tracking/SERPs, authenticated by a SheerSEO-issued API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

Sheetlabs exposes public per-API REST endpoints for reading/writing spreadsheet-backed APIs, plus a separate authenticated SQL query HTTP endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the public docs.

Google Sheets exposes one REST API surface at sheets.googleapis.com/v4 and an MCP server page; the API uses OAuth2 bearer auth via Google Cloud credentials, while the MCP details could not be confirmed from accessible docs.
Shields.io exposes a public badge-rendering HTTP API at https://img.shields.io with no developer auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
ShipEngine exposes one documented developer surface: the REST API at https://api.shipengine.com/v1, authenticated with a ShipEngine API key sent in the `API-Key` header.

Shippo’s public developer surface includes the REST API, hosted/local MCP servers, and Shipping Elements; credentials are Shippo API tokens, OAuth bearer tokens, and short-lived Shipping Elements JWTs.
ShipStation exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (v2 and legacy v1, both using `API-Key` headers) and one public MCP docs server at `https://docs.shipstation.com/mcp`.
Shipt exposes a single documented public developer surface: the Shipt Platform HTTP API, authenticated with partner-issued OAuth2 password-grant credentials exchanged for Bearer JWT access tokens.
shop-pro.jp exposes one authenticated shop API, one authenticated CLI (`colorme`), and one authenticated App Store API; no GraphQL or MCP surface was confirmed from the available docs.

surfaces.name: カラーミーショップ CLI
カラーミーショップ API docs

domains.summary: Shopify exposes a public MCP server at `https://shop.app/mcp`, plus authenticated GraphQL Admin and Storefront APIs; Storefront API also supports tokenless public access for some queries.

Shopee Open Platform exposes one developer HTTP API surface on the Open Platform portal, authenticated via partner app credentials plus shop authorization tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Shopify exposes the GraphQL Admin API, REST Admin API, Storefront GraphQL API, and Shopify CLI; Admin APIs require a Shopify access token, while the Storefront API supports tokenless public access.
Shopify.dev exposes the GraphQL Admin API, Storefront GraphQL API, Shopify CLI, and a public Shopify.dev MCP server; auth is via Shopify access tokens, Storefront tokens, or `shopify login` for CLI.
Merchant API exposes a public MCP server at https://shoppingcontent.googleapis.com/mcp and an authenticated REST API on shoppingcontent.googleapis.com using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.
Shortcut exposes a REST API v3 and a hosted MCP server; both use a workspace-specific Shortcut API token.
Shorten.rest exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://api.shorten.rest, using an API key sent in the `x-api-key` header.

Shotstack exposes two authenticated REST API surfaces: sandbox at https://api.shotstack.io/stage and production v1 at https://api.shotstack.io/v1, both using an `x-api-key` header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed from the docs.
Shotstack exposes one public REST API surface family (Edit, Serve, Ingest), plus a streamable HTTP MCP server and a CLI; API access uses Shotstack API keys.

Shufti Pro exposes one authenticated REST API at api.shuftipro.com and an OAuth2-protected MCP server at shuftipro.com/mcp; auth supports Basic client ID/secret or Bearer OAuth tokens, with OAuth client registration via ai.shuftiapps.com.
Shutterstock exposes one HTTP API and one CLI, both requiring an app-issued client ID/secret for basic auth or an OAuth access token; all requests also require a User-Agent header.

Sigma exposes a REST API requiring Sigma-issued API client credentials and bearer access tokens, plus an MCP server using organization OAuth; Sigma CLI is documented but its auth mechanics were not pinned down.

SignalWire exposes one authenticated REST API surface, client-side token endpoints for browser/video use, an Agents SDK CLI, and an MCP Gateway documented in the SDK; public docs show Basic Auth with Project ID + API Token for REST, plus bearer room/subscriber tokens for client surfaces.
Signicat exposes multiple authenticated REST APIs under developer.signicat.com; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
SIGNL4 exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated REST API v3 using an API key in `X-S4-Api-Key`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

SignNow exposes one public REST API at https://api.signnow.com, a hosted MCP server at https://mcp-server.signnow.com/mcp, and SDK/tooling; REST auth supports API key bearer auth or OAuth2 via basic token + bearer access token.
SigNoz exposes a Cloud OTLP ingestion HTTP surface and a hosted MCP server; ingestion uses `signoz-ingestion-key` or basic auth, and MCP uses OAuth 2.1 on the regional hosted endpoint.
SignWell exposes one authenticated HTTP API, plus authenticated MCP and CLI surfaces; all use the user-minted SignWell API token.

Simcardo exposes a public MCP server at https://simcardo.com/mcp and public read-only HTTP endpoints surfaced via llms.txt for the Connectivity Planner, plus public RSS/feed endpoints; no developer-issued credentials were confirmed.
Similarweb exposes a REST API and Batch API secured by a Similarweb API key, plus an MCP server at https://mcp.similarweb.com; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Simple Analytics exposes three HTTP APIs: Stats API, Export API, and Admin API; Stats and Export are public for public websites, while Stats/Admin can use an `Api-Key` and Admin also requires `User-Id`.
SimplyRETS exposes one public developer surface: a Basic-auth REST API at https://api.simplyrets.com/ with docs at https://docs.simplyrets.com/.
Sinao exposes one public HTTP API at https://api.sinao.app/v1 with OpenAPI spec https://api.sinao.app/v1/swagger.yaml; it accepts either a Bearer token or an Api-Key header.

Sinao exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.sinao.app/v1 with OpenAPI spec https://api.sinao.app/v1/swagger.yaml; it accepts either a user Bearer token or an admin-created API key, and no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.

Mapped Sinch’s public developer integration surface: SMS REST API, Conversation API, Voice API, Verification API, and Sinch CLI; discovered Sinch documentation MCP endpoint at https://developers.sinch.com/mcp from docs, and recorded auth for SMS (Bearer API token), Conversation (OAuth2), and CLI (project access keys).
M-Files exposes one developer integration surface here: the MFWS REST API, authenticated with either a pre-shared key or OAuth 2.0.

Google Site Verification API: one authenticated HTTP surface at https://www.googleapis.com/siteVerification/v1 using Google OAuth 2.0 user consent with scopes siteverification or siteverification.verify_only.

Sketch exposes a public local MCP server, plus a licensed local CLI (sketchtool) and Sketch JavaScript API for plugins/scripts; no separate cloud HTTP/GraphQL public API surface was confirmed from the developer docs.

SketchUp exposes public developer docs plus two integration surfaces: the in-product SketchUp Ruby API (public, no credential) and Extension Warehouse web flows that require a Trimble ID session; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Fortnite REST API at `https://skynewz-api-fortnite.herokuapp.com/api` requires a JWT bearer token; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Skyscanner exposes one public developer HTTP API surface (Travel APIs) authenticated by `x-api-key`, plus a case-by-case MCP server with auth details not publicly specified.

SkyWatch has an authenticated REST API, authenticated MCP server, and authenticated WebSocket API; all use the same API key in the `x-api-key` header, with DirectView/WMTS also covered by the same key.
SkyWatch exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server, both using an `x-api-key` API key; no separate GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Slack exposes the Web API, Events API, SCIM API, Slack MCP server, and Slack CLI; auth is primarily OAuth 2.0 access tokens, plus service tokens for CLI and per-surface OAuth for MCP.
Slack.dev exposes Slack Web API, Slack SCIM API, Slack Audit Logs API, Slack MCP Server, and the Slack CLI; auth is via Slack OAuth tokens, while Slack Status API is public.
Sleep Cycle exposes one public HTTP API (Cough Radar) plus Android and iOS SDKs; all use a Sleep Cycle API key, with Cough Radar explicitly sending it as `API-Key`.

SlideRoom exposes one public developer surface: a REST HTTP API at https://api.slideroom.com/ requiring an organization OAuth token sent as `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Google Slides exposes a REST HTTP API at slides.googleapis.com requiring OAuth 2.0, plus a documented MCP server; no public CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
SlidesGPT exposes a keyless public MCP server at https://mcp.slidesgpt.com/mcp and a separate authenticated Enterprise REST API that uses a SlidesGPT-issued API key.
SlidesGPT exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.slidesgpt.com via the OpenAPI spec https://slidesgpt.com/openapi.json; auth is required with a bearer token.

smart-me exposes one documented HTTP API surface (smart-me API v1) plus a public Realtime Webhook API; OAuth application registration is documented, but the API auth scheme could not be pinned to a machine-readable spec.

SmartBear exposes HTTP APIs for BugSnag, PactFlow, Swagger Portal, and Zephyr Essential Cloud, plus SmartBear MCP servers (remote OAuth endpoints and a local npm-based multi-product server); no GraphQL or standalone CLI surface was confirmed.
Smartlook exposes a bearer-token REST API plus public Web SDK and Mobile SDK docs; no MCP server, GraphQL API, or CLI was found.
Smartsheet exposes one REST API and one MCP server; both use a Smartsheet API token, and the REST API also supports OAuth client apps.
Smarty exposes an HTTP API suite authenticated with Smarty secret or embedded key pairs, plus a documented MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Smithery.ai exposes one main HTTP Platform API, a public docs MCP server, and the Smithery CLI; API access uses a Smithery API key or scoped service token, while CLI auth is via `smithery auth login` / `smithery auth token`.

sms77.io (seven.io) exposes one public developer API surface: a REST/HTTP API at https://gateway.seven.io/api, authenticated with a seven API key; plus a seven-cli command-line client that uses a CLI login/session credential.
SMTP2GO exposes a required-auth HTTP API, a required-auth remote MCP server, and SMTP relay access via SMTP username/password; webhooks are delivery callbacks, not an integration surface.

Snap.com exposes the Snapchat Marketing API (Ads API, Ads Gallery API, Conversions API, Public Profile API) over HTTP, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens from a Snap Business Manager OAuth app; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Snowflake exposes two documented developer HTTP surfaces: the Snowflake SQL API (authenticated with OAuth bearer tokens or key-pair JWT-based auth) and broader Snowflake REST APIs (auth mechanism not fully pinned from the retrieved pages).

Snowplow exposes a Console REST API, a Snowplow CLI, a local CLI MCP server, and a remote Snowplow MCP server; auth is via Console API key pair plus org ID, exchanged for a JWT bearer token for the Console API and remote MCP, while the CLI/CLI MCP use `snowplow-cli setup`.
Snyk exposes a REST API and the Snyk CLI; both use Snyk-issued tokens, with REST requiring `Authorization: token`/`Authorization: bearer` and CLI login via `snyk auth`.

Snyk exposes a required-auth REST/OAuth2 API on api.snyk.io, a required-auth CLI (`snyk`), and an MCP server mentioned in the CLI/docs article; no public unauthenticated developer surface was confirmed.
Socket exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated CLI, and one authenticated MCP server; API tokens can also be used by the CLI, and MCP uses OAuth sign-in.

SocketLabs exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Bearer or Basic with a SocketLabs API key) plus an authenticated account-level event-webhook management endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Socrata exposes the public SODA HTTP API; developer docs also reference publisher APIs (Dataset Management API and SODA Producer API), but I could not confirm their auth mechanics from the pages read.
Socure exposes a protected RiskOS HTTP API, public webhooks documentation, and an MCP server; API access uses a Socure-issued API key, while docs access is gated by DevHub login/email link.
Solo.io exposes a documented Gloo Mesh HTTP API surface and the meshctl CLI; CLI auth is via `meshctl login`, while the API auth mechanics were not pinned from the available docs.
Sonar.trading exposes one developer surface: a GraphQL Sonar API authenticated with personal access tokens via `Authorization: Bearer`; no other public API, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Sonatype exposes an authenticated IQ Server REST API and an authenticated Sonatype Guide MCP server; no public GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
soulfamily.ai exposes a public MCP server at https://soulfamily.ai/mcp and no other developer surfaces were confirmed.
Sourcegraph exposes a protected GraphQL API, an OAuth-enabled MCP server, and the `src` CLI; auth is via access tokens or OAuth tokens, with MCP also supporting dynamic client registration.
SourceHut exposes multiple GraphQL APIs across sr.ht services, authenticated with either personal access tokens or OAuth2 bearer tokens; playgrounds are public but operate on production data.
Cloud Source Repositories exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Google OAuth/Bearer) plus a public web console; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.

Southeast Healthcare Real Estate exposes one public MCP surface at https://www.southeasthre.com/_api/mcp; the site’s public MCP docs indicate visitor-token-based calls for Wix API actions, but the endpoint itself also returns an authorization error when called directly.
Spaceship exposes one documented public HTTP API requiring API key + API secret headers, plus a community MCP server; no official GraphQL or CLI was found.

spanner.googleapis.com exposes a public MCP server, a Google Cloud Spanner REST API authenticated with Google OAuth bearer tokens, and Spanner-related use via the gcloud CLI after `gcloud auth login`.

SparkPost exposes one public developer surface: the REST API at https://api.sparkpost.com/api/v1 (and EU at https://api.eu.sparkpost.com/api/v1), authenticated with an API key via Authorization header or HTTP Basic; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs I found.
SparkSpot exposes a public web app marketplace; no developer-facing API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was discoverable from the available public docs.
Speakeasy exposes a public OpenAPI docs/library site, a required-auth Speakeasy CLI, and a docs MCP product surface with auth not fully determined.

Speca exposes one documented integration surface: the public Speca API (auth unknown from docs) and the mock API server at https://mock.speca.io, which requires HTTP Basic auth using a Speca account username/password.

SpectroCoin exposes one public developer surface: a REST/JSON HTTP API at https://spectrocoin.com/api/public/oauth, authenticated either by API key/secret or OAuth client credentials; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Cloud Speech-to-Text exposes one public HTTP API surface at https://speech.googleapis.com for both V1 and V2 docs, authenticated with Google Cloud project credentials (OAuth2/service account bearer tokens); no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was identified.

Speechify exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs on https://api.speechify.ai: the Build API and the Agents API, both using a Speechify API key via `Authorization: Bearer`; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Speedscale exposes a hosted platform API, the proxymock CLI, and a proxymock MCP server; proxymock uses a Speedscale API key for login/auth, while the local proxymock runtime is public.
S&P Global Energy exposes a programmatic HTTP API and an MCP surface; public docs show account-based access in the portal, but no public OpenAPI spec or separate CLI/GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Spinbot exposes one documented authenticated REST API on `api.spinbot.net` with an API key, plus a public website rewriter UI; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

Spinify exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated Spinify Events HTTP API at https://api.spinify.com/v1 with an API key in the Authorization header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Spinitron exposes one authenticated REST API (`https://spinitron.com/api`, API key via Bearer header or `access-token` query param) and one public web widget integration surface (`https://widgets.spinitron.com/widget/now-playing-v2`).
Spinitron exposes one authenticated HTTP API (v2 API via API key) and two public web-widget integration surfaces; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Splice exposes one developer integration surface: the authenticated Splice MCP Server at https://mcp.splice.com/mcp; no public HTTP/GraphQL API or developer CLI was documented.

Split.io exposes one public developer integration surface: the Split Admin HTTP API, authenticated by either a Split Admin API key (`Authorization: Bearer ...`) or a Harness PAT/SAT (`x-api-key: ...`).
Splunk Cloud Platform exposes one documented REST API surface and one MCP server; both require a Splunk authentication token.

Spoonacular exposes one public developer surface: the Spoonacular Food API at https://api.spoonacular.com, authenticated with a spoonacular API key sent either as `apiKey` query param or `x-api-key` header; docs and SDKs are on spoonacular.com.
SportsDataIO exposes public developer docs for multiple league REST APIs; the integration surface is the league API family, authenticated with a SportsDataIO API key.

Spotify exposes one public developer surface: the Spotify Web API at https://api.spotify.com/v1, authenticated with OAuth access tokens; apps are created in the Spotify Developer Dashboard using a client ID and client secret.
Spreedly exposes one authenticated REST API (API V1), a public docs MCP server, and a self-hosted MCP server authenticated by environment key + access secret.

Springer Nature exposes one developer-facing HTTP API surface at api.springernature.com, authenticated with an API key passed as the `api_key` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Springer Nature exposes four developer HTTP APIs on api.springernature.com: Open Access, Metadata, Meta v2, and Full Text; all require a Springer Nature API key passed as `api_key`.

sqladmin.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP Cloud SQL Admin API and one public MCP server at https://sqladmin.googleapis.com/mcp; auth for the API is Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.

Squarespace exposes one public developer surface for integrations: the Squarespace Commerce REST API at https://api.squarespace.com/1.0, authenticated by either a site API key or an OAuth access token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Square exposes an authenticated REST API, a GraphQL surface, and an MCP server; REST auth uses either a Square bearer access token or an OAuth app registration flow.
SST exposes public documentation and a GraphQL tutorial page; no public developer API, MCP server, or CLI auth surface was confirmed.

Stable Baseline exposes one MCP endpoint and one REST API, both authenticated by MCP keys or OAuth 2.1, plus public docs; REST spec URL still needs confirmation from a machine-readable openapi document.

StackBlitz exposes public HTTP developer surfaces for the JavaScript SDK, POST API, and WebContainer API; Codeflow/pr.new/Web Publisher requires a StackBlitz account session, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

Stack Exchange exposes one HTTP API (v2.3) and one MCP server; both require Stack Apps-issued OAuth/API credentials, with public read-only API access available via API key and authenticated/user-write access via OAuth access token.
Stainless exposes an authenticated HTTP API and CLI for managing projects/builds/configs, plus MCP documentation for generated servers; no public GraphQL surface was found.
Staircase AI exposes an authenticated MCP server and documented HTTP developer APIs; no public GraphQL or standalone CLI surface was confirmed.

Starburst exposes three developer surfaces: Galaxy API, Enterprise REST API, and an MCP server; all are authenticated, with Galaxy using an API token and Enterprise REST API supporting Basic or Bearer auth.

Statsig exposes a Console API (OpenAPI-backed), an HTTP API for feature/config evaluation and event logging, an MCP server at https://api.statsig.com/v1/mcp, and the Statsig CLI (`siggy`), with Console API keys, Client API keys, and MCP OAuth as the main credentials.

StatSocial appears to offer a logged-in web platform and data delivery/integration paths via API, clean room, or file transfer, but I could not verify any public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface or authentication details beyond the login portal.

StatusCake exposes one public developer surface: a Bearer-token authenticated REST API at https://api.statuscake.com/v1 with OpenAPI 2.0 spec at https://developers.statuscake.com/redocusaurus/plugin-redoc-0.yaml; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Statuspage.io exposes one public developer surface: the Statuspage REST API at https://api.statuspage.io/v1/, authenticated with a Statuspage API token via `Authorization: OAuth <token>` or `api_key` query param.

Steadily exposes two authenticated REST APIs: a Partner API and a Rater Quotes API, both on `https://api.steadily.com/v1`, using either the issued API key (query or header) and, for rater quoting, a two-step agent bearer token flow.

Steel exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for sessions, agent traces, files, extensions, captchas, and profiles; a CDP WebSocket connect endpoint; and the Steel CLI, all using the same org-scoped API key.
Steercorp.io exposes one planned HTTP API surface, Steer Intelligence API, but no public auth or machine-readable spec is published yet.

Stitch exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Connect API and Import API) plus public post-load webhooks; Connect uses a partner-issued Bearer token, and Import API uses a Stitch-generated Bearer access token.
Stoplight exposes a required-auth Catalog API using workspace tokens, plus a required-auth Stoplight CLI publishing surface using a per-project CI token.

Google Cloud Storage exposes the authenticated Cloud Storage JSON API plus authenticated CLIs `gcloud storage` and `gsutil`; I found no separate public MCP or GraphQL surface for storage.googleapis.com.

surfaces.name: Cloud Storage IAM for gcloud storage

domains.summary: Storage Transfer Service exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://storagetransfer.googleapis.com; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.
Storecove exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API documented at https://www.storecove.com/docs/ and using a Storecove API key via Authorization: Bearer.
Storecove exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.storecove.com/api/v2 using a Storecove API key sent as `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Stormglass exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated HTTP Weather API at `https://api.stormglass.io/v2`, using a personal API key in the `Authorization` header; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Storyblok exposes authenticated Management and Content Delivery HTTP APIs, an authenticated GraphQL API, and a Storyblok MCP server with auth mechanics not fully pinned from public docs.
Strapi exposes a Content API and GraphQL API authenticated by API tokens, plus an MCP server authenticated by Admin tokens; the Strapi CLI is public.

Streak exposes a Basic-authenticated REST API at https://api.streak.com/api/v1 and /api/v2 (OpenAPI at https://streak.com/api/openapi.json) plus an MCP integration surface referenced from Streak’s site; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.

Stream exposes two HTTP APIs: the Stream REST API and the Stream Chat API; both require app credentials, with Stream REST using app API key plus JWT signed by the app secret, and Stream Chat using app credentials per its OpenAPI docs.
Stream exposes three public developer HTTP APIs: REST, Chat API, and Video API; all require Stream-issued API key plus JWT auth token, and no MCP/GraphQL/CLI surface was confirmed.
Street View Publish API exposes one authenticated REST surface at https://streetviewpublish.googleapis.com, using either a Google API key or OAuth 2.0 Bearer access token.
Google Cloud STS exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://sts.googleapis.com; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

Stytch exposes a REST API (Consumer auth), Programmatic Workspace Actions API, an MCP server, and a CLI-oriented connected-app flow; auth is via project_id/secret basic auth, workspace management key basic auth, and MCP/OAuth-connected app credentials.

surfaces.name: Stytch Connected Apps OAuth / Authorization Code Flow

domains.summary: Stytch exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs (Consumer and Workspace Management) and one authenticated MCP server; no public API, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Stytch Workspace Management / Programmatic Workspace Actions API

domains.summary: Subdivide exposes no developer-facing API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI; only a public marketing/docs website surface was found, with no integration credentials.
subscriber.read appears to expose no public developer surface of its own; search results led only to Apollo GraphOS docs, so no subscriber.read integration surface was found.
SubscriptionFlow exposes a REST API requiring OAuth v2 bearer tokens, public webhook documentation, and an MCP server with undisclosed auth mechanics; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Substack exposes an OAuth HTTP integration surface and third-party MCP servers that rely on browser session-cookie auth; no public CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Success.co exposes one GraphQL public API secured by Bearer API keys and one OAuth-protected MCP server at https://success.co/mcp.
Sumsub’s public developer surface is a REST API and an MCP server; the REST API uses a Sumsub-issued app token, while the MCP auth mechanics remain unknown from the available docs.

Superhuman exposes one developer integration surface: the Superhuman Mail MCP server at https://mcp.mail.superhuman.com/mcp, authenticated via a Superhuman Mail account/OAuth flow (no public HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found).
Supermetrics exposes one public HTTP API and one MCP server; both require a Supermetrics API key, and no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
SuperTokens exposes public FDI HTTP docs, authenticated CDI HTTP, GraphQL session verification guidance, and MCP auth tooling; Core access uses an `api-key` header while FDI is public.
SureVoIP exposes one documented developer integration surface: a VoIP HTTP API at https://www.surevoip.co.uk/api/ using OAuth 2.0 apps; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

SurrealDB exposes one HTTP REST API, one GraphQL-over-HTTP API, one MCP server, and the `surreal` CLI; auth is via SurrealDB system user credentials or JWT tokens, with MCP auth not fully pinned from the docs read.

City of Surrey exposes one developer integration surface we could confirm: an ArcGIS-based HTTP Open Data API at gisservices.surrey.ca/arcgis/rest/services/OpenData/MapServer, authenticated via an ArcGIS OAuth bearer token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: City of Surrey Open Data API

domains.summary: SurveyMonkey exposes a REST API at https://api.surveymonkey.com/v3 and an MCP server at https://mcp.surveymonkey.com/mcp; both use OAuth 2.0, with no GraphQL or CLI surface confirmed.
Svix exposes one authenticated REST API, a CLI, and public webhook-consumer documentation; auth is via per-environment Bearer API token.
Swagger.io exposes public docs and open-source tools (Editor, UI, Codegen) plus the SwaggerHub cloud platform, which requires a SmartBear ID login.
SwaggerHub exposes one documented developer surface: the Swagger Portal API at https://api.portal.swaggerhub.com/v1, authenticated with a Swagger API key sent as Authorization: Bearer {API_KEY}.
Sweetspot exposes a password/SSO-authenticated web console only; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was verified.

Swell exposes a REST Backend API, an experimental storefront GraphQL API, and the Swell CLI; auth is via store ID + secret key for the Backend API, a bearer-style secret key for GraphQL, and browser login for the CLI.

swop.cx exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs: GraphQL at https://swop.cx/graphql and REST at https://swop.cx/rest, both using the same API key via Authorization: ApiKey ... or ?api-key=...; no MCP, CLI, or public developer surface found.
Sybill exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.sybill.ai/v1 and one authenticated MCP server at https://mcp.sybill.ai/mcp, both using the same Sybill API key.

symbol.chat exposes one preview HTTP API and one SSE MCP server; both require a Symbol API key (`sym_...`), with Claude Code/Claude Desktop using OAuth/browser sign-in for MCP and other MCP clients sending `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>`.
Synctera offers a single documented public developer surface: a REST API at api-sandbox/api.synctera.com with Bearer API key auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Systembolaget exposes one authenticated HTTP API and one public MCP server; the API uses a bearer Systembolaget API key from the API portal.
Telegram exposes the Bot API over HTTP, MTProto client API, and a Gateway API; Bot API auth uses bot tokens, MTProto uses api_id/api_hash or bot token, and no MCP/CLI surface was confirmed.
Tableau exposes one public HTTP REST API and one GraphQL Metadata API; both require Tableau-issued auth tokens via PAT, username/password sign-in, or JWT-based connected app flows.

tableau.github.io exposes public docs plus the Tableau Server REST API via the Python TSC library, the Analytics Extensions API, and public Extensions/Embedding documentation; no MCP or CLI surface was confirmed.
TAFQIT exposes one HTTP API surface (TAFQIT API at https://api.tafkit.com) documented on tafqit.com, but no first-party credential or spec was discoverable; authentication remains unknown.
Taggun exposes one developer-facing HTTP API for receipt/invoice OCR at https://api.taggun.io/api/receipt/v1, authenticated with a Taggun API key sent in the `apikey` header.

Google Tag Manager exposes one developer integration surface: the REST API v2 at https://tagmanager.googleapis.com, authenticated with a Google OAuth 2.0 access token in the `Authorization: Bearer` header.
Tailscale exposes one documented HTTP API, the Tailscale CLI, and an Aperture MCP-server proxying feature; API access uses API tokens or OAuth client credentials, while the CLI uses auth keys.

TalkJS exposes one public developer surface: an authenticated REST API at https://api.talkjs.com/v1 using Bearer admin tokens (or legacy secret keys) from the TalkJS dashboard; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Target.com exposes only a developer portal landing page; no public API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was discoverable from the provided docs.
Open Targets Platform exposes a public GraphQL API and a public MCP server; no developer credential is required for either.
Tavily exposes one authenticated HTTP API, one authenticated MCP server, and the authenticated `tvly` CLI; auth is via Tavily API key, with CLI also supporting browser OAuth login.

Tavily exposes one HTTP API and one remote MCP server; both require a Tavily API key, with the HTTP API using `Authorization: Bearer` and the MCP server accepting `tavilyApiKey` in the connect URL query string.
TaxHeaven ChatGPT App exposes one public MCP server at https://taxheaven-chatgpt-app.vercel.app/mcp; no other developer surfaces were found.

TaxJar exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://api.taxjar.com/v2/ using a personal API key via Authorization: Token token=... or Authorization: Bearer ...; sandbox is https://api.sandbox.taxjar.com/v2/.
TaxRate.io exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
tcgdex.net exposes two public developer surfaces: a REST API at https://api.tcgdex.net/v2 and a GraphQL API at https://api.tcgdex.net/v2/graphql; no auth credentials are required.

Teamwork.com exposes three public HTTP APIs: Teamwork.com API, Teamwork Desk API, and Teamwork Spaces API; authentication is documented for Basic and OAuth 2.0, with OAuth app registration via the Developer Portal.
Telegram exposes 3 developer surfaces: Bot API, Gateway API, and MTProto/Telegram API; all require credentials and no MCP/CLI surface was found.
Damoov exposes one authenticated REST API surface plus public AI-integration/docs tooling; no GraphQL or MCP server was confirmed.
TelemetryDeck exposes a bearer-authenticated Query API, an ingest HTTP API with unknown auth from public docs, and an MCP server mentioned in AI docs with undisclosed connect/auth details.

Teller exposes one public developer surface: the REST API at https://api.teller.io, authenticated by mTLS client certs plus HTTP Basic access tokens for end-user data; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in docs reviewed.

Telnyx exposes a public REST API v2, OAuth 2.0 endpoints, a Telnyx CLI, MCP Apps over streamable HTTP, and a public x402 inference endpoint; credentials include Telnyx API keys, OAuth client credentials, and x402 pay-per-call (no signup).

Temporal exposes a Cloud Ops HTTP/gRPC API, the Temporal Server gRPC API, the `temporal` CLI, and an MCP knowledge-base server; auth is API key or mTLS for Cloud/CLI, OAuth for MCP, and server API auth was not fully determined.
Termly exposes one public developer surface: a REST API documented at docs.termly.io, authenticated with manually issued API keys in the Authorization: Bearer header.

Terraform exposes the HCP Terraform HTTP API, the Terraform CLI, and a Terraform MCP server; HCP Terraform API/CLI use user API tokens acquired via web login, while MCP auth could not be fully determined from the available docs.
testing.googleapis.com maps to Google Cloud REST APIs; auth via Google user ADC/OAuth bearer tokens or API keys, with no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface identified.
Text2Data exposes one public integration surface: an HTTP REST API at https://api.text2data.com requiring an API key plus secret, obtained from the user’s administration panel after registration.

Text2data exposes one developer surface: a REST API at http://api.text2data.com/v3/ authenticated by an API key, with an optional secret supported in the SDK; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were found.

surfaces.name: Text2data Text Analytics & Sentiment Analysis API

domains.summary: Cloud Text-to-Speech exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API at https://texttospeech.googleapis.com; no public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was found.
TfL exposes one public developer integration surface: the Unified REST API at https://api.tfl.gov.uk, authenticated with a TfL API subscription key via the `app_key` query parameter.
The Blue Alliance exposes two authenticated REST APIs (Read v3 and Trusted Write v1) plus webhook delivery; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: The Blue Alliance Trusted API v1

domains.summary: TMDB exposes one public developer surface: the v3 HTTP API at https://api.themoviedb.org/3, authenticated by API key or Bearer read token, and user session_id for user-scoped calls.

Theneo exposes public docs plus authenticated web app/editor surfaces; the docs mention API Explorer/testing and MCP server generation, but no public developer API or CLI with a discoverable spec was confirmed.
Noun Project exposes one developer integration surface: a REST HTTP API at https://api.thenounproject.com secured by OAuth 1.0a using a user-created API key + API secret.

The SMS Works exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.thesmsworks.co.uk/v1 (OpenAPI docs at api.thesmsworks.co.uk/docs/api-reference.html, spec download linked), using either a JWT or an API key/secret pair to mint a JWT.
TheTVDB exposes one developer integration surface: an authenticated HTTP API; I found no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface from the official docs/pages reviewed.

They Said So exposes one REST API surface at https://quotes.rest/ for quotes and inspiration, with API-key auth via `Authorization: Bearer` or `api_key` query param; some endpoints are public and rate-limited.

surfaces.name: They Said So Quotes & Inspiration REST API

domains.summary: ThoughtSpot exposes REST API v1/v2, a GraphQL API, and an MCP server; REST uses bearer or session-cookie auth, MCP uses OAuth, and GraphQL auth could not be pinned from the docs read.
ThoughtSpot exposes REST API v1/v2.0 and a Spotter MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

Thumbtack exposes one developer HTTP API surface (Partner Platform API) on https://api.thumbtack.com/api, authenticated with a Thumbtack-issued OAuth2 client via HydraOAuth; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Ticketmaster exposes the public Discovery API v2 via API key, plus a Partner API, API Explorer, and a community MCP server; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Ticket Tailor exposes one HTTP API at https://api.tickettailor.com and one MCP server at https://mcp.tickettailor.ai/mcp, both using the same box-office API key via HTTP Basic auth.
Ticket Tailor exposes one authenticated REST API and a public MCP server; the API uses a box-office-scoped API key via HTTP Basic Auth.

Tiger Data offers a Tiger Cloud REST API, Tiger CLI, and Tiger MCP server, all authenticated with Tiger Cloud credentials (personal access token via Authorization: Bearer for REST; `tiger login` for CLI; MCP OAuth/login flow), plus an additional Tiger Cloud REST API preview surface with auth still unknown.
Tigris exposes a CLI, an S3-compatible object storage HTTP API, a partner-integrations HTTP API, and an OAuth-protected MCP server.

TikTok exposes several developer HTTP APIs (Content Posting, Login Kit/OAuth, Display, and Data Portability), all gated by the app’s client key/client secret; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: TikTok Login Kit / OAuth user access token management

domains.summary: Timescale.com exposes developer docs for TimescaleDB/Tiger Cloud SQL integration; no REST/GraphQL/MCP/CLI public integration surface or explicit app-issued credential could be confirmed from the available docs.
TinaCMS exposes GraphQL content APIs (hosted Content API and local CLI-run GraphQL API); no public REST, MCP, or standalone CLI integration surface was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
Tines exposes a tenant REST API, SCIM API, and built-in MCP server; all require auth via Tines API keys, with the MCP server also supporting OAuth.
tldraw.com exposes the public tldraw SDK/docs and a public demo sync surface; the MCP App is announced but its client endpoint/auth are not public in the docs.
tldraw.dev exposes a public SDK runtime JS API and public `npm create tldraw` CLI, plus tldraw sync and an MCP app with authentication/details not fully specified in the docs.
tldraw.notion.site exposes Notion developer surfaces: Notion API, Notion CLI (`ntn`), and Notion MCP at https://mcp.notion.com/mcp; all authenticate with Notion bearer/OAuth credentials.
Togai exposes a REST HTTP API at api.togai.com using Bearer API keys; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Together AI exposes an OpenAI-compatible HTTP inference API at https://api.together.ai/v1, plus the Together CLI (`tg`); both use project-scoped API keys via `Authorization: Bearer`, and the docs mention a coding-agent MCP integration page but no public MCP connect endpoint was confirmed.
TokenJay exposes one public HTTP API surface at https://api.tokenjay.app/ with OpenAPI at https://api.tokenjay.app/api-docs and no documented auth requirement.

Token Terminal exposes a bearer-token REST API at `https://api.tokenterminal.com/v2` and a remote MCP server at `https://mcp.tokenterminal.com/mcp`; the API key is issued from the user account page, while MCP auth is documented as OAuth 2.1 with PKCE but the exact integration binding couldn’t be fully pinned from the pages read.

Tomba.io exposes one REST API (`https://api.tomba.io/v1`), one MCP surface (`https://tomba.io/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json`), and one CLI (`tomba`); all use the same Tomba API key + secret credential pair.
Tomorrow.io exposes one documented Weather API HTTP surface (API key via `apikey` query param or header) and a referenced MCP surface with unclear connect details; no GraphQL or CLI surfaced.

TomTom exposes a public remote MCP server at https://mcp.tomtom.com/maps, authenticated by TomTom API key via `tomtom-api-key`; local setup is self-hosted from GitHub and no separate public CLI/API surface was confirmed.
Tonita exposes one public developer surface: a Bearer-authenticated MCP server at https://mcp-generic.tonita.co/mcp using a Tonita API key.
Toolhouse.ai exposes a documented Workers HTTP API authenticated with a Toolhouse API key; MCP servers are documented but the connect endpoint/auth mechanics were not determined.

ToolPipe exposes one public integration surface found: an MCP server at toolpipe.dev/stdio, authenticated by an optional `TOOLPIPE_API_KEY` for higher rate limits; no verified HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found from toolpipe.ai.
toolresults.googleapis.com exposes one HTTP API, Cloud Tool Results API, authenticated with Google OAuth user credentials; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Cloud TPU exposes the TPU API (v1 and v2) over https://tpu.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 / ADC, plus CLI surfaces `gcloud compute tpus` and `ctpu`.
Traccar exposes one public REST API surface and one MCP endpoint; the REST API supports session/basic and API key auth, and MCP uses Traccar OAuth/client setup with the `/api/mcp` endpoint.
Traceloop exposes a project/environment-scoped API key for its dashboard API and ingestion endpoints, plus a self-hosted Hub OpenAI-compatible HTTP API with undocumented auth on the pages reviewed.
Tradematic Cloud exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API at https://api.tradematic.com authenticated with an API key in `X-API-KEY`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Tradematic.com exposes a public Tradematic Trader developer-facing product page/docs, but I could not confirm any public HTTP/GraphQL/MCP/CLI integration surface or developer-issued credentials from the site.
Trade Me exposes a public developer HTTP API on https://api.trademe.co.nz with OAuth 1.0a app and member authentication; Catalogue methods are public, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Traffic Director exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://trafficdirector.googleapis.com; no public MCP/CLI/GraphQL surface found.

Trakt exposes one public developer surface: the Trakt REST API at https://api.trakt.tv, authenticated with a Trakt OAuth app client ID (`trakt-api-key`) and, for user-protected endpoints, an OAuth Bearer access token; no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Transavia exposes a partner-only HTTP API via the partner developer portal; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the domain itself.
Transcoder API exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface plus the `gcloud transcoder` CLI; both use Google Cloud OAuth/ADC credentials.
Cloud Translation API exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface at https://translation.googleapis.com; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Transloadit exposes a single REST API at api2.transloadit.com, an MCP server at api2.transloadit.com/mcp, and multiple SDK/CLI integrations; authentication uses workspace Auth Key + Auth Secret signatures or short-lived Bearer tokens, with Uppy/browser integrations additionally supporting signature-based auth.
trapstreet.com exposes a public Shopify storefront and a public UCP MCP endpoint; no developer auth credentials were identified from the available docs.

Trash Nothing exposes one public developer integration: a REST API at https://trashnothing.com/api/v1.4 with OpenAPI spec https://trashnothing.com/api/v1.4/trashnothing-openapi.yaml, authenticated either by API key or OAuth2.
Travel Impact Model exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST API and one public MCP server; the REST API uses a Google Cloud API key, and the MCP endpoint is unauthenticated per docs.
travelpartner.googleapis.com exposes the Google Travel Partner API v3 REST surface at `https://travelpartner.googleapis.com/v3`, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer access tokens.
TravExp exposes a public MCP server at /mcp and OAuth authorization/token endpoints on its own domain for client-based auth.

Travis CI exposes one public developer surface: the Travis CI API v3 HTTP API at https://api.travis-ci.com, authenticated with a Travis CLI-minted API access token via `travis login --pro` / `travis token --pro`.
Tray.ai exposes a GraphQL Embedded API and an MCP Agent Gateway, both authenticated with bearer tokens; no public CLI or REST surface was confirmed.
Tray.io exposes a GraphQL Embedded API with master/user token auth, plus a hosted MCP server at api.tray.io/mcp using Tray Identity OAuth2.
Treasury Prime exposes REST HTTP APIs for production and sandbox, authenticated with per-environment API key pairs via HTTP Basic Auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Treblle exposes a public docs site and a Treblle platform/developer portal that uses a Treblle API key plus SDK token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
Tredict exposes one developer HTTP API and one MCP server; both accept either a personal access token or OAuth2 client-authenticated bearer token flow.
Trello exposes one documented public integration surface: the Trello REST API, authenticated with an API key plus user token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

surfaces.name: Trello REST API public keyless endpoints

domains.summary: trevorblades.com exposes one public GraphQL API at https://countries.trevorblades.com/; no developer-issued credentials, MCP server, HTTP REST API, or CLI were found.

Trigger.dev exposes a required-auth HTTP Management API and Realtime API at https://api.trigger.dev (auth via `TRIGGER_SECRET_KEY` for management/CLI, or scoped Public Access / Trigger Tokens for realtime), plus an MCP server and CLI that use the same Trigger.dev login/secret-key auth flow.

Trimble exposes Trimble Connect developer surfaces: 6 authenticated HTTP APIs (Core, Core Account, Model, Organizer, Property Set, Topics) using Trimble Identity OAuth bearer tokens, plus a public Workspace API package for browser extensions/components.
Tripadvisor exposes the Terra Partner HTTP API secured by `X-TripAdvisor-API-Key`, plus a public MCP server at `https://docs.terra.tripadvisor.com/mcp`; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.

trivago exposes one documented HTTP integration surface with unknown FastConnect auth and one authenticated Conversion API endpoint using `X-Trv-Ana-Key`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Tropic exposes one authenticated REST API, one authenticated MCP server, and webhook delivery verification; no GraphQL or CLI surface found.
TruAnon exposes a public marketing site and a developer console login; no public API/OpenAPI, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the accessible pages.

TrueLayer exposes one documented programmatic HTTP API surface plus an experimental MCP server; HTTP uses Console-issued client credentials to mint Bearer access tokens, while MCP auth could not be determined from the docs read.

surfaces.name: TrueLayer MCP server for AI assistants

domains.summary: Truffle Journal exposes one developer integration surface: an MCP server for ChatGPT at https://mcp.trufflejournal.com/api/v1/mcp, authenticated by logging in with a Truffle Journal account.

Trulioo’s public integration surface consists of HTTP REST APIs (Platform API via OAuth, legacy Normalized API via Basic auth, plus an mTLS endpoint) and an HMAC-signed webhook receiver; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Truora exposes two documented HTTP APIs for developers—Validators API and Checks API—both using a Truora API key; Digital Identity web/mobile docs mention integrations but I couldn’t confirm a separate public developer surface.
trustrebound.com exposes only a public website and Scam Assistant page; no developer-facing HTTP APIs, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.
Channel3 exposes one authenticated HTTP product API, a public MCP server, and an authenticated CLI; all use the same Channel3 API key where required.
Chroma exposes HTTP APIs for Core/Cloud and a streamable-http MCP Package Search server, all using a Chroma API key passed as `x-chroma-token`.
Finch exposes one HTTP API and one MCP server; both use a Finch access token, minted via Finch Connect using sandbox app credentials.
Kopi exposes one developer integration surface: a remote MCP server at https://www.trykopi.ai/mcp using OAuth 2.0 via the Kopi account.
Marrow on trymalcolm.com exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated MCP server, both using a Bearer API key; no GraphQL or CLI surface was found.
TSAPI exposes one public HTTP API example service with OpenAPI spec at /swagger/v1/swagger.json; no credentials are required.

TUI exposes 7 developer HTTP APIs (Meta Partner Packages & Flights, Partner Content, NewSkies GoNow, PriceFile, NSKCC Availability Search, CheckInHandler, Meta-Search-Generic, Meta Partner Package Live Search, and NewSkies Payment) secured mainly by OAuth2 client-credentials bearer tokens from `https://prod.api.tui/oauth2/token` / `https://pre-prod.api.tui/oauth2/token`, with NewSkies GoNow explicitly using an `x-apikey` header.

surfaces.name: TUI Meta Partner Packages & Flights API
TUI Meta Partner Package Live Search API

domains.summary: tuio-mcp-demo.vercel.app exposes no discoverable public integration surface beyond the reported MCP endpoint, which returned 404; no auth credentials were identified.
turbopuffer exposes one authenticated HTTP API using Bearer API keys; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Turkish Airlines exposes a gated developer portal and API products; access requires a portal account plus per-application OAuth2 client credentials, with docs available only after sign-in.
Turso exposes a protected Platform REST API, a CLI (`turso`), and a hosted MCP server at `https://mcp.turso.ai/mcp`, with Bearer API tokens for the API and OAuth-based browser login for CLI/MCP.
tusalario.app exposes a public MCP server at https://tusalario.app/mcp; no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
TVmaze exposes a public REST API at https://api.tvmaze.com with no auth, and a premium user REST API at https://static.tvmaze.com/apidoc/ using HTTP Basic with TVmaze username + dashboard API key.

Twelve Data exposes one documented HTTP market-data API at https://api.twelvedata.com, authenticated with a user-specific API key passed as `apikey` query parameter; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Twilio exposes a public MCP docs server and authenticated REST APIs over HTTP Basic using either API key + secret or Account SID + Auth Token.
Twist exposes one public developer surface: the Twist API v3 HTTP API at https://twist.com, authenticated with OAuth 2 Bearer tokens via Twist’s OAuth client registration.

X exposes a private X API REST surface at https://api.x.com/2, plus two hosted MCP servers: https://api.x.com/mcp and public docs MCP at https://docs.x.com/mcp; auth is via app-only Bearer token or OAuth 2.0 user-context credentials, with `xurl mcp` as the easiest MCP login path.

Tyk exposes documented MCP surfaces and admin HTTP APIs for MCP proxy management; the public API-to-MCP docs indicate an MCP server, while direct GraphQL/CLI surfaces were not confirmed from the retrieved pages.

surfaces.name: Tyk Dashboard API extensions for MCP proxy management

domains.summary: Tyk exposes the internal Gateway API (and OSS Gateway API) over HTTP with the gateway admin secret, plus an MCP Gateway surface; the docs also point to Dashboard-backed control-plane APIs, but their auth mechanics weren’t fully pinned down.
Typeform has 3 authenticated HTTP APIs (Create, Responses, Webhooks) and 1 authenticated MCP server; no separate CLI or GraphQL surface was found.
Typesense exposes one public developer surface: the REST API, authenticated with API keys via `X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs.

Uber exposes the Riders REST API and Uber Eats Marketplace REST APIs; both use OAuth-based auth via Uber developer app credentials, with the Riders API also supporting legacy server tokens, and there is an external MCP server project for Uber rides whose auth could not be pinned from public docs.
Uber Eats exposes three authenticated HTTP developer APIs (Integration Activation & Configuration, Store, Order Fulfillment); no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were found.

surfaces.name: Uber Eats Integration Activation & Configuration API

domains.summary: Ubersuggest exposes one public integration surface: an authenticated MCP server at https://ubersuggest-mcp.neilpatelapi.com/mcp using Ubersuggest account OAuth 2.0; no HTTP API, GraphQL API, or CLI was found on ubersuggest.com.
UCSB exposes one developer-facing integration surface: the UCSB API Gateway HTTP API at https://api.ucsb.edu, authenticated by a portal-issued Consumer Key sent as the `ucsb-api-key` header.
uebermaps.com exposes one HTTP API surface at https://uebermaps.com/api/v2; no public auth mechanics, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI were confirmed.
UIIC exposes one developer surface: an authenticated HTTP API on Apisetu (`https://apisetu.gov.in/uiic/v3`) with two endpoints, secured by API key + clientId.

surfaces.name: United India Insurance Company Limited API

domains.summary: Umbraco Compose exposes authenticated HTTP APIs for Management, Ingestion, and GraphQL, plus a Developer MCP server and CLI; auth is via project-scoped Personal Access Tokens for Ingestion/GraphQL, OAuth client-credentials for Management, and an API User for the MCP/CLI path.
unavatar.now.sh exposes one public HTTP avatar API surface at unavatar.io with no required developer credential on the free tier; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

UniCourt exposes one documented developer surface: the UniCourt Enterprise API (Court Data API / Legal Analytics API / PACER API) via docs.unicourt.com, with auth required and no public API spec or separate CLI/MCP/GraphQL surface found.
Unified.to exposes a required-auth REST API, required-auth MCP server, and an unknown-auth CLI; the main credential is a workspace API key used as Bearer auth.
United Rentals exposes one authenticated customer portal surface, Total Control at https://totalcontrol.ur.com; no public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface was found.
Unstructured exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP API at https://platform.unstructuredapp.io/api/v1 authenticated with a workspace-scoped API key in the `unstructured-api-key` header.

Unthread exposes one documented HTTP API, three MCP surfaces, and webhooks; the API uses a service-account API key, while the MCP/auth details and webhooks auth could not be fully determined from the pages read.
Updown.io exposes a REST API authenticated by API key plus a public pulse push endpoint; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Found one authenticated HTTP API surface on upenn.edu: the Penn Computational Memory Lab MediaWiki API at https://memory.psych.upenn.edu/mediawiki/api.php, using a site login/session credential; no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.

surfaces.name: Penn Computational Memory Lab MediaWiki API

domains.summary: Uploadcare exposes three public integration surfaces for developers: Upload API and REST API over HTTP, plus the public URL API/CDN; it also ships a CLI (`uploadcare`) authenticated with the project public and secret keys.
UPMSP exposes one HTTP API surface via APIs.eGov/APIs.guru for Class X/XII marksheet verification, authenticated with an APIsetu clientId; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: UP State Board of High School and Intermediate Education Certificate API

domains.summary: Mapped one MCP surface for Upplai: the authenticated MCP server at https://upplai-mcp-server-production.up.railway.app/mcp, using OAuth client credentials from Upplai’s registration flow.
UP results portal is public and exposes only a web-based results surface at https://results.upmsp.edu.in; no developer-authenticated APIs, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
Upsun exposes a REST API, a CLI, and an MCP server; REST/CLI use Upsun-issued API tokens, while the MCP endpoint is documented but its auth mechanics are not pinned down.
UptimeRobot exposes a protected REST API v3, a public quick-monitor setup flow for AI agents, and an MCP endpoint; API key auth is used for the REST API.

Uptrace exposes an authenticated JSON REST API, authenticated MCP server, Terraform CLI provider, and source-map upload/list/delete HTTP endpoints; auth uses a user API token or project DSN/token depending on surface.
Upwork exposes one public developer integration surface: an OAuth 2.0-protected GraphQL API; no MCP or CLI surfaced from the docs I found.

Urlbox exposes one developer API surface: the REST API at https://api.urlbox.com, authenticated with a project secret key via Bearer auth, plus secure render-link URLs using the Urlbox API key path segment and HMAC token.
Bruno offers a public CLI (`bru`) for running collections; the docs also describe GraphQL request support as a client feature, but no Bruno-issued API credential or hosted developer API was found.
Fathom Analytics exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface (v1) using a Bearer API token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs reviewed.
useminutes.app exposes a public local-first Minutes MCP server and CLI, both unauthenticated and installable via `npx minutes-mcp` and Homebrew; no HTTP or GraphQL developer API was found.
Needle exposes only public website/docs content and free tools in the sources reviewed; no public developer API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was evidenced.
Pylon exposes a bearer-token HTTP API and an MCP server; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
USPTO exposes the Open Data Portal HTTP API (with USPTO.gov account + personalized API key), plus several ODP API families linked from the portal; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.

VA.gov exposes the VA API Platform with public open-data APIs (VA Forms) and authenticated REST APIs including Benefits Documents and patient-health; Benefits Intake auth could not be confirmed from the pages read.
Vantage exposes one authenticated REST API and two MCP surfaces: the Docs MCP is public/undetermined auth, and the Vantage MCP accepts either a Vantage API token or remote MCP authorization.
Vast.ai exposes one authenticated REST API and one CLI, both using the same Vast.ai API key; no GraphQL or MCP surface was found.
VAT API exposes one authenticated HTTP API surface (v2) using an `x-api-key` header; no other developer surfaces were found.

Google Vault exposes one public developer surface: the Vault API (HTTP/REST) at https://vault.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

Vectara exposes one public developer surface: the REST API v2 at https://api.vectara.io/v2 with OpenAPI spec https://docs.vectara.com/vectara-oas-v2.yaml, authenticated by either API key (`x-api-key`) or OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Vectara exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated REST API v2 at https://api.vectara.io/v2/ with OpenAPI spec and API key or OAuth 2.0 client-credentials auth; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was identified.

Vectorize exposes one documented HTTP API (Bearer auth with API access token), one MCP connect endpoint, and one local MCP CLI server; both MCP surfaces use the agent API key, with the MCP endpoint also requiring the agent ID in its URL.

vectortile.googleapis.com exposes the Google Semantic Tile API over HTTP/JSON; it requires either a Google Maps API key (`key` query parameter) or a Google OAuth 2.0 access token (`Authorization: Bearer`).

VEED exposes one authenticated MCP surface and one authenticated HTTP API surface; both use VEED OAuth-based access, with MCP on https://www.veed.io/api/v1/mcp and the Fabric 1.0 API documented at https://www.veed.io/api.
Vendr exposes one public HTTP API and one MCP server; both require a Vendr access key, and no GraphQL or CLI surfaces were found.
Vendure exposes a GraphQL API with API-key or admin bearer-token auth, plus a public docs MCP server at https://docs.vendure.io/mcp.
Venturu exposes one MCP server (OAuth/PKCE, streamable HTTP) and one authenticated Partner HTTP API (OpenAPI available).
Veracode exposes authenticated REST APIs, XML APIs, and a VRM GraphQL API; all use Veracode-issued API credentials, with REST also supporting OAuth or HMAC and XML/GraphQL using HMAC.

Vercel exposes a REST API at https://api.vercel.com authenticated by Bearer access tokens, a Vercel CLI authenticated via `vercel login`, and a Vercel MCP server whose auth mechanics were not determined from the docs reviewed.
Veriff exposes a REST-only public API v1 secured by `X-AUTH-CLIENT` plus `X-HMAC-SIGNATURE`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was found.

surfaces.name: Veriff Public v1 Sync API for SSN validation

domains.summary: Chrome Verified Access API exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST surface at https://verifiedaccess.googleapis.com with v1/v2 discovery docs; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.

VersionEye exposes one public developer surface: an OpenAPI v1 HTTP API at https://www.versioneye.com/api/v1, authenticated with VersionEye account basic auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
VersionHistory exposes one public REST API at https://versionhistory.googleapis.com/v1; no separate auth, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.
Vespa exposes HTTP Document and Query APIs plus the Vespa CLI; the CLI uses a Vespa Cloud developer API key, while the public HTTP auth details were not fully determined from the docs reviewed.

viaNexus exposes one REST API, one MCP server, one Python Agent SDK CLI/library surface, and one hosted Financial Chat Agent; all require either a personal `pk_...` token or, for SDK agent registration, a signed software-statement JWT.
Viator exposes two authenticated HTTP APIs for developers: the Partner API and the Reservation System API, both using an API key in HTTP headers; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced in the docs I found.
VictorOps exposes one public HTTP REST API on portal.victorops.com, authenticated with `X-VO-Api-Id` and `X-VO-Api-Key`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Google Cloud Video Intelligence exposes authenticated REST APIs in v1 and beta versions; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.
Vimeo exposes a REST API at https://api.vimeo.com with OAuth2/app registration and bearer access tokens, plus a public MCP server at https://mcp.vimeo.com/mcp.
Vio.com exposes a private Partners REST API and a public MCP server; the Partners API uses an `X-Partner-Key` profile key, while MCP is anonymously usable with optional partner API key query auth.

Virgin Atlantic exposes a single public developer integration surface: the NDC 21.3 HTTP API at ndc.virginatlantic.com, authenticated with a Virgin Atlantic-issued developer key obtained via the NDC portal.

VisibleThread exposes two integration surfaces for developers: VT Writer generative AI configuration and the VTRAG REST API; both are customer-configured, OpenAI-compatible integrations that use a customer-provided API key/bearer token rather than a VisibleThread-issued credential.
Visier exposes one public HTTP API suite and one MCP server; both require authentication, and no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed.
Google Cloud Vision API exposes one HTTP REST surface at https://vision.googleapis.com/v1, authenticated with either a Google OAuth2 Bearer token or an API key.
Visual Crossing exposes one HTTP Weather API and one MCP server; both require the Visual Crossing API key.

Visualping exposes one REST API and one MCP server; REST auth accepts either API keys or user email/password bearer tokens, while MCP uses OAuth-backed Visualping sign-in over https://visualping.io/mcp/sse.
Viven.ai exposes a public MCP server at https://vivin.ai/mcp; OAuth client registration exists at https://vivin.ai/oidc/oauth/register for OIDC-based authentication.
Google Cloud VM Migration API exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST surface at vmmigration.googleapis.com; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.

vmware.com exposes Broadcom’s VCF Operations REST API and VCF PowerCLI; the API requires either an OpsToken or VCF SSO Bearer token, while PowerCLI auth mechanics were not fully pinned down from public docs.
VocaDB exposes one public REST API at https://vocadb.net/api/; authenticated usage is documented for site accounts via HTTP Basic auth, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.
Vonage offers public developer docs plus a public MCP docs server; its main programmable surfaces are OpenAPI-based HTTP APIs authenticated by API key/secret, JWT, or product-specific basic auth.
Voyage AI exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://api.voyageai.com/v1 using a Bearer `Authorization` header with the user’s `VOYAGE_API_KEY`; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

vpcaccess.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP REST surface: the Serverless VPC Access API at https://vpcaccess.googleapis.com, documented via Google discovery docs and using Google OAuth bearer access tokens.
VTEX exposes a public Developer MCP surface, plus VTEX IO GraphQL APIs that accept bearer auth via app auth token, user token, or VTEX IO CLI login.
Vybit exposes one authenticated REST API and one authenticated hosted MCP server; both accept either an API key or an OAuth access token.
W3C exposes no public developer integration surface on w3.org beyond public web pages and developer resources; no API, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI found.
waldo.fyi exposes one developer API surface (Waldo Build API) and one MCP surface, both using a Waldo-issued API key; no public GraphQL or CLI surfaced.

Google Wallet exposes one developer HTTP API (walletobjects.googleapis.com / Wallet API) authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0, plus a Google Wallet MCP reference page whose connect endpoint could not be confirmed.

Walmart.com exposes authenticated HTTP integration surfaces for Walmart Marketplace: the Token API at `https://marketplace.walmartapis.com/v3/token` and the main Marketplace API at `https://marketplace.walmartapis.com`, both using Walmart Marketplace credentials via HTTP Basic auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
W&B exposes a hosted MCP server and a GraphQL-backed public API, both authenticated with a W&B API key.

surfaces.name: W&B Public API (Python SDK GraphQL-backed public API)

domains.summary: Warp exposes the Oz Agent HTTP API and Oz CLI, both authenticated with a Warp API key, plus public MCP support in Warp for connecting external MCP servers.
Warrant.dev redirects developers to WorkOS FGA: a Bearer-authenticated HTTP API at https://api.workos.com and an OAuth-based MCP server at https://mcp.workos.com/mcp.
Watchful exposes one developer surface: the Watchful REST API (OpenAPI spec at https://app.watchful.net/api/v1/api-docs/api-docs.json) authenticated with a Watchful API key via Bearer Authorization.
Water Linked exposes one public developer surface: the Underwater GPS HTTP API; no auth mechanism or machine-readable spec was confirmed from the docs I could access.
Wayground exposes an OAuth 2.0 authorization server with dynamic client registration and `full_access` scope; no other public developer surfaces were confirmed.

Wealthreader exposes one documented HTTP REST API at https://api.wealthreader.com/entities/ (OpenAPI-backed docs at the API reference), authenticated with a Wealth Reader-issued `api_key`; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Weatherbit exposes public docs plus authenticated HTTP APIs for weather and air quality; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaced.
WeatherPromise exposes no public developer surfaces discoverable from its site beyond a partner-facing API mention; no authenticated API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI details were published.
Weatherstack exposes one public developer surface: an HTTP REST API at `http://api.weatherstack.com/` authenticated with a per-user `access_key` API key.
Weaviate exposes a public REST API, a GraphQL/gRPC search API with auth unclear from docs, and a local Streamable HTTP MCP server that uses Bearer API keys when auth is enabled.
weber-gesamtausgabe.de exposes one public HTTP REST API (WeGA API) at https://weber-gesamtausgabe.de/api/v1 with no authentication required; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Webex exposes a bearer-token-authenticated REST API plus MCP servers for Meetings and Messaging; developers can use a short-lived personal access token or an OAuth integration client, and MCP clients authenticate with a Webex OAuth bearer token.
Webflow exposes a required-auth Data REST API, an SSE MCP server, and a CLI; auth uses OAuth 2.0 plus site/workspace bearer tokens, all acquired through Webflow browser/CLI login flows.
Google Fonts exposes one public developer surface: the Web Fonts Developer API at https://webfonts.googleapis.com/, authenticated by a Google API key passed as the `key` query parameter.
