Webhook.site exposes one HTTP API and one WebSocket listener, both using `Api-Key` for authenticated account/private-token access, plus a public CLI (`whcli`) with no service credential.

WebhookRelay exposes one authenticated HTTP API at https://my.webhookrelay.com (OpenAPI at https://webhookrelay.com/swagger.json) and an authenticated CLI (`webhookrelay`); no public MCP surface was confirmed.

Webiny exposes GraphQL developer surfaces (Headless CMS plus the main Webiny GraphQL API) and a Webiny CLI; the documented Headless CMS GraphQL API uses an API key, while the other surfaces’ auth mechanics are not fully pinned down from the docs read.

surfaces.name: Webiny Headless CMS GraphQL API
surfaces.url: GraphQL API URL varies by API type (manage, preview, read) and locale; retrieve it from the API Playground or `yarn webiny info --env dev`.

surfaces.name: Webiny Main GraphQL API
surfaces.url: Main GraphQL API URL is available in the API Playground; the docs describe it as the API used by Page Builder, Headless CMS, File Manager, and other default Webiny applications.

domains.summary: webrisk.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Web Risk API) on https://webrisk.googleapis.com, using Google OAuth2 access tokens in Authorization: Bearer; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.
WebScraping.AI exposes one authenticated HTTP API, an MCP server, and a CLI; all use the service-issued API key.
Web Security Scanner API exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at `https://websecurityscanner.googleapis.com/v1`, using Google Cloud OAuth2 (`cloud-platform` scope) via OAuth access tokens.
Webflow exposes one public developer API surface (Data API over HTTP) plus one MCP server; auth is via OAuth app, site token, or workspace token for the API, and OAuth for MCP.
monday.com exposes a GraphQL platform API at https://api.monday.com/v2 with personal V2 API tokens, plus a Platform MCP endpoint at https://monday.com/w/mcp.

Whapi.Cloud exposes a Bearer-auth HTTP API at https://gate.whapi.cloud, an MCP surface at https://whapi.cloud/mcp, and a CLI-installable AI Agent Skill via npx; all require a Whapi token except the skill installer itself, whose runtime auth could not be pinned.

WhatsApp’s public developer surface is the WhatsApp Business Platform Cloud API on graph.facebook.com, authenticated with a Meta-issued WhatsApp Business access token in the Authorization: Bearer header.
WHATWG exposes only public standards/specification pages; no developer API, GraphQL, MCP, CLI, or auth-based integration surface was found.
wheretocredit.com exposed no discoverable public developer surface; only an external Boomi MCP connector doc was found, so no credentials or native surfaces were recorded.
Whimsical exposes one public developer API (beta) requiring a Whimsical workspace access token, plus a Remote MCP surface using the same token; no GraphQL or CLI surface was found.
Who Hosts This exposes one public HTTP detection API at https://www.who-hosts-this.com/API, authenticated with a single API key passed as the `key` query parameter.
wikimedia.org exposes public MediaWiki HTTP APIs: the Action API and REST API, both unauthenticated.
WikiPathways exposes a public JSON API and a public SPARQL endpoint; no credentials are required.
Wikipedia.org exposes public MediaWiki Action and REST APIs, plus Wikimedia Enterprise APIs requiring a Wikimedia account; the MediaWiki CLI (`mw`) is developer tooling with auth unclear.
williamkapke.github.io exposes one public MCP surface for Kapture browser automation via a local bridge/WebSocket endpoint; no service-issued credentials or HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surfaces were found.
windows.net resolves to Microsoft Fabric developer surfaces: REST API, GraphQL API, and an MCP-related doc page; auth appears to use Microsoft Entra ID OAuth bearer tokens.
WinSMS exposes HTTP APIs for REST, XML, and HTTP docs, plus public inbound webhooks; API key auth is supported and legacy user/password remains available for XML, while no MCP or CLI surfaced.

wirefreethought.github.io exposes one documented public integration surface: the RapidAPI-hosted Mood Metrics REST API; GeoDB docs surfaced only as RapidAPI discussion links, with no directly accessible spec or independent surface confirmed from this domain.
WireMock Cloud exposes a REST API, an MCP server, and a CLI; REST auth supports API token, legacy basic auth, and enterprise OAuth device flow.

Wise exposes a REST/OpenAPI Wise Platform API with sandbox and production base URLs; auth is via OAuth 2.0 partner client credentials plus user access tokens, or a personal API token for SMB endpoints.
Orb exposes one public developer surface: a Bearer-authenticated REST API at https://api.withorb.com, with API key creation in the Orb dashboard.
Persona exposes one authenticated REST API family (including Webhooks and Relay) plus a GraphQL surface with unknown auth and a public MCP server at docs.withpersona.com/_mcp/server.

Wix exposes an HTTP API surface (Wix JavaScript SDK / unified API reference, with REST/GraphQL under it) and an MCP server at https://mcp.wix.com/mcp; auth is via Wix API key plus account/site IDs for account-managed/self-managed use, or Wix OAuth app credentials (app ID, app secret, instance ID) for installed apps, and MCP can also be used with no credential or with API key + account ID.

surfaces.name: Wix JavaScript SDK / unified API reference

domains.summary: Wiz exposes a region-specific GraphQL API and a preview MCP server; both use Wiz-issued OAuth client credentials (client ID + client secret), with the GraphQL endpoint documented as `https://api.<region>.app.wiz.io/graphql`.

wizards.com exposes no public developer API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface; only branded web portals (Magic, WPN, Support) were found, with a general Wizards Account login but no documented developer integration auth.
WMATA exposes one developer HTTP API surface (Metro Transparent Data Sets API) authenticated with a WMATA API key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were found.

surfaces.name: WMATA Metro Transparent Data Sets API

domains.summary: Wolfram|Alpha exposes multiple HTTP APIs (Full Results, Simple, Short Answers, Fast Query Recognizer, Summary Boxes, Instant Calculators, Spoken Results), all authenticated by a Wolfram|Alpha AppID query parameter, plus a Wolfram Cloud MCP surface whose auth could not be determined from the page.

WooCommerce exposes a public Store API, authenticated REST API, authenticated MCP integration, and a WP-CLI-based WooCommerce CLI; REST/MCP/CLI can use WooCommerce REST API keys, with app-based key issuance via `/wc-auth/v1/authorize`.
Woopra exposes public HTTP tracking endpoints and authenticated RESTish data APIs using per-user HTTP Basic auth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Woosmap exposes one authenticated REST API at https://api.woosmap.com using a Woosmap API key (`key` query param), plus a Woosmap MCP server surfaced in docs with unknown auth mechanics.

Word Associations Network exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at https://api.wordassociations.net with Swagger JSON/YAML spec links; authentication is required via the API account/subscription portal, and no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Wordnik exposes one public developer surface: the Wordnik REST API at https://api.wordnik.com/v4, authenticated with an API key (header or query) and optionally an auth token for private word lists.

WordPress.com exposes a REST API, WordPress.com Connect, an MCP surface, and WP-CLI; REST and Connect use OAuth2-based credentials, while MCP and CLI auth mechanics were not fully pinned from the available docs.
Workable exposes one authenticated HTTP API (Bearer API access token) and one authenticated MCP server (OAuth2 via Workable sign-in); no public GraphQL or CLI surface found.
Workato exposes a required-auth Developer API, a CLI authenticated via `workato login`, and a public MCP developer page/server; no GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Workday exposes a public developer documentation portal and a logged-in developer portal; I could confirm no separate public API/MCP/CLI surfaces from the pages found.
workflowexecutions.googleapis.com exposes one authenticated HTTP API: Google Cloud Workflow Executions API via OAuth2/Cloud Platform scopes.
Google Cloud Workflows exposes one authenticated HTTP/REST API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.
Workload Manager exposes one authenticated HTTP REST API surface (v1) on workloadmanager.googleapis.com, using Google Cloud OAuth/ADC Bearer tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was found.

WorkOS exposes one REST API, one Client GraphQL API, and one MCP server; all require WorkOS-issued credentials, with API key auth for REST, a short-lived client bearer token for GraphQL, and OAuth-based MCP access via AuthKit.
Cloud Workstations exposes a Google-authenticated REST API at workstations.googleapis.com and a gcloud CLI surface (`gcloud workstations`); no public or MCP/GraphQL surface found.

Wowza exposes two developer surfaces: the Wowza Streaming Engine REST API (basic auth with admin credentials) and the Wowza Video REST API (JWT bearer token), plus a Wowza Streaming Engine MCP server with auth unknown.

Wrike exposes a REST API v4 and an MCP server; both require Wrike-issued OAuth/app credentials or a Permanent Access Token, while the DataHub Public API surface was identified but its auth could not be pinned from the docs read.
WSO2 API Manager exposes documented product REST APIs and Store APIs; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed for wso2apistore.com.

surfaces.name: WSO2 API Manager product REST APIs

domains.summary: WunderGraph Cosmo exposes a required-auth control-plane HTTP API, a STDIO MCP server, and the `wgc` CLI, all using a Cosmo API key (`COSMO_API_KEY`).
Google provides the Drive API v3 over HTTPS at www.googleapis.com with OAuth 2.0 bearer auth, plus a Drive MCP server that uses the same Google OAuth login.

surfaces.name: Google Drive API v3 MCP server

domains.summary: xAI exposes a Bearer-authenticated Inference REST API at https://api.x.ai, a Docs MCP surface with unknown auth, and a public Grok Build CLI (`grok-build`).

X exposes one REST API (https://api.x.com/2) plus two hosted MCP servers: X MCP at https://api.x.com/mcp and Docs MCP at https://docs.x.com/mcp; auth uses app-only Bearer tokens, OAuth 2.0 client credentials, or OAuth 1.0a user tokens, while Docs MCP is public.

Xata exposes a control-plane HTTP API, branch SQL HTTP gateway, branch PostgreSQL WebSocket gateway, and the `xata` CLI; auth is via Xata account access token for control-plane and branch connection string for gateways.
Xero exposes one public REST API, one MCP server, and one CLI surface; all require Xero OAuth-based authentication, with no API-key auth advertised.
xkcd.com exposes a public, unauthenticated JSON API for comic metadata; no credentials, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.
yahoo.co.jp exposes the LY Ads developer APIs: Search Ads and Display Ads HTTP APIs, both requiring OAuth 2.0 with a LY Ads API application (Client ID/Client secret) and Bearer access tokens.

yahooapis.jp exposes three documented HTTP API surfaces (Shopping, YOLP, Text Analysis) plus Yahoo! ID連携 v2 docs; developer auth centers on a Yahoo! JAPAN Client ID, with shopping also supporting Yahoo! ID連携 tokens.

surfaces.name: YOLP (Yahoo! Open Local Platform) Web APIs

domains.summary: Yango exposes one public integration surface: an unauthenticated MCP server at https://yango.com/mcp; no public HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI developer surfaces were confirmed.

Yardi exposes one discoverable developer integration surface: the RentCafe API / Data Exchange Program for partner-based HTTP integration, gated by a manual interface-partnership agreement; no public spec, GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

surfaces.name: Yardi RentCafe API / Data Exchange Program

domains.summary: Supabase exposes a project-scoped Data REST API, hosted MCP server, and CLI; auth uses publishable/secret or legacy anon/service_role keys for REST, browser OAuth for MCP, and `supabase login` for CLI.
Yelp exposes HTTP APIs for Places, Partner, and AI, plus a GraphQL API; Places/Partner/GraphQL auth is documented, while AI auth mechanics were not fully pinned down.

YepCode exposes a REST API, a Run API, and an MCP server; auth is via an API Credential that mints OAuth client credentials, a Run API token, and MCP access, plus a CLI that logs in to the cloud account.

Yoti exposes five public HTTP integration surfaces: Identity Verification, Age Verification, Facial Age Estimation, Trust, and Proof of Age APIs; all require Yoti-issued credentials (API key/bearer token, app/SDK ID, and/or private-key signed requests).
YNAB exposes one public developer surface: the authenticated REST API at https://api.ynab.com/v1, supporting Personal Access Tokens and OAuth apps via the Authorization: Bearer header.
Yousign exposes one public developer surface: the Yousign Public API v3 HTTP API, authenticated with a Yousign API key via `Authorization: Bearer`.
YouTube exposes the public IFrame Player API and the authenticated YouTube Data API v3 plus YouTube Reporting API; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.

YouTube exposes four developer HTTP surfaces: Data API v3, Live Streaming API, Analytics API, and Reporting API; all require Google API keys and/or OAuth 2.0, with no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface found.
YouTube Analytics API (v2) exposes one authenticated HTTP surface at https://youtubeanalytics.googleapis.com/v2 using Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.
YouTube Reporting API exposes one HTTP REST surface at youtubereporting.googleapis.com, authenticated with Google OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.
YouVersion exposes a REST API and OAuth/sign-in/data-exchange HTTP surfaces, all using the app key; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed.
YouVersion exposes a REST API at api.youversion.com with app-key auth, plus an OAuth sign-in surface using the same app key and Bearer tokens; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaced.

Yugabyte exposes the YugabyteDB Anywhere REST API, YugabyteDB Aeon REST API, the `ybm` CLI, and a YugabyteDB MCP server; confirmed auth for YBA uses `X-AUTH-YW-API-TOKEN`, and `ybm` is initialized with `ybm auth`.

Zalando exposes one confirmed developer HTTP surface: the Merchant Platform Orders API (OpenAPI YAML at developers.merchants.zalando.com) authenticated with a bearer merchant access token; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI integration surface was confirmed.
zappy.app exposes Zapier’s developer integration platform CLI and a Zapier MCP surface; auth is via a Zapier account/login, while the MCP endpoint’s authentication could not be confirmed.

Zeet exposes two authenticated GraphQL APIs (v0 at https://anchor.zeet.co/graphql and v1 at https://anchor.zeet.co/v1/graphql) plus the `zeet` CLI, all using a user/team API key obtained from Zeet account/team API key settings.

Vercel (zeit.co) exposes a REST API at https://api.vercel.com, a `vercel` CLI, and Vercel MCP; auth is via Vercel access tokens for the API, `vercel login` for the CLI, and MCP auth could not be determined.
Zen7.com exposes one documented developer surface: the Zen7 Payment Agent MCP server; no public HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surface or clear authentication details were confirmed.

Zendesk exposes the Zendesk APIs/Ticketing API over HTTP at https://{subdomain}.zendesk.com/api/v2 with an OpenAPI spec at https://developer.zendesk.com/zendesk/oas.yaml, authenticated via Zendesk OAuth access tokens in the Authorization: Bearer header.
Zeno.fm exposes one documented authenticated REST API (Aggregators API), one metadata HTTP endpoint with unclear auth, and an MCP server-card URL; no CLI or GraphQL surface found.
Zep exposes one authenticated HTTP API, a public docs MCP server, an enterprise Memory MCP server with identity-provider auth, and the zepctl CLI with login or ZEP_API_KEY.
ZeroTier exposes two Central REST APIs, a local ZeroTierOne service API, and the zerotier-cli CLI; no GraphQL or MCP surfaced.
Zilliz exposes a required-auth REST API, MCP server, and CLI; all accept either a Zilliz Cloud API key or cluster username/password, and no public unauthenticated developer surface was confirmed.

Zillow exposes several HTTP APIs: Bridge-hosted Public Records, Zestimate, and Agent Reviews APIs requiring Bridge credentials, a Mortgage Get Current Rates API requiring `partnerId`, plus public Neighborhood/Real Estate Metrics downloads and an Rentals Lead API with auth undetermined.

surfaces.name: Zillow Mortgage Get Current Rates API

domains.summary: Zillow Group exposes several invite-only REST APIs plus one public mortgage rates API; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces were identified.

surfaces.name: Zillow Group Mortgage Get Current Rates API
Zillow Group Mortgage Prospect Sync API
Zillow Group Mortgage Prospect Trigger API
Zillow Group Mortgage Rate Cloud API
Zillow Group Mortgage Lead Posting API

domains.summary: ZipRecruiter exposes one public integration surface: a no-auth MCP Job Search server at https://api.ziprecruiter.com/mcp; no HTTP REST, GraphQL, or CLI developer surface was confirmed.

ZITADEL exposes public OIDC and SAML endpoints plus authenticated HTTP APIs for Management, Project, and self-hosted System access; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was confirmed from the docs reviewed.
Zocks exposes one developer HTTP API (OAuth app-based) and one MCP server at https://mcp.zocks.io/v1/mcp using OAuth 2.0; no CLI or GraphQL surface was confirmed.
Zoho CRM exposes an OAuth2-protected REST API v8, plus GraphQL, MCP, and ZDK CLI surfaces; only the REST API auth could be confirmed.
Zoho CRM exposes authenticated HTTP REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, and an MCP surface; the ZDK CLI exists but its auth mechanics were not fully determined.
Zoho.eu exposes at least Zoho Desk REST API (OAuth, `Authorization: Zoho-oauthtoken`) and a Zoho Books MCP setup page; no GraphQL or CLI surface was confirmed from the EU domain docs I found.

Zoho.in exposes Zoho CRM developer surfaces: REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, an MCP page with unclear connect details, and the ZDK CLI; REST and GraphQL use Zoho OAuth bearer tokens, and the CLI uses `zdk login`.
zoho.jp exposes Zoho CRM developer surfaces: an authenticated REST API, plus GraphQL, MCP, and ZDK CLI surfaces with auth not yet pinned from the pages scraped.
Zoho on zoho.sa exposes Zoho CRM as a public-facing web app surface of unknown developer auth, and Zoho MCP at https://mcp.zoho.com requiring Zoho account OAuth-based access.
Zoho.uk exposes Zoho Books API (HTTP), Zoho Books MCP, and Zoho Finance CLI; the HTTP API uses Zoho OAuth, while MCP auth is unclear from public docs.
Zoho exposes a REST API surface for Zoho products; authentication is via Zoho OAuth client-based access/refresh tokens or the older CRM auth token in the Authorization header.
zohomcp.ae exposes one integration surface: Zoho MCP over streamable HTTP at https://mcp.zoho.com, authenticated with a Zoho account.
Zoho MCP exposes one MCP integration surface at https://mcp.zoho.com, authenticated via a Zoho account obtained through Zoho MCP signup.
zohomcp.com.au maps to Zoho MCP only: an MCP surface at https://mcp.zoho.com requiring a Zoho account login; no REST/GraphQL/CLI surface was found.
Zoho MCP exposes one authenticated MCP surface at https://mcp.zoho.com, with Zoho account login required; no HTTP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Zoho MCP exposes one authenticated MCP surface at https://mcp.zoho.com, using a Zoho account; no HTTP/GraphQL/CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Zoho MCP exposes one public integration surface: an authenticated MCP server at https://mcp.zoho.com; no separate REST, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.
Zoho MCP exposes one MCP surface at https://mcp.zoho.com and requires a Zoho account sign-in; no REST, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

Zoom exposes one public developer surface: the Zoom REST API at https://api.zoom.us/v2/ requiring Bearer access tokens obtained via OAuth user/app credentials or server-to-server OAuth; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found.

Zoom Connect exposes one public developer surface: a REST API at https://www.zoomconnect.com/app/ authenticated by account email + API token via headers or HTTP Basic; no GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaced.
Zoopla exposes a developer docs portal, but the linked API reference pages were unavailable from the public site and no machine-readable API specs or other developer surfaces were confirmed.
zrok offers a CLI (`zrok2`) authenticated by an account token, plus the hosted myzrok web console; I did not find a public OpenAPI/GraphQL/MCP surface from the available docs.

Zscaler Integrations MCP Server exposes one MCP surface; it requires Zscaler OneAPI/ZIdentity credentials for server-to-Zscaler access, while MCP client authentication applies separately to HTTP transports.
Zuora exposes a required-auth HTTP v1 Billing API and a required-auth remote MCP server; both use OAuth-issued bearer access tokens, with OAuth client credentials as the minting credential.
Zuplo exposes a documented programming HTTP API and an MCP server handler; OAuth 2.0 client registration is via auth.zuplo.com with the observed authorize/token endpoints.
Zuplo exposes a web-based management portal; public developer docs were inaccessible from probes, and no public API/GraphQL/MCP/CLI surface could be confirmed from the available pages.
Zus Health exposes authenticated FHIR REST and FHIR GraphQL APIs; I found no public MCP server or CLI surface in the available docs.
